CHAPTER 161 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section one hundred sixty -five
a -8 ( 165a ) of of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand
nine hundred thirty -six ( 1936 ) Edition , to provide for the
exchange of information between the Division of Banking
and other properly authorized supervisory authorities .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section one hundred sixty-five a- 8 (165a - 8 )

CHAPTER 161 761
of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine
hundred thirty -six ( 1936 ) Edition , be and hereby is
amended and re-enacted , so that the same when thus
re-enacted , shall read as follows , to - wit :
Every bank organized and doing business under
the laws of this state shall be subject to the inspec-
tion of the Director of the Division of Banking of
the Department of Business Regulation . Such Di-
rector shall personally or by the Assistant Director
of the Division of Banking or a state bank examiner
visit each bank and examine the cash , bills , col-
lateral , securities , other assets , books of accounts ,
and all other papers and books of such bank . On
such examination inquiry shall be made as to the
condition and resources of the corporation , the mode
of conducting and managing its affairs , the actions
of its directors , the investment and disposition of
its funds , the safety and prudence of its manage-
ment , the security afforded to those by whom its
engagements are held , and whether the requirements
of its charter and of the laws of this state have been
complied with in the administration of its affairs ,
and as to such other matters as the Director may
deem necessary . Such examination shall be made
of every bank at least once every year , but not to
exceed twice in any one year , unless it shall appear
from examination or from the report or reports of
a bank that such bank has failed to comply with the
laws relating to banks , or a regulation of the Divi-
sion of Banking of the State Department of Busi-
ness Regulations .
The Director of the Division of Banking , the As-
sistant Director , and every state bank examiner shall
have the power to administer an oath to any person
whose testimony may be required in the examination
of any bank ; and to compel the appearance of any
such person for the purpose of such examination ,

762 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
which shall be made in the presence of one or more
of the officers of the corporation being examined .
No notice of the intended examination shall be given
to the bank to be examined until the examining of-
ficer is prepared to proceed immediately with the
examination . No officer or employee of the Division
of Banking nor the examiner conducting any exami-
nation shall impart any information obtained by him
during such examination , except as far as may be
necessary in the performance of his official duty as
provided by law ; and except such Division of Bank-
ing may furnish to and exchange information and
reports with officials and/or Examiners of any other
properly authorized supervisory authorities , state
or federal , including the Federal Deposit Insurance
Corporation and the Reconstruction Finance Cor-
poration , and the duly accredited representatives
thereof . Provided , that any bank or trust company
or combined bank and trust company which shall be-
come a member of a federal reserve bank created
and organized under the act of Congress known as
the federal reserve act , shall be subject to the ex-
amination required under the term of the said fed-
eral reserve act , and the Director of the Division of
Banking may , in his discretion , accept examinations
made by the federal reserve authorities in lieu of
examinations made under the laws of this Common-
wealth ; and the Director is further authorized and
directed to furnish to the federal reserve agent of
the district in which such member bank may be sit-
uated , copies of reports and examinations made of
such member bank .
Approved by the Governor : March 11 , 1938.
CHAPTER 162 .
AN ACT to clarify the license taxation by municipalities of
banks , trust companies , combined banks and trust companies ,

CHAPTER 163 763
and trust , banking and title insurance companies organized
under the laws of this State .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Whereas National banks doing business in Ken-
tucky cannot be subjected to a license tax by any city
or municipality , it is hereby enacted that no city or
municipality in the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
anything in their respective charter powers to the
contrary notwithstanding , shall have the power to
impose or collect any license tax upon any bank,
trust company , combined bank and trust company ,
or trust , banking and title insurance company or-
ganized and doing business in this State .
All laws and parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
Approved by the Governor : March 11 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 163 .
AN ACT pertaining to banking and to amend and re -enact Sec-
tion 584a of Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's Edition ( Baldwin's
1936 Revision ) .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 584a of Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
Edition (Baldwin's 1936 Revision ) be and the same
is hereby amended and re -enacted so as to read as
follows :
Any bank or trust company or combined bank and
trust company organized under the laws of this
Commonwealth may subscribe for and own stock of
the Federal reserve bank within the Federal reserve
district where such bank or trust company or com-
bined bank and trust company is located and may

764 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
take any steps necessary to become a member of
such Federal Reserve Bank .
Any bank or trust company or combined bank and
trust company organized under the laws of this
Commonwealth is hereby empowered upon the au-
thority of a majority of its board of directors to do
anything necessary or appropriate to acquire or
maintain insurance of its deposits in the Federal
Deposit Insurance Corporation , established pur-
suant to Section 12B of the Federal Reserve Act , as
amended , or in any Federal corporation that may
succeed or be substituted for same ; and also with-
out limiting the generality of the foregoing to ac-
quire stock or securities of such corporation and to
enter into and take advantage of all contracts , rights
and privileges , which may at any time be available
to said banks or trust companies or combined banks
and trust companies , or their depositors or receivers
or liquidators , pursuant to said or other Federal
Act . Approved by the Governor : March 11 , 1938.
CHAPTER 164 .
AN ACT to prohibit any person , or officer of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky or any subdivision thereof from instituting or
prosecuting proceedings to escheat real estate of lending
corporations under the supervision of duly constituted public
authority , and acquired in satisfaction of loan indebtedness to
it, without first having obtained the consent of said super-
vising authority .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. No person or officer of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky or any subdivision thereof , shall insti-
tute or proseute proceedings to escheat real prop-
erty the title to which was acquired by lending corpo-

CHAPTER 165 765
rations in satisfaction of debts previously contracted
in the course of its business , or that it may purchase
under a judgment for any such debt in its favor ,
where said lending corporations are under the super-
vision of the Division of Banking of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , Comptroller of Currency of
the United States , or any other duly constituted
supervising banking authorities , state or federal ,
without first obtaining the consent of the supervising
authority having supervision over the lending
corporation . Approved by the Governor : March 11 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 165 .
AN ACT to amend subdivision 1 of Article V of Chapter 32 of
the Kentucky Statutes by the inclusion therein of a new sec-
tion , 768 -a, to be inserted after Section 768 , in Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , Baldwin's Revised Edition , 1930 , to extend
the powers of railroad companies so as to authorize railroad
companies ( 1 ) to engage in the business of transporting per-
sons and property as common carriers by motor vehicles on
the highways and by aeroplanes in the air , and ( 2 ) to pur-
chase or lease the property , rights and franchises of any indi-
vidual , firm or corporation engaged in motor vehicle or air
transportation , to subscribe to or otherwise acquire the capital
stock of any motor carrier or air company and to make any
agreement or arrangement , not inconsistent with law , with
any individual , firm or corporation engaged in motor vehicle
or air transportation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That subdivision 1 of Article V of Chapter 32 of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1930 Revi-
sion , be and the same is amended by adding thereto
a new section , 768 -a, to follow Section 768 , and to
read as follows :
" Section 768 - a. In addition to the powers now
conferred upon railroad companies , every such com-

766 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
pany heretofore or hereafter created under the laws
of this State shall have the power and right ( 1 ) to
engage in the business of a common carrier for the
transportation of persons and property by motor
vehicles operated upon the highways and by aero-
planes in the air, and (2 ) to purcase or lease the
property , rights and franchises of any individual ,
firm or corporation engaged in transportation of
persons and property by motor vehicles upon the
highways or by aeroplanes in the air , and to sub-
scribe to or otherwise acquire the capital stock of
any such corporation , or make any agreement or
arrangement , not inconsistent with law, with any
individual , firm or corporation engaged or author-
ized to engage in either of said methods of trans-
portation ; provided , however , that all railroad com-
panies exercising in this State the powers herein
Igranted shall , in their operation of motor vehicles ,
be deemed common carrier operators of such vehi-
cles and be subject to all the laws of this State appli-
cable to , or authorizing , regulating and governing ,
such motor carriers and their business , or prescrib-
ing the conditions under which common carrier
operators of motor vehicles may operate such vehi-
cles upon the highways of this State . All railroad
companies organized under the laws of other States ,
but authorized to do business in this State may exer-
cise in this State the powers hereinabove granted to
companies organized under the laws of this State ,
subject , however , to the limitations hereinabove set
forth." Approved by the Governor : March 5 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 166 .
AN ACT to promote the Agricultural , Horticultural and Live
Stock Interest of the Commonwealth of Kentucky by enabling
the State Board of Agriculture to co-operate with County and

CHAPTER 166 767
Community Fairs in offering of premiums for agricultural ,
horticultural and live stock exhibits at such Fairs .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the State Board of Agriculture is
authorized and empowered to co -operate with Coun-
ty Fairs and Community Fairs in the Commonwealth
of Kentucky by supplementing premiums offered at
such fairs for exhibits of cattle , hogs , sheep , mules ,
Jacks , jennets , and other livestock, poultry ; for
breeding classes for horses including two years old
or under and for breeding stock of any age ; and for
any and all classes of fruits , vegetables , seeds or
other crops for which exhibits are had at such fairs
to the extent of from $ 500 to $ 1000 annually , pro-
vided the fair association receiving such support of-
fers at least an equal amount in these classes . If the
fair association offers a lesser amount the financial
support of the Commonwealth of Kentucky will be
reduced in proportion thereto .
$§ 2. That any fair association offering special
prize money for 4-H Club or for Smith -Hughes Agri-
cultural student projects may receive an additional
sum ranging from $ 250 to $ 500 provided each fair
appropriates an equal amount in these classes .
$§ 3. The State Board of Agriculture is author-
ized to make such rules and regulations as may be
necessary for carrying this act into effect , and to
issue an order to the State Treasury for payment
of funds due each fair complying with its rules upon
receipt of an accurate and complete report from
the secretary and president of the fair claiming
financial assistance under this act .
Approved by the Governor : March 11 , 1938 .

768 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 167.
AN ACT amending and re-enacting section 4308-3 , Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1936 edition , being Section 3 of Chapter 5 of
the Acts of the General Assembly of 1936 , relating to the
plans of the State Highway Commission and the fiscal courts
for the use of the funds appropriated for county roads and
and bridges .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That section 4308-3, Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition , being Section 3 of Chapter 5 of the
Acts of the General Assembly of 1936 , be amended
and re -enacted so as to read as follows :
The funds apportioned the several counties by
Section two ( 2 ) of this Act shall be used for the pur-
poses provided herein by the State Highway Com-
mission in accordance with previously agreed to and
accepted plans prepared jointly by the Fiscal Courts
of the several counties and the State Highway Com-
mission, or prepared by one or the other and jointly
approved . Said plans shall take into consideration
the mileage of county roads in the particular county ,
the condition and needs of such roads and the man-
ner in which said funds shall be expended by the
State Highway Commission for the fiscal year to
which the agreement applies . Said plan for each
county shall be formulated and agreed to as soon
as practicable after the effective date of this Act , for
the then current fiscal year and annually thereafter .
If at the end of thirty days after the beginning of a
fiscal year of the State Highway Commission , the
State Highway Commission and the fiscal court of
any county have failed to agree on a plan , or the
fiscal court of a county has refused to submit a plan ,
the State Highway Commission shall have authority
to expend the money apportioned that county on the
county roads and bridges thereof in such a manner

CHAPTER 168 769
as the State Highway Commission shall determine .
Due to the fact that the fiscal year of the Highway
Department starts April 1 , an emergency is declared
to exist, and this Act shall become effective upon its
passage and approval by the Governor . The term,
State Highway Commission , as used in this Act and
the title thereto , shall be construed to mean , the De-
partment of Highways . Whereas , it is deemed im-
portant by the General Assembly that the provision
of this Act be effective immediately to enable the
Department of Highways to conform its budget and
program for the ensuing fiscal year , an emergency
hereby is declared to exist and this Act shall be effec-
tive immediately upon its passage and approval by
the Governor . Approved by the Governor : March 11 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 168 .
AN ACT providing for the escheat to the Commonwealth of
bank deposits , other deposits , dividends , stocks , bonds ,
moneys , credits , and all intangible personal property, and
providing that all escheated property and the proceeds thereof
shall be paid into the State Treasury and credited to the
general expenditure fund .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. All bank deposits , deposits of money , stocks ,
and bonds made to secure payment for services ren-
dered , loans made , and against possible damage or
harm , dividends , stocks , bonds , and all moneys ,
credits , and claims for moneys and credits , and all
intangible personal property whatsoever held with-
in this Commonwealth by any corporation , com-
pany, partnership , agency or person , whether as
bailee , trustee , receiver , executor , or administrator ,
the last known owner of which is not known to be
Acts-25

770 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
living , and has not been heard of for eight years ,
and no account can be given of said owner , his heirs ,
devisees , or distributees , such person shall be pre-
sumed to have died without heirs , devisees or dis-
tributees and the said property shall vest in the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , and shall be delivered
or paid to the Department of Revenue , or if not so
paid or delivered may be recovered by the Common-
wealth by an action in equity .
§ 2. The Department of Revenue is hereby au-
thorized to require state and national banks , cor-
porations , companies , partnerships , agencies , and
persons in this Commonwealth to make reports of
all property held by them, whether as bailee , trus-
tee , executor , administrator , or receiver , the last
known owner of which is not known to be living and
has not been heard of for a period of five years or
more , and who is not known to have relatives who
would be his heirs or distributees if such owner
were dead and , the last known address of such
owner .
The Department of Revenue , through the Com-
missioner, agents and employees thereof, is also au-
thorized to examine all records of such state and
national banks , corporations , companies , partner-
ships , agencies , and persons where there is reason
to believe that there has been or is a failure to re-
port property which should be reported under the
provisions of this act .
For each failure to make a report or to permit
an examination of records as is provided in this
section a penalty of one hundred dollars is hereby
imposed , and the same shall be recoverable by ap-
propriate action instituted by the Commissioner of
Revenue in the name of the Commonwealth .
3. A corporation , company , partnership ,
agency , or person who shall transfer to the Depart-

CHAPTER 168 771
ment of Revenue of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
property to which the Commonwealth of Kentucky
is entitled under the provisions of section one of this
act is hereby relieved of any liability to the rightful
owner of such property for such transfer . Pro-
vided that if any corporation , company , partner-
ship , agency or person cannot be relieved of liability
by the provisions of this section , the Commonwealth
of Kentucky shall reimburse such corporation , com-
pany , partnership , agency or person for liability to
the rightful owner of property incurred by reason.
of compliance with the provisions of section one ( 1 )
of this Act . Claims for such reimbursement shall
be filed with the Commissioner of Revenue , who may ,
with the written consent of the Attorney General ,
approve such claim and certify same for payment.
in the same manner that other claims against the
Commonwealth are paid .
4. All suits to enforce the transfer of property
under the provisions of section one of this act shall
be instituted by the Attorney General of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky on relation of the Commis-
sioner of Revenue .
§ 5. All property received by the Department of
Revenue under the provisions of section one of this
act shall be deposited in the State Treasury to be
held in trust for the rightful owner or owners there-
of . All stocks , bonds , and other such evidences of
indebtedness shall be delivered by the Treasurer to
the Commissioner of Revenue and shall be sold by
the Commissioner of Revenue , at public sale in
Frankfort , to the highest responsible bidder . The
Isale shall be advertised in at least one issue of a
newspaper published in the county where the money
was on deposit , said notice to appear at least seven
days before the date fixed for the sale . After de-
ducting the cost of advertising of the sale , the bal-

772 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ance of the proceeds shall be paid into the State
Treasury and credited to the general expenditure
fund .
§ 6. After the receipt of property by the De-
partment of Revenue , any claimant or claimants of
the property may file his or their claim before the
Commissioner of Revenue . Said claim shall be in
writing and shall state the facts on which the same
is based and affidavits of responsible persons in sup-
port thereof . The Commissioner of Revenue may ,
with the written consent of the Attorney General ,
approve such claim and certify same for payment.
in the same manner that other claims against the
Commonwealth are paid .
7. A claimant, whose claim has been rejected
by the Department of Revenue , may file suit in the
Franklin Quarterly Court , Franklin Circuit
Court or court of competent jurisdiction in the
county where the money was deposited against the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , through its agent , the
Commissioner of Revenue , for the payment of
money or the return of property claimed . The suit
shall be governed by the rules governing ordinary
suits for claims for money or for the return of prop-
erty in the respective courts and upon a final judg-
ment for the payment of money or the return of
the property , the appropriate official shall draw his
warrant on the Treasurer for the payment of the
money or the return of property .
$ 8. All moneys , and the proceeds from the sale
of all property , which have escheated to the State ,
whether under the provisions of this act or any other
act relating to the escheat of property , shall be paid
into the Treasury of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky , and credited to the general expenditure fund .
Approved by the Governor : March 10, 1938 .

CHAPTER 169 773
CHAPTER 169 .
AN ACT to enable cities of the second , third , fourth and fifth
class , and counties containing a city of such class of the Com-
monwealth to acquire land for aviation fields , providing that
such cities and counties may acquire such lands within their
corporate limits or anywhere in the county in which the city
is located , for aviation fields and to establish and equip the
same airports : and authorizing cities and counties to use ,
operate and control the same for the benefit of such cities
and counties and the inhabitants thereof.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter 12 , Section 1 , of the Acts of 1930
General Assembly (now Section 2741q , Kentucky
Statutes ) be repealed , amended and re - enacted so
that when so amended and re-enacted said section
shall read as follows : That any city of the second ,
third , fourth and fifth class and any county contain-
ing such city within this Commonwealth be and it is
hereby authorized and empowered to acquire , either
by leasing, purchase , gift or condemnation in the
same manner as park property is acquired any and
all land necessary or suitable for establishing avia-
tion fields and airports in any city , or in the county
in which such city is situated , and the county may
acquire same anywhere within its territorial bounda-
ries , and they are further authorized and empowered
to erect , equip , operate and maintain on such land
such buildings , equipment , runways and other fur-
nishings as may be necessary , incidental to or proper
or desirable for the establishment and maintenance
of such aviation fields and airports . Such cities and
counties , for the purpose of the purchase of said
aviation fields and the equipment , establishment and
maintenance of same as airports and other matters
and purposes provided herein may expend out of
any funds in their hands such money as may be nec-

774 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
essary , and may maintain same out of their funds
to the extent that they are authorized to maintain
public parks in such cities and counties , and said
aviation fields and airports are authorized as public
parks of such cities and counties . In the event any
such city or county shall have purchased or leased
land for airport purposes , as above set out , and shall
desire to have a responsible person , association or
corporation operate such property in order to assure
more successful and proper operation , then , the gov-
erning body of any such city or county may lease ,
assign or sub - lease the whole or any part of any
such airport or municipal field for aviation purposes
to one or more responsible persons , associations or
corporations jointly or severally for any purpose
directly or incidentally and necessarily required for
the successful and proper operation of such prop-
erty , upon terms as shall insure fair , financial return.
to the leasee and impartial operation thereof and
rendition of services and conveniences thereon . The
leasee may be granted in said lease execlusive fees
for commercial and schooling services together with
exclusive concessions for equipment used on said
field . Approved by the Governor : March 11 , 1938.
CHAPTER 170 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Chapter 4 , of the Acts of the
General Assembly of Kentucky of One Thousand nine hundred
twelve , as amended , being Sections 165a -1 through 165a -22,
both inclusive , of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition ,
by adding a new section , to-wit ; 165a -23 , and to declare the

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Sections 165a - 1 through 165a -22 , both inclu-

CHAPTER 171 775
sive , of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition ,
be amended by adding at the end thereof another

shall read as follows , to -wit : That nothing herein

CHAPTER 171 .
AN ACT repealing , amending and re -enacting Section 27391-78 ,
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , regulating and
governing the transportation for hire of persons and prop-
erty by motor vehicles on public highways in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That section 2739j -78 , and be and the same are
hereby repealed , amended and re -enacted to read as
follows :
§ 1. On or before January 1 , 1939 and annually
thereafter , and as a condition precedent to the use
of the highways of the Commonwealth , every opera-
tor of a motor vehicle for hire used primarily for
the transportation of property shall pay to the De-
partment of Revenue or its agent at Frankfort , for
the privilege of using the highways , an annual ex-
cise tax equal to one dollar for each hundred pounds ,
or fractions thereof, of weight of each motor vehicle
operated for hire by him , said weight to be the

776 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
weight of the motor vehicle at the time of the appli-
cation for the use of the highways of the Common-
wealth and when empty and ready for operation .
The excise tax imposed by this section shall in each
instance be increased fifty ( 50 % ) per cent on each
motor vehicle for hire not equipped wholly with
pneumatic tires . There shall be added to the tax
a penalty of twenty ( 20 % ) per cent for failure to
pay the tax when the same is due , and , in addition
thereto , a penalty of one ( 1 % ) percent for each
month , or fraction thereof , the excise tax imposed
by this section is in default .
2. Every operator of a motor vehicle for hire
upon the payment of the excise tax as provided in
the previous section shall cause to be printed or
posted on the left side of the cab or hood of each
motor vehicle the weight of such motor vehicle when
empty and ready for operation in figures at least
three inches in height and preceded by words or let-
ters indicating that this is the weight of the motor
vehicle when empty , and shall display so as to be
visible any weight tags furnished by the Director of
the Division of Motor Transportation .
§ 3. Each application for the use of the high-
ways upon the payment of the tax herein provided
for shall be accompanied by a statement , duly at-
tested before a notary public , to the effect that the
motor vehicle was weighed at the time of the making
of the application and that the weight stated therein
is the true weight of the motor vehicle at such time .
4. In addition to any other penalties now or
hereafter imposed , any misrepresentation with re-
spect to the weight of a motor vehicle shall be
grounds for the revocation of any permit or certifi-
cate in the manner provided for the revocation of
permits and certificates in section 2739j -89 , Car
roll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition .

CHAPTER 172 777
§ 5. After paying the excise tax no operator of
a motor vehicle , subject to the provisions of this
act, shall increase the weight of a motor vehicle by
any other than ordinary repairs without first ob-
taining the permission of the Director of the Divi-
sion of Motor Transportation and paying the addi-
tional excise tax on the weight by which the weight
of the motor vehicle is so increased .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 172 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend and partially re -enact Sections 165a -23
to and including 165a -61 of Baldwin's 1936 revision of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , and being a part of Chapter seventeen of
the Acts of the General Assembly of 1932 , relating to banking
and securities .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Definitions . When used in this act the fol-
lowing terms shall, unless the text otherwise indi-
cates , have the following respective meanings :
(a) "Security " shall include any note , stock ,
treasury stock , bond , debenture , transferable share ,
voting trust certificate , evidence of indebtedness ,
certificate of interest or participation , certificate of
interest in a profit -sharing agreement , certificate of
interest in an oil , gas or mining lease , royalty or
title , collateral trust certificate , pre -organization
certificate , pre -organization subscription , any share ,
investment contract or beneficial interest in or title
to property , profits or earnings or any other instru-
ment commonly known as a security.
(b) " Person " shall include a natural person, a
corporation created under the laws of this or any
other state , country , sovereignty , or political sub-

778 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
division thereof , a partnership , an association , a
joint stock company , a trust and any unincorporated
organization . As used herein the term " trust " shall
be deemed to include a common law trust , but shall
not include a trust created or appointed under or by
virtue of a last will and testament , or by a court of
law or equity, or any public charitable trust .
(c) The terms " sale , " " sell , " " offer to sell , " or
"offer for sale ," shall include every sale , resale or
disposition of, attempt or offer or solicitation to dis-
pose of, or solicitation of an offer to buy, or public
offering, promotion , repromotion , or repromotional
distribution , of a security or interest in a security ,
for value ; and shall include an exchange , an option
of sale , a subscription or an offer to sell or to invite
offers for or inquiries about securities by personal
solicitation , directly or by telephone , or by an agent ,
or by letter , circular , radio , advertisement or other-
wise , except that such terms shall not include pre-
liminary negotiations or agreements between an
issuer and any underwriter . Any security given or
delivered with , or as a bonus on account of , any pur-
chase of securities , or any other thing , shall be con-
clusively presumed to constitute a part of the sub-
ject of such purchase and to have been sold for
value .
(d ) " Dealer " shall include every person other
than a salesman who in this state engages either
for all or part of his time directly or through an
agent in the business of selling any security whether
exempt or not exempt issued by another person or
purchasing or otherwise acquiring such securities
from another for the purpose of reselling them or
offering them for sale to the public , or offering , buy-
ing selling or otherwise dealing or trading in securi-
ties as agent or principal for a commission or at a
profit , or who deals in futures or differences in mar-

CHAPTER 172 779
ket quotations of prices or values of any securities
or accepts margins on purchases or pretended pur-
chases or sales of such securities , provided that the
word " dealer " shall not include a person having no
place of business in this state who sells or offers to
sell securities exclusively to brokers or dealers actu-
ally engaged in buying and selling securities as a
business . An issuer of securities , selling such se-
curities in this state , shall be deemed to be a dealer .
Provided that banks and trust companies that in the
ordinary course of their business purchase and sell
notes that are secured by a first lien on real prop-
erty situated in, Kentucky , the total value of which
notes do not exceed 70 per cent of the fair and rea-
sonable value of the property on which they are a
lien and do not exceed twenty -five thousand dollars ,
shall not be dealers within the meaning of this sec-
tion or act .
(e ) " Issuer " shall mean and include every
person who proposes to issue , has issued , or shall
hereafter issue any security . Any natural person
who acts as a promoter for and on behalf of a cor-
poration trust or unincorporated association or
partnership of any kind to be formed shall be
deemed to be an issuer .
(f) " Salesman " shall include every natural per-
son , other than a dealer , employed or appointed or
authorized by a dealer , or issuer to sell , buy , offer
for sale , offer to buy or otherwise deal or trade in
securities in any manner in this state . The part-
ners of a partnership and the executive officers of a
corporation or other association registered as a
dealer shall not be salesmen within the meaning of
this definition .
(g) " Agent " shall mean salesman as herein-
above defined .

780 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(h) " Director " shall mean the Director of the
Division of Securities .
(i) " Broker " shall mean dealer as herein de-
fined .
(j ) "Fraud " and " fraudulent " shall include
any misrepresentation in any manner of a relevant
fact, such misrepresentation being intentional or
due to gross negligence ; any promise or representa-
tion or prediction as to the future not made honestly
or in good faith , or the intentional failure to disclose
a material fact : Provided , however , that nothing
contained herein shall limit or diminish the full
meaning of the terms " fraud " and " fraudulent " as
applied or accepted in courts of law or equity.
§ 2. Exempt Securities . Except as hereinafter
otherwise expressly provided , the provisions of this
act shall not apply to any of the following classes
of securities :
(a ) Any security issued or guaranteed by the
United States or any territory or any insular pos-
session thereof or by the District of Columbia or
by any state or political subdivision or agency
thereof .
(b ) Any security issued or guaranteed by any
foreign government with which the United States
is at the time of the sale or offer of sale thereof
maintaining diplomatic relations , or by any state ,
province or political subdivision thereof having the
power of taxation or assessment .
(c) Any security issued or payment thereof
guaranteed by a national bank or by a federal land
bank or joint stock land bank or national farm loan
association under the provisions of the Federal
Farm Loan Act of July 17 , 1916 , or by any corpora-
tion created or acting as an instrumentality of the
government of the United States pursuant to au-

CHAPTER 172 781
thority granted by the Congress of the United
States ; provided , that such corporation is subject
to supervision or regulation by the government of
the United States .
(d ) Any security issued or guaranteed as to
principal and interest or dividend by a corporation
owning or operating a railroad or any other public
service utility ; provided , that such corporation is
subject to regulation or supervision as to its rates.
and charges or as to the issue of its owns securities
and those guaranteed by it by a public commission ,
board or officer of the government of the United
States , or any state , territory or insular posses-
sion thereof , or any municipality located therein , or
of the District of Columbia , or of the Dominion of
Canada, or any province thereof ; also equipment
notes or bonds based upon chattel mortgages , leases
or agreements for conditional sale of cars , motive
power or other rolling stock mortgaged , leased or
sold to or furnished for the use of or upon a rail-
road or other public service utility corporation , or
equipment trust certificates , or equipment notes or
bonds where the ownership or title of such equipment
is pledged or retained in accordance with the provi-
sions of the laws of the United States , or any state ,
or the Dominion of Canada , to secure the payment
of such equipment trust certificates , bonds or notes ;
also bonds , notes or other evidences of indebtedness
issued by a holding corporation and secured by col-
lateral consisting of any securities hereinabove in
this clause ( d ) described ; provided , that the col-
lateral securities equal in fair value at least 125 per
centum of the par value of the bonds , notes or other
evidences of indebtedness so secured .
(e) Any security issued by a corporation organ-
ized exclusively for educational , benevolent , frater-
nal , charitable or reformatory purposes and not for

782 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
pecuniary profit , and no part of the net earnings of
which inures to the benefit of any private stock-
holder or individual .
(f) Securities issued , outstanding and fully
listed on the New York Stock Exchange , New York
Curb Exchange , and the Chicago Stock Exchange
which securities have been so listed upon application
of the issuer and pursuant to official authorization
by such exchanges , and also all securities of equal
or senior rank to any securities so listed , or evi-
dences of indebtedness guaranteed by companies any
stock of which is so listed ; such securities to be
exempt only so long as such listing shall remain in
effect : provided the Director may at any time for
cause withdraw the exemption allowed by this sec-
tion from any security dealt in on any stock ex-
change herein mentioned .
(g ) Any security a direct obligation of or pay-
ment thereof guaranteed by a state bank , trust com-
pany, title company , real estate mortgage company
engaged in the business of making mortgage loans
on real estate and the selling and offering for sale
to the public the evidences of such loans , whether
notes or bonds , or its own obligations secured by
such notes or bonds , or savings institutions incor-
porated under the laws of this or any state subject
to the examination , supervision and control of this
or any state or territory of the United States or any
insular possession thereof .
(h ) Negotiable promissory notes or commercial
paper issued , given or acquired in a bona fide way
in the ordinary of legitimate business , trade or com-
merce , where such notes or commercial paper are
not made the subject of a public offering .
(i ) Fifty ( 50 ) per cent of the capital stock first
issued by a corporation organized under the laws
of this state for the purpose of doing business in this

CHAPTER 172 783
state , to enable it to transact business under Section
543 of the Kentucky Statutes where no expense in
excess of one per cent of the proceeds from the sale.
of its capital stock so issued is incurred , and no
commission , compensation or other remuneration is
paid or given for or in connection with the sale or
disposition of such capital stock , and no part of the
issue so disposed of is issued directly or indirectly
in payments for patents , services , good will , trade
marks , leases , copyrights , processes , formulae or
other intangible assets . The president and secretary
of the corporation shall prior to such disposal , file
with the Director a written verified statement set-
ting forth the existence of the above facts , and that
the corporation is formed for the purpose of doing
business in this state .
§ 3. Exempt Transactions . Except as herein-
after expressly provided the provisions of this act
shall not apply to the sale of any security in any of
the following transactions :
( a) At any judicial , executor's , administrator's
guardian's , or other fiduciary's sale , or at any sale
by a receiver or trustee in insolvency or bankruptcy .
(b ) By or for the account of a pledge holder or
mortgagee selling or offering for sale or delivery
in the ordinary course of business and not for the
purpose of avoiding the provisions of this act , to
liquidate a bona fide debt , a security pledged in good
faith as security for such debt .
( c ) An isolated transaction in which any securi-
ty is bought , sold , offered for sale , subscription or
delivery by the owner thereof , or his representative
for the owner's account , such purchase , sale or of-
fer for sale , subscription or delivery not being made
in the course of repeated and successive transac-
tions of a like character by such owner or on his
accounts by such representative , and such owner or

784 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
representative not being the underwriter of such
security .
( d ) The distribution by a corporation actively
engaged in the business authorized by its charter
of capital stock , bonds or other securities to its stock-
holders or other security holders as a stock dividend
or other distribution out of earnings or surplus , or
the issue of securities to the security holders or
other creditors of a corporation in the process of
bona fide reorganization of such corporation made
in good faith and not for the purpose of avoiding
the provisions of this act , either in exchange for the
securities of such security holders or claims of such
creditors or partly for cash and partly in exchange
for the securities or claims of such security holders
or creditors , or the issue of increased capital stock
of a corporation sold or distributed by it directly or
through an agent entirely among its own stockhold-
ers , or their assigns , where no commission or other
remuneration is paid or given directly or indirectly
in connection with the sale or distribution of such
increased capital stock .
( e ) The sale , transfer or delivery of securities
to any broker or dealer ; provided , that such broker
or dealer is actually engaged in buy-ing and selling
securities as a business ; and provided further that
such broker or dealer must qualify such securities
before selling or offering them for sale to the public .
(f) The transfer or exchange by one corporation
to another corporation of their own securities in
connection with a consolidation or merger of such
corporations .
(g) Bonds or notes secured by mortgage upon
real estate so long as the entire mortgage together
with all of the bonds or notes secured thereby are
sold to a single purchaser at a single sale .
(h ) Any security other than common stock out-

CHAPTER 172 785
standing and in the hands of the public for a period
of not less than 18 months upon which no default
in payment of principal , interest or dividends exists ,
and upon which no such default has occurred for a
continuous immediately preceding period of five
years .
(i) Transactions by registered dealers , executed
upon customers ' orders on any exchange or in the
open or counter market , but not the solicitation of
such orders , where there is no intent to avoid the
provisions of this act or not involving a public of-
fering : Provided , That such dealer shall keep and
maintain for a period of two years from the date of
the order a record of all such transactions executed
upon customers ' orders , giving the name and ad-
dress of each such customer , the name and identity
of the security or securities involved , the date of
such transactions , the price paid for or received for
such security or securities , and the commission or
other expenses charged to such customer .
§ 4. Securities Required to be Registered-
Resale Rights and Conversions. No securities , ex-
cept of a class exempt under any of the provisions
of section 2 of this act , or unless sold in any trans-
action exempt under any of the provisions of section
3 of this act , shall be sold within this state unless
and until such securities have been registered by
notification or by qualification as hereinafter pro-
vided , unless they fall within the classification of
securities to be registered for resale or for trading
purposes , as provided in section 9 of this act . How-
ever , registration of any security shall be deemed to
include the registration of any right or conversionprvilege embodied in or made a part of such security
but the registration of such right or privilege shall
not be deemed to be a registration of the security
to be issued or exchanged for such right or privilege ,

786 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and such security to be exchanged for such right or
privilege shall be registered before such exchange ,
and if such right or privilege is immediately exer-
cisable upon its registration such security to be ex-
changed for such right or privilege shall be regis-
tered at the same time the security bearing such
right or privilege is registered : Provided , further ,
that the exemption of sales of securities in the trans-
actions enumerated in said section 3 of this act shall
have application only to the sales of such securities
as are directly involved in such transactions , and
such securities shall not thereafter be sold unless
and until duly registered or subsequently sold under
the exemption provisions of said section 2 or 3.
A record of the registration of securities shall be
entered in a register of securities to be kept in the
office of the Divisions of Securities , in which regis-
ter of securities shall also be recorded any orders ,
entered by the Director with respect to such securi-
ties , and such register and application with respect
to the securities registered therein shall be open to
public inspection .
§ 5. Registration by Notification . The following
classes of securities shall be entitled to registration
by notification in the manner provided in this sec-
tion :
(a ) Securities issued by a corporation , partner-
ship, association , company , syndicate or trust own-
ing a property , business or industry which has been
in continuous operation not less than three years or
which is a consolidation or merger of a property ,
business or industry which has been in continuous
operation for not less than three years, and which
has shown during a period of not less than two
years or more than ten years next prior to the close
of its fiscal year preceding the offering of such se-
curities , average annual net earnings , after deduct-

CHAPTER 172 787
ing all prior charges not including the charges upon
securities to be retired out of the proceeds of sale ,
as follows :
( 1 ) In the case of interest bearing securities , not
less than one and one-half times the annual interest
charge thereon and upon all other outstanding in-
terest bearing obligations of equal rank .
(2 ) In the case of preferred stock , not less than
one and one-half times the annual dividend require-
ments of such preferred stock and on all other out-
standing stock of equal or senior rank .
(3 ) In the case of common stock , not less than
six per centum upon all outstanding common stock
of equal or senior rank , together with the amount
of common stock then offered for sale reckoned upon
the price at which such stock is then offered for sale
or sold.
(b) Bonds or notes secured by first mortgage
upon real estate leased to a corporation for a term
of years at a net rental sufficient to pay the interest
and to retire the principal of all bonds or notes se-
cured by said mortgage during the term of the lease
where the lease is irrevocable and is pledged under
the mortgage securing said bonds or notes .
(c ) Any bond or note secured by first mortgage
upon agricultural lands used and valuable princi-
pally for agricultural purposes ( not including oil ,
gas or mining property or leases ) , or upon city , town
or village real estate or leaseholds situated in any
state or territory of the United States or in the Dis-
trict of Columbia or in the Dominion of Canada as
follows :
(1 ) When the mortgage is a first mortgage upon
such agricultural lands , used and valuable princi-
pally for agricultural purposes , and when the ag-
gregate face value of such bonds or notes not includ-

788 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ing interest notes or coupons , secured thereby does
not exceed 70 per centum of the then fair market
value of said lands without improvements , plus 70
per centum of the insured value of any improve-
ments thereon ; or
(2 ) When the mortgage is a first mortgage upon
city, town or village real estate or leaseholds , and
when the aggregate face value of such bonds or
notes , not including interest notes or coupons , se-
cured by such real estate or leaseholds , does not
exceed 60 per centum of the then fair market value
of said mortgaged real estate or leaseholds , respec-
tively , including any improvement appurtenant
thereto , and when said mortgage property is used
principally to produce through rental a net annual
income , after deducting operating expenses and
taxes , or has a fair rental value after deducting op-
erating expenses and taxes , at least equal to the an-
nual interest plus not less than 3 per centum of the
principal of said mortgage indebtedness ; or
( 3) When the mortgage is a first mortgage upon
city, town or village real estate or leaseholds upon
which real estate or leaseholds a building or build-
ings is or are about in good faith forthwith to be
erected according to the expressed terms of the
mortgage , and when reasonably adequate provision
has been made for financing the full completion of
said building free and clear of any lien superior to
said mortgage , and when the aggregate face value of
the bonds or notes , not including interest notes or
coupons , secured by first mortgage does not exceed
70 per centum of the fair market value of such mort-
gaged property , including the building or buildings
to be erected thereon as aforesaid , and when said
mortgaged property will have a net annual income ,
after deducting operating expenses and taxes , at
least equal to the annual interest plus not less than

CHAPTER 172 789
3 per centum of the principal of said mortgage in-
debtdness ; provided , that all advertisements , cir-
culars and letters advertising the sale of said bonds
or notes and all receipts of payments therefor shall
bear in bold type upon the face thereof a legend
stating that said bonds or notes are construction
bonds or notes and all other written or printed of-
ferings of said bonds or notes shall contain a state-
ment to the same effect .
The provisions of this subsection ( c) shall not
apply in the case of bonds or notes secured wholly
or partly by first mortgage on leaseholds , the value
of which leaseholds is required to meet the ratio of
property value to face value of obligation above in
clauses ( 2 ) and ( 3 ) provided , unless all advertise-
ments , circulars and letters advertising the sale of
said bonds or notes and all receipts of payments
therefor , and said bonds and notes shall be in bold
type not less than 18 points upon the face thereof a
legend stating that said bonds or notes are secured
wholly or partly by mortgage on a leasehold , as the
case may be, and all other written or printed of-
ferings of said bonds or notes shall contain a state-
ment to the same effect .
When used in this subsection ( c) the term " Mort-
gage " shall be deemed to include a deed of trust to
secure a debt .
§ 6. Registration by Notification- Procedure-
Information Required- Fees . Securities entitled to
registration by notification shall be registered by
the filing by the issuer or by any registered dealer
interested in the sale thereof in the office of the Di-
rector of a statement with respect to such securities
containing the following :
(a) Name of issuer .
(b) A brief description of the security including
amount of the issue .

790 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(c ) Amount of securities to be offered in the
state .
(d ) A brief statement of the facts which show
that the security falls within one of the classes de-
fined in section 5.
( e ) The price at which the securities are to be
offered for sale .
In the case of securities falling within the class
defined by subsections ( a ) or ( b ) , if the prospectus
to be used for public offering is not filed with the
statement , then a copy of such prospectus shall be
filed in the office of the Director within five days
thereafter or within such further time as the Director
may allow
In the case of securities falling within the class de-
fined by subsection ( c ) , the prospectus to be used for
the public offering shall be filed with the statement .
The filing of such statement in the office of the
Director and the payment of the fee hereinafter
provided shall constitute the registration of such
security. Upon such registration , such securities
may be sold in this state by any registered dealer
giving notice in the manner hereinafter provided in
section twelve , subject , however , to the further or-
der of the Director as hereinafter provided .
If , at any time in the opinion of the Director the
information contained in the statement or pro-
spectus filed is misleading , incorrect , inadequate or
incomplete , or the sale or offering for sale of the
security may work or tend to work a fraud or im-
position the Director may require from the person
filing such statement such further information as
may in his judgment be necessary to establish the
classification of such security as claimed in said
statement or to enable the Director to ascertain
whether the sale of such security would be fraudu-
lent or deceitful , or would result in fraud , or imposi-

CHAPTER 172 791
tion and the Director may also suspend the right to
sell such security pending further investigation by
entering an order specifying the grounds for such
action , and by notifying personally by mail , tele-
phone or telegraph the person filing such statement
and every registered dealer who shall have notified
the Director of an intention to sell such security .
The refusal to furnish information required by the
Director within a reasonable time to be fixed by
the Director may be a proper ground for the entry
of such order of suspension . Upon the entry of any
such order of suspension no further sales of such
security shall be made until further order of the
Director .
In the event of the entry of such order of suspen-
sion the Director shall upon request give a prompt
hearing to the parties interested . If no hearing is
requested within a period of twenty ( 20 ) days from
the entry of such order or if upon such hearing the
Director shall determine that any such security does
not fall within a class entitled to registration under
this section , or that the sale thereof would be fraudu-
lent or deceitful , or would result in fraud or imposi-
tion he shall enter a final order prohibiting sales of
such security , with his findings with respect thereto ,
provided that if the finding with respect to such
security is that it is not entitled to registration under
this section , the applicant may apply for registration
by qualification by complying with the requirements
of section seven . Appeals from such final order may
be taken as hereinafter provided . If however , upon
such hearing , the Director shall find that the se-
curity is entitled to registration under this section ,
and that its sale will neither be fraudulent or deceit-
ful nor result in fraud or imposition , he shall forth-
with enter an order revoking such order of suspen-
sion and such security shall be restored to its status

792 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
as a security registered under this section , as of the
date of such order of suspension .
At the time of filing the application as herein-
before prescribed in this section , the applicant shall
pay to the Division of Securities a fee of one -twen-
tieth of one per centum ( 1/20th of 1 % ) of the ag-
gregate price at which such securities are to be sold
in this state , but in no case shall such fee be less than
twenty -five dollars or more than one thousand dol-
lars for each class of securities for which registra-
tion is sought .
§ 7. Registration by Qualification - Procedure—
Information Required- Fees . All securities re-
quired by this act to be registered before being sold
in this state , and not entitled to registration by no-
tification , shall be registered by qualification in the
manner provided by this section , unless they fall
within the classification of securities to be regis-
tered for resale or for trading purposes , as provided
in section 9 of this act .
The Director shall receive and act upon applica-
tions to have securities registered by qualification ,
and may prescribe forms on which he may require
such applications to be submitted . Applications shall
be in writing and shall be duly signed and sworn
to by the applicant , and filed in the office of the Divi-
sion of Securities and may be made either by the
issuer of the securities for which registration is
applied or by any registered dealer desiring to sell
same within this state .
The Director shall require the applicant to sub-
mit to him the following information respecting the
issuer and such other information as he may in his
judgment deem necessary to enable him to ascertain
whether such securities shall be registered pursuant
to the provisions of this section :

CHAPTER 172 793
(a ) The name under which the issuer is doing or
intends to do business .
(b ) The name of the state or other sovereign
power under which the issuer is organized .
(c) The location of the issuer's principal busi-
ness office and of its principal office in this state , if
any .
(d) The names and addresses of the directors ,
trustees , and officers , if the issuer be a corporation
or association or trust organized or existing under
the common law (as hereinbefore defined ) of all
partners , if the issuer be a partnership , and of the
issuer , if the issuer be an individual .
(e) The purpose of incorporation ( if incorpo-
rated ) and the general character of business actu-
ally to be transacted by the issuer , and the purpose
of the proposed issue .
(f) A statement of capitalization of the issuer ;
a balance sheet showing the amount and general
character of its assets and liabilities on a day not
more than ninety ( 90 ) days prior to the date of
filing such balance sheet ; a detailed statement of the
plan upon which the issuer proposes to transact
business ; a copy of the security for the registration
of which the application is made ; and a copy of all
circulars , prospectuses , advertisements or other de-
scriptions of such securities then prepared by or for
such issuer and or by or for such applicant (if the
applicant shall not be the issuer ) to be used for dis-
tribution or publication in this state .
(g ) A statement of the amount of the issuer's
income , expenses and charges during the last three
years , or if in actual business less than three years
then for such time as the issuer has been in actual
business .
(h) A statement showing the price at which such
security is proposed to be sold , together with the

794 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
maximum amount of commission or other form re-
muneration to be paid in cash or otherwise , directly
or indirectly , for or in connection with the sale or
offering for sale of such securities .
(i ) A detailed statement showing the items of
cash , property , services , patents , good -will and any
other consideration for which such securities have
been or are to be issued in payment , and the Director
may fix the amount of stocks , bonds or other securi-
ties that may be issued by any corporation , foreign
or domestic , in payments for property , patents ,
good -will , promotion and intangible assets at a value
he should find same to be worth ,
(j ) The amount of capital stock which is to be
set aside and disposed of as promotion stock , and a
statement of all stock issued from time to time as
promotion stock .
(k) If the issuer is a corporation , there shall be
filed with the application a certified copy of its ar-
ticles of incorporation with all amendments and
of its existing by-laws , if not already filed in the of-
fice of secretary of this state . If the issuer be a
trustee there shall be filed with the application a
copy of all instruments by which the trust is created
or declared and in which it is accepted and acknowl-
edged . If the issuer is a partnership or an unincor-
porated association , or joint stock company , or any
other form of organization whatsoever there shall
be filed with the application a copy of its articles of
partnership or association and all other papers per-
taining to its organization if not already filed in the
office of the secretary of state of this state .
(1 ) The names and addresses of the underwrit-
ers , if any .
( m ) A copy of any agreement or agreements ( or ,
if identic agreements are used , the forms thereof )
made with any underwriter or dealer ,

CHAPTER 172 795
The Director may in his discretion limit the
amount of securities that may be registered at any
one time of any one issue .
All of the statements , exhibits and documents of
every kind required by the Director under this sec-
tion , except properly certified public documents ,
shall be verified by the oath of the applicant or of
the issuer in such manner and form as may be re-
quired by the Director .
At the time of filing the application as hereinbe-
fore prescribed in this section , the applicant shall
pay to the Division of Securities a fee of one - twen-
tieth of one per centum ( 1/ 20th of 1 % ) of the aggre-
gate price at which such securities are to be sold
in this state , but in no case shall such fee be less
than twenty-five dollars or more than one thousand
dollars for each class of securities for which regis-
tration is sought .
If upon examination of any application the Direc-
tor shall find that the sale of security referred to
therein would not be fraudulent or deceitful or would
not work or tend to work a fraud or imposition upon
the purchaser , or that the enterprise or business of
the issuer is not based upon unsound business prin-
ciples , then upon the payment of the fee provided
in this section , he shall record the registration of
such security in the register of securities , and there-
upon such security so registered may be sold by the
issuer or by any registered dealer who has notified
the Director of his intention so to do , in the manner
hereinafter provided in section twelve , subject , how-
ever , to the further order of the Director as here-
inafter provided .
-$ 8 . Escrow Agreements Impounding of
Funds . If the statement containing information as
to securities , as provided for in section seven of this
act , shall disclose that any such securities shall have

796 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
been or shall be intended to be issued for any patent
right , copyright , trade-mark , process , lease , formu-
lae or good -will, or for promotion fees or expenses
or for other intangible assets , the amount and nature
thereof shall be fully set forth and the Director shall
require that such securities so issued in payment of
such patent right , copyright , trade-mark , process ,
lease , formulae or good -will , or for promotion fees
or expenses , or for other intangible assets shall be
delivered in escrow to the Director under an escrow
agreement that the owners of such securities shall
not be entitled to withdraw such securities from
escrow until all other stockholders who have paid
for their stock in cash shall have been paid for two
years a dividend or dividends aggregating not less
than six per cent , shown to the satisfaction of said
Director to have been actually earned on the invest-
ment in any common stock so held, and in case of
dissolution or insolvency during the time such se-
curities are held in escrow , that the owners of such
securities shall not participate in the assets until
after the owners of all other securities shall have
been paid in full .
Whenever it shall appear in the filing of an ap-
plication for registration under section 7 of this act
that the funds to be obtained from the sale of any
security sought to be registered are to be used for
promotional purposes , The Director shall cause to
be impounded in a bank or trust company selected
by the issuer , and approved by the Director , such
an amount from the proceeds of the sale of the se-
curities as will insure completion of the promotional
project ; but in no event shall the amount so im-
pounded be less than fifty ( 50 % ) per cent of the
total amount of money to be raised from the sale of
the securities registered .
9. Registration of Outstanding Securities for

CHAPTER 172 797
Resale or for Trading Purposes . Any security the
entire issue of which has been issued and is out-
standing in the hands of the public , or any stock of
the same rank and parity lawfully issued by way of
stock split -up or a stock dividend thereon , and which
may not be lawfully sold or offered for sale by rea-
sons of an exemption under section 2 of this act , or
in an exempted transaction under section 3 of this
act, may be registered for resale or for dealing in or
trading purposes by filing by a registered dealer or
registered dealers in the office of the Director :
1. ( a ) The name and description of the security
sought to be registered .
(b) Notice of intention to offer for resale or to
deal or trade generally in any such security .
2. If requested by the Director there shall also
be filed :
(a ) A statement , with such supporting data or
verifications as may be available , evidencing the fact
that such security and the entire issue of which such
security is a part was issued and was outstanding
in the hands of the public prior to the effective date
of this act , or that such security consists of stock of
the same rank or parity lawfully issued by way of
stock split -up or a stock dividend thereon ;
(b ) The price at which such security is to be of-
fered for resale , if presently known , and if not
known then the method or formula for arriving
at the offering price from time to time and such ad-
ditional information as the dealer has tending to
establish the fairness of such price ;
(c ) A copy of the latest available balance sheet
or other available financial data tending to establish
the condition of the issuer thereof .
If it shall appear to the Director from the in-
formation so filed with him or otherwise available to
him that such security , or the price at which it is to

798 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
be sold , or the methods of the sale are such as would
work or tend to work a fraud on the purchasers
thereof , the Director shall , after notice and oppor-
tunity to be heard or after a hearing thereon , re-
fuse registration thereof ; otherwise he shall regis-
ter such security for resale or for dealing and trad-
ing generally therein by registered dealers , subject
to the provisions of Sections 10 and 18 .
A registration fee of $25.00 shall be paid at the
time of registration under this section .
The Director may duly fix at not exceeding twenty
per centum the maximum amount of commission or
other form of remuneration which the Dealer sub-
mitting application for registration under this sec-
tion may receive .
§ 10. Consent to Service . Upon any application
for registration by notification under section five
made by an issuer , and upon any application for
registration by qualification under section 7, whether
made by an issuer or registered dealer, where the
issuer is not domiciled in this state , there shall be
filed with such application the irrevocable written
consent of the issuer that suits and actions , growing
out of the violation of any provision or provisions of
this act , may be commenced against it in the proper
court of any county in this state in which the plain-
tiff may reside , or in which the contract was made ,
by the service of any process or pleading authorized
by the laws of this state , on the Director of the Di-
vision of Securities , said consent stipulating and
agreeing that such service of such process or plead-
ings on such Director shall be taken and held in all
courts to be as valid and binding as if due service
had been made upon the issuer himself , and said
written consent shall be authenticated by the seal
of said issuer, if it has a seal , and by the acknowl-
edged signature of a member of the copartnership

CHAPTER 172 799
or company , or by the acknowledged signature of
any officer of the incorporated or unincorporated as-
sociation , if it be an incorporated or unincorporated
association , duly authorized by resolution of the
board of directors , trustees or managers of the cor-
poration or association , and shall in such case be
accompanied by a duly certified copy of the resolu-
tion of the board of directors , trustees or managers
of the corporation or association , authorizing the
officers to execute the same . In case any process
or pleadings mentioned in this act are served upon
the Director , it shall be by duplicate copies , one of
which shall be filed in the office of the Director and
the other immediately forwarded by registered mail
to the principal office of the issuer to an address
which once filed shall be conclusively considered the
permanent address of the issuer until the Director
has been notified of a new one , against which said
process or pleadings are directed .
§ 11. Revocation , Suspension , of Issuer's Regis-
tration -Annual Reports of Issuers- Examination
of Issuer's Affairs-Termination of Registration .
The Director may revoke the registration of any
security by entering an order to that effect , with
his findings in respect thereto , if upon examination
into the affairs of the issuer of such security , either
in the performance of routine duties , or upon field
examination , or upon hearing , it shall appear that
the issuer of the security :
(a) is insolvent ; or
(b) is of bad business repute ; or
(c) does not conduct its business in accordance
with law ; or
( d ) that its affairs are in an unsound condi-
tion ; or
(e ) that the enterprise or business of such issuer

800 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or the security is not based upon sound business
principles ; or
(f) that the issuer of the security has violated
any of the provisions of this act , or any order of the
Director of which such person had notice , relating
to the registration or sale of such security ; or
(g) has been or is engaged or is about to engage
in fraudulent conduct with regard to issues being
issued or about to be issued by it , or with regard to
issues registered by it ; or
(h ) is in any other way acting dishonestly or has
made any fraudulent representations in any pro-
spectus or in any circular or other literature dis-
tributed concerning the issuer or its securities , or
the security registered ; or
(i ) has knowingly made or caused to be made to
the Director any false representation of a material
fact, or has suppressed or withheld from the Direc-
tor any formation which such person possesses ,
and which if submitted by him would have rendered
such security incompetent to be registered under
this act ; or
(j ) has refused to permit an examination to be
made by the Director , or has failed to file any an-
nual report required by this act , within the time
fixed by the Director for filing such report , or to pay
the fee required for the filing and examination of
any such report , or to file any report or furnish any
information that may be required by the Director
under the provisions of this act .
In connection with any such examination the Di-
rector may require a balance sheet exhibiting the
assets and liabilities of any such issuer or his income
statement , or both , to be certified to by a public ac-
countant either in this state or in any other state
where the issuer's business is located , approved by
the Director .

CHAPTER 172 801
Whenever the Director may deem it necessary , he
may also require such balance sheet or income state-
ment , or both , to be made more specific in such par-
ticulars as such Director shall point out or to be
brought down to the latest practicable date .
A verified annual report in such form as the Di-
rector may prescribe giving complete current finan-
cial information regarding each security and the
issuer of each security registered , including therein
a report as to the number of securities sold in Ken-
tucky , and the aggregate sale price thereof , by
virtue of such registration , shall be filed with the
Director by the issuer as of the close of business.
December 31st of each year , or as of such other
dates as fixed by the Director , such reports to be on
file with the Director within sixty days following
such dates and the filing thereof to be accompanied
by a filing fee of $ 5.00 . Discontinuance of such an-
nual reports shall take place upon the filing of a
verified statement showing either that such security
is fully sold and outstanding, or that the issuer de-
sires cancellation by the Director of the unsold part
of such issue of such registered security , whereupon
appropriate orders shall be entered by the Director ,
but such discontinuance of reports or such cancella-
tion shall not prevent the Director from investi-
gating such issuer or any sale or sales or any cir-
cumstances connected with the registration or sale
of such security or from taking any action author-
ized by law .
If the Director shall deem it necessary he may en-
ter an order suspending the right to sell securities
pending any investigation , provided that the order
shall state the Director's grounds for taking such
action .
Notice of the entry of such order shall be given
in writing , served personally or sent by mail or by

telegraph , to the issuer and to every registered
dealer who shall have notified the Director of an

Before any final order of revocation is entered
the issuer shall, upon application , be entitled to a
hearing .

the applicant's principal place of business is located ,

Every dealer shall file with his application an ir-

Application for registration of an agent shall be

Every registration under this section shall expire

date of registration , but the Director shall renew

scribed by the Director , and the filing of any addi-

Applications for such renewals of registration

newal application shall be treated as an original
application .
The filing fee for registration or renewal thereof
shall be one hundred dollars ( $100 ) in the case of a
dealer , and five dollars ( $5.00 ) in the case of a sales-
man , and such fee shall be paid to the Director at
the time of filing such application for registration
or renewal.
Changes in registration occasioned by changes in
the personnel of a partnership or in the principals ,
copartners , officers , directors , of any dealer may be
made from time to time by written application set-
ting forth the facts with respect to such change .
Every registered dealer who intends to offer any
security of any issue , registered or to be registered ,
shall notify the Director in writing of his intention
to do so . The notice shall contain the name of the
dealer and shall state the name of the security to be
offered for sale and whenever a dealer shall have
prepared such notice and shall have forwarded the
same by mail to the Director , such dealer , as to the
contents of such notice and filing thereof , shall be
