992 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
service . The retirement allowance of each member
whose age of retirement is seventy (70 ) years of age
or over shall be one ( 1 ) per cent of his annual salary
for each year of prior service , plus 1 1/2 per cent
of his annual salary for each year of subsequent
service . No retirement allowance shall be greater
than one-half of the member's annual salary , nor
greater than $ 1,000 . No retirement allowance shall
be less than $ 100 , and any allowance which is com-
puted at less than $ 100 shall be $100 .
§ 44. Disability allowance . Any member, after
he has attained the age of fifty (50 ) , but before he
has attained the age of sixty ( 60) years , who has
completed twenty ( 20 ) years of accredited service
in the public schools of the state of Kentucky , five
(5 ) of which immediately preceded retirement , may
retire for disability and shall be granted a disability
allowance to be paid from the funds of the retire-
ment system . The disability allowance of each such
member shall be one -half of 1 per cent of his annual
salary for each year of prior service , plus three-
quarters of 1 per cent of his annual salary for each
year of subsequent service . No disability allowance
shall be greater than one -half of the member's an-
nual salary , nor greater than $ 1,000 . No disability
allowance shall be , or shall be reduced to , less than
$100 , and any allowance which is computed to be less
than $100 shall be $100 .
§ 45. Medical examination for disability . Re-
tirement for disability shall be granted only after
the applicant has definitely established his claim
through medical examination . The medical exami-
nation of a member for disability retirement shall be
made upon the application of the member or of a
person acting in his behalf, or the local board of
education of the school district , or the board of
re-
gents or board of trustees of any institution
in

CHAPTER 1 993
which the member is employed , stating that said
member is physically or mentally incapacitated for
the performance of duty and ought to be retired . If
such medical examination conducted by physician or
physicians selected by the board of trustees shows
that said mmeber is physically or mentally incapaci-
tated for the performance of duty and ought to be
retired , the examining physician or physicians shall
so report to the board of trustees and the board of
trustees shall retire said member for disability ,
forthwith .
A member retired for disability shall , until reach-
ing the age of sixty ( 60 ) years , be required to un-
dergo periodic examinations at the discretion of the
board of trustees in order to determine whether the
disability allowance shall be continued . When an
examination shows that the disability has been re-
moved , the allowance shall cease . If the beneficiary
of a disability allowance is able to engage in a gain-
ful occupation other than teaching , the benefit shall
be reduced , until the beneficiary reaches the age of
sixty (60 ) years , to an amount which with the earned
income shall not exceed the average salary for the
five (5 ) years preceding retirement , provided that in
no case shall the disability allowance be reduced to
less than $100 .
§ 46. Payment of annuities . The first payment
to an annuitant shall be made at the payment date
at the end of one full payment period after his re-
tirement and shall consist of one regular quarterly
payment .
§ 47. Refund upon death of beneficiary . In case
of death of a member who has retired by reason of
superannuation or disability , any portion of the
member's contributions including regular interest
to the date of retirement, that has not been paid as
an allowance or benefit in lieu thereof, shall be paid
Acts- 32

994 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
to the member's estate in such manner as the board
of trustees may elect .
$ 48. Benefit options . Until the first payment
of a retirement allowance is made , the beneficiary
may elect to receive retirement allowances in the
form of a life annuity , as provided in Section 43, or
equivalent benefits under such options as the board
of trustees may approve . No such option shall pro-
vide for a benefit whose actuarial value at the age of
retirement is greater than that provided in Section
43. No payment during any year on account of one
beneficiary shall be greater than $ 1,000 under such
options . This section shall not apply to disability
allowances .
$ 49. Provisions for existing systems . A local
teachers ' retirement system may be merged with the
state retirement system whenever a majority of all
members participating in a local retirement system
shall apply for membership in the state teachers '
retirement system created by this act by a petition
duly signed and verified , approved by the local board
of education , and filed with the board of trustees of
the state retirement system . All the members in-
cluded in the membership of such local teacher re-
tirement system shall become members of the state
retirement system created by this act at such time ,
within three ( 3 ) months after the filing of such pe-
tition and after compliance with the other provisions
of this act relative to the dissolution and discon-
tinuance of such local retirement systems , as the
board of trustees shall designate .
If a local retirement system votes to merge with
the state retirement system as provided in this act ,
the local system shall be discontinued ; the payment
of all benefits to members on the retired roll at the
time of discontinuance shall become theobligation
of the school district in which the local system was

CHAPTER 1 995
operated prior to its discontinuance ; and all of the
members of the dissolved retirement system may
become members of the state system on the same
basis as other present teachers in the state and shall
receive the state's contribution for prior service the
same as other present teachers of the state system .
Prior to voting upon merging with the state re-
tirement system , or within thirty ( 30 ) days after a
local retirement system votes to merge with the state
retirement system , the board of directors of the local
retirement system shall employ an expert approved
by the local board of education in which the local
system is operated to evaluate all of the funds of
the local retirement system and compute the present
value of liabilities on account of annuitants on the
roll of local retirement system at the time the local
system is discontinued , and the expenses of such
evaluation shall be borne by the local retirement
system . The expert employed shall present a com-
plete report of his findings to the local board of
education and to the local retirement system .
After the report of the evaluation is filed with the
local board of education and the local retirement sys-
tem and after a majority of the members of the
local retirement system shall have voted to merge
with the state retirement system , the secretary of
the local retirement system shall certify to the board
of trustees of the local retirement system the names
of all members of the local retirement system and
the amount of refundable deposits due each member
in accordance with the laws and regulations of the
local retirement system . Upon approval by the
board of trustees of the local retirement system of
the names and amounts listed in the secretary's re-
port it shall be transmitted to the secretary of the
local board of education .
All funds of the local retirement system shall then

996 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
be transferred to the local board of education , which
within a period of twelve months after the funds are
transferred to the local board of education , shall
pay to members of the local retirement system the
refundable deposits certified to the secretary of the
local board of education by the board of trustees of
the local retirement system . If the remaining sum
is less than the present value of liabilities on account
of annuitants on the rolls of the local system at the
time the local system is discontinued , the local board
of education or the taxing authority which levies
school taxes for the local board of education
upon recommendation of said board of educa-
tion shall annually levy a tax rate sufficient
to discharge in full the obligation to annuitants , pro-
vided that in cities of the first class such levy shall
not exceed (1 ) one cent on each $ 100.00 of assessed
value of property made by such taxing authority .
The levy of such tax shall be mandatory. Payments
to annuitants in cities of the first class shall not ex-
ceed the amount being received by them at the time
such local retirement system is discontinued . What-
ever sum may remain after the death of all an-
nuitants shall be used by the local board of educa-
tion for general school purposes .
In the case of recently inaugurated local retire-
ment systems where there are no annuitants at the
time of the merger of the local and state systems ,
all refundable deposits shall be returned to the indi-
vidual members by the local retirement system , any
proper expense items paid , and the sum remaining
shall be paid to the local board of education to be
used for general public school purposes .
If the merger of the local with the state retirement
system takes place more than a year after the state
system becomes operative , the board of directors of
the local retirement system , prior to transferring

CHAPTER 1 997
their funds to the local board of education , shall
turn over to the state retirement fund such portion
of the local fund as will equal the accumulated con-
tributions of the local system from July 1 , 1940 , to
the date of the merger , and the necessary amount
shall be deducted from the refundable deposits as
certified by the local retirement board . Should the
refundable deposit of any member be less than the
accumulated contributions since July 1 , 1940 , this
member shall be assessed the difference which shall
be collected by the local retirement system and
turned over to the state retirement system at the
time of the merger of the two systems .
The local board of education shall continue to in-
vest the funds transferred to it for the benefit of the
existing annuitants as long as such annuitants shall
live . Such investment shall be governed by the pres-
ent law dealing with the local retirement systems ,
except that the local board of education is substi-
tuted for the board of trustees of the local retire-
ment system . The local board of education shall
keep all funds transferred to it by the local retire-
ment system and all income from the investment of
such funds in a separate fund to be known as the
Annuity Fund . The local board of education is au-
thorized to pay from the Annuity Fund any reason-
able expenses necessary for the fund's administra-
tion and general management . The local board of
education shall safeguard the Annuity Fund by re-
quiring such additional surety bond of the treasurer
as seems necessary to them , by providing for an an-
nual audit by a reputable auditing firm , by spread-
ing on the minutes of the board of education at
least annually , a report of investments , assets and
liabilities , and the names , addresses , and annuities of
annuitants , and by levying such a tax rate or making
such an appropriation to the Annuity Fund from

998 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
local school revenues as will guarantee the full and
complete discharge of all obligations to annuitants .
$ 50. Protection against fraud . Any person who
shall knowingly make any false statement , or shall
falsify or permit to be falsified any record or rec-
ords of this retirement system in any attempt to de-
fraud such system as a result of such act , shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor , and shall be punishable
therefor under the laws of the state of Kentucky .
Should any change or error in record result in any
employee or beneficiary receiving from the retire-
ment system more or less than he would have been
entitled to receive had the records been correct , then ,
on discovery of any such error , the board of trustees
shall correct such error , and , as far as practicable ,
shall adjust the payments in such a manner that the
actuarial equivalent of the benefit to which he was
correctly entitled shall be paid .
§ 51. Exemption from taxation and attachment .
The right of a teacher to a retirement allowance , to
the return of contributions , any benefit or right ac-
crued or accruing to any person under the provi-
sions of this article , and the moneys in the various
funds created hereunder , are hereby exempt from
any state or municipal tax , and shall not be subject
to execution , garnishment , attachment . or any other
process whatsoever , and shall be unassignable ex-
cept as in this article specifically provided .
§ 52. Provided that if any section of this Act
shall be held unconstitutional or invalid , it shall not
affect any of the remaining parts or sections of this
Act ; that it is the intention of the General Assembly
that the remaining parts and sections of this Act
shall be held in full force and effect regardless of
such invalidity or unconstitutionality .
Approved by the Governor : April 21 , 1938 .

CHAPTER 2 999
CHAPTER 2.
AN ACT relating to the creation of a Legislative Council of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , providing for its duties , the
number of members , their qualifications and the manner in
which they shall be appointed , providing for their term of
office and for the filling of vacancies in the membership of
said Council, providing for the maximum number of days said
Council shall meet and the compensation therefor ; setting
forth the powers of said Council ; repealing Sections 4618-138 ,
4618-139 , 4618-140 , 4618-141 and 4618-142 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes, Baldwin's 1936 Revision , and declaring an emergency
to exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby created a Legislative Coun-
cil of twenty -one members , whose duty it shall be :
( a ) To collect information concerning the govern-
ment and general welfare of the State , examine the
effects of previously enacted statutes and recom-
mend amendments thereto , deal with important is-
sues of public policy and questions of statewide in-
terest , and prepare a legislative program in the
form of bills or otherwise , as in its opinion the wel-
fare of the State may require , to be presented at the
next session of the General Assembly ; ( b ) To re-
ceive and consider , after the first Monday in Jan-
uary, 1940 , the reports of the Auditor of Public Ac-
counts and to report to the respective Houses there-
on , for the purpose of perfecting the contact of the
General Assembly with the administration through
the Auditor of Public Accounts and in order to in-
sure that the Auditor's reports shall receive the at-
tention they merit ; and ( c ) To encourage and ar-
range conferences with officials of other states and
of other units of government ; carry forward the
participation of this State as a member of the Coun-
cil of State Governments , both regionally and na-

1000 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tionally and formulate proposals for cooperation be-
tween this State and other States .
§ 2. The Legislative Council shall consist of
eight senators , eight representatives and five admin-
istrative officials of the State government . The eight
senators shall be appointed by the Lieutenant Gov-
ernor , the eight representatives by the Speaker of
the House of Representatives , before the close of
the present extraordinary session of the General
Assembly and at any session of the Legislature ,
special or regular , during the year of a regular ses-
sion , provided , that said appointment shall be made
prior to the first day of July of such year . Such
appointment shall, for house members , be approved
by a majority vote of the House of Representatives
and for senate members shall be approved by a
majority vote of the Senate . The five state officials
shall be named by the Governor of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and the Governor shall be an
honorary member of the Council . The Lieutenant
Governor shall be ex officio member and chairman
and the Speaker of the House of Representatives
shall be ex officio member and Vice Chairman . The
party representation on the Council shall be in pro-
portion generally to the relative members of the
different political parties in each House of the Gen-
eral Assembly, but in no event , shall the majority
party be represented by more than five - eighths of
the members appointed from the House of Repre-
sentatives and by more than five - eighths of the mem-
bers appointed from the Senate .
In the event there shall be a vacancy of the house
appointees , the Speaker of the House is hereby au-
thorized to fill such appointment , whether or not
the General Assembly is in session and such ap-
pointment need not be confirmed by the House of
Representatives .

CHAPTER 2 1001
In the event there shall be a vacancy of the senate
appointees , the Lieutenant Governor is hereby au-
thorized to fill such appointment , whether or not the
General Assembly is in session and such appoint-
ment need not be confirmed by the Senate .
In the event of a vacancy of the members ap-
pointed by the Governor from the officials of the
Commonwealth , said vacancy shall be filled by ap-
pointment by the Governor .
Such members appointed in the manner set forth
in this Act , shall hold their office as members of said
Legislative Council until the next regular session
of the General Assembly or until their successors
are appointed , in the manner prescribed by this Act ,
except that if any House or Senate member ap-
pointed to said Council should fail to be reelected
to either branch of the General Assembly , then the
term of office of such member shall automatically
end on the 15th day of November succeeding the
regular election and at which members of the Gen-
eral Assembly are elected , except that the Lieu-
tenant Governor and Speaker of the House , shall
continue to be ex efficio members of said Council
until their successors are elected and qualified .
§ 3. The Council shall meet as often as may be
necessary to perform its duties , not exceeding forty
days between regular sessions of the General As-
sembly . Meetings of the Council shall be called by
the Chairman , except when fixed by resolution of
the Council ; provided , however , upon written re-
quest of five members , the Chairman shall call a
meeting of the Council , if the Council has not al-
ready met forty days between regular sessions of
the General Assembly .
Members of the Council shall be compensated for
the time spent in attendance at sessions of the Coun-

1002 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cil at the rate of ten dollars per day and for actual
expenses incurred in attending said sessions .
§ 4. In the discharge of any duty herein imposed ,
the Council shall have the authority to administer
oaths , issue subpoenas , compel the attendance of
witnesses and the production of any papers , books ,
accounts , documents and testimony , and to cause the
deposition of witnesses , either residing within or
without the State, to be taken in the manner pre-
scribed by law for taking depositions in civil actions
in the circuit courts . In case of failure on the part
of any person to comply with any subpoena issued in
behalf of the Council or on the refusal of any wit-
ness to testify to any matters regarding which he
may be lawfully interrogated , it shall be the duty of
the circuit court of any county , on application of
the Council , to compel obedience by proceedings for
contempt , as in the case of disobedience of the re-
quirements of a subpoena issued for such court or
a refusal to testify therein . Each witness who ap-
pears before the Legislative Council by its order ,
other than a State officer or employee , shall receive
for his attendance the fees and mileage provided for
witnesses in civil cases in courts of record , which
shall be audited and paid upon the presentation of
proper vouchers sworn to by such witness and ap-
proved by the chairman of the Council .
Each officer , board, commission or department of
State government or any local government , shall
make such studies for the Council as it may require
and as can be made within the limits of its appro-
priation . The Council may employ a research direc-
tor and such assistants and engage the services of
such research agencies as it may deem desirable and
its appropriation permits , in the preparation of a
program of legislation or in regard to any matters

CHAPTER 2 1003
of state - wide public importance within the jurisdic-
tion of the legislative branch .
§ 5. The Governor shall have the right to send
messages to any session of the Council containing
his recommendations and explaining the policy of
the administration .
Any member of the General Assembly shall have
the right to attend any of the sessions of the Coun-
cil , and may present his views on any subject which
the Council may at any particular time be consider-
ing ; but he shall not have the right to participate in
any decision which the Council may make .
$ 6. The Council shall keep complete minutes of
its meetings and shall make periodic reports to all
members of the General Assembly, and keep said
members fully informed of all matters which may
come before the Council , the actions taken thereon ,
and the progress made in relation thereto .
The recommendations of the Council shall be com-
pleted and made public , preferably thirty days prior
to any session of the General Assembly at which
time such recommendations shall be mailed to the
postoffice address of each member of the General As-
sembly . At the same time a copy shall be filed with
the Governor and with each elective State officer ,
and five copies shall be filed with the State Librarian
and five with the Librarian of the University of
Kentucky .
§ 7. Sections 4618-138 , 4618-139 , 4618-140 , 4618-
141 and 4618-142 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
Baldwin's 1936 Revision , are hereby repealed .
§ 8. WHEREAS , the General Assembly is now
in Extraordinary Session and under the terms of
this Act , it is necessary for the appointment of said
members to be made during this session and to be

1004 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
confirmed by the respective Houses , an emergency
is declared to exist and this Act shall become effec-
tive upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved by the Governor : April 9, 1938 .

AN ACT amending and re -enacting section 2739g -2d , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , relating to registration fees
for trucks ; and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That section 2739g- 2d , Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1936 edition , be amended and re -enacted to read
as follows :
Section One . That all motor vehicles , excluding
such as are mentioned in subsection c of Section two
thousand seven hundred and thirty -nine g-2 ( 2739g-
2d ) , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine
hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition , and excluding motor
vehicles engaged in hauling passengers for hire , are
classified as trucks and the annual registration fee
shall be as follows :
" Those having a capacity of one thousand pounds
or less , ten ($10.00 ) dollars ; those having a capacity
of more than one thousand pounds and up to two
thousand pounds , twenty -two ( $22.00 ) dollars ; those
having a capacity of more than two thousand pounds
and up to three thousand pounds , forty ( $40.00 ) dol-
lars ; those having a capacity of more than three
thousand pounds and up to seven thousand pounds ,
fifty-two ( $52.00 ) dollars ; those having a capacity
of more than four thousand pounds and up to five
thousand pounds , sixty -three ( $ 63.00 ) dollars ; those
having a capacity of more than five thousand pounds
and up to six thousand pounds , seventy -one ( $ 71.00 )

CHAPTER 3 1005
dollars ; those having a capacity of more than six
thousand pounds and up to seven thousand pounds ,
ninety -three ( $ 93.00 ) dollars ; those having a ca-
pacity of more than seven thousand pounds and up
to eight thousand pounds , one hundred and twelve
( $ 112.00 ) dollars ; those having a capacity of more
than eight thousand pounds and up to nine thousand
pounds , one hundred and thirty -eight ( $ 138.00 ) dol-
lars ; those having a capacity of more than nine thou-
sand and up to ten thousand pounds , one hundred
and eighty- seven ( $ 187.00 ) dollars ; those having a
capacity of more than ten thousand pounds , two
hundred and thirty ( $230.00 ) dollars ; and twenty-
five ( $25.00 ) dollars for each additional ton or frac-
tion of a ton in excess of ten thousand pounds . The
applicant for license for a motor truck shall state
in his application the capacity of the truck .
" Providing that any person who applies for the
registration of a truck having a capacity of three
thousand pounds or less , in addition to the require-
ments to secure registration for said truck , files with
the county court clerk an affidavit stating that he
is a farmer solely engaged in the production of
crops , livestock or dairy products , and that he owns
a truck of the capacity of three thousand pounds or
less , and that he has caused to be printed upon each
side of the bed of said truck the words ' Farmer's
Truck ' , in red letters not less than three ( 3 ) inches
in height , and that said truck for the next twelve
months shall not be used in the transportation of
anything for hire but is to be used only in transport-
ing persons , food , provender , feed and machinery
used in operating his said farm and the products
grown upon said farm , and for no other purpose ;
then , in that event said person shall be permitted to
register said truck and shall be charged therefor
only four dollars and fifty cents ( $4.50 ) .

1006 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Providing that any person who applies for the
registration of a truck used solely in the transport-
ing , hauling and carrying school children or persons
employed in said school or schools in the district
shall file with the County Court Clerk an affidavit
stating that the truck or trucks are engaged solely
in the transporting , hauling or carrying of said
school children and persons employed in said school
or schools in said district or districts and that he has
caused to be printed on each side of the bed of said
truck and on the rear door or doors or part of the
bed the words " School Bus " in letters of a color
easily seen and sufficiently large and that said truck
for the next twelve months shall not be used for any
other purpose except that stated ; then, in that event
said person or persons shall be permitted to register
said truck and shall be charged therefor only four
dollars and fifty cents ( $4.50 ) .
"Providing that any person who applies for reg-
istration of a truck , in addition to the requirements
to secure registration for said truck , files with the
county clerk an affidavit stating that a well drilling
outfit for the drilling of water wells has been mount-
ed on and bolted to the chassis of the truck, and that
the truck for the next twelve months shall not be
used in the transportation of anything for hire but
is to be used only in the transportation of the well
drilling outfit, in that event said person shall be per-
mitted to register said truck and shall be charged
therefor only four dollars and fifty cents ( $4.50 ) .
Section Two . The registration fees as provided
herein , the issuance of receipts , duplicates , tags, and
the transfer of such receipts and tags , and the ac-
counting for fees , and all other things required to
be done in connection with the same , shall be done as,
and at the time and in the manner as is now or may
hereafter be provided by law. One -half of all

CHAPTER 4 1007
moneys raised by this tax shall be evenly distributed
among the one hundred and twenty counties , one two
hundred and fortieth ( 1/240 ) of all truck licenses
going to each of the counties , for the county road
funds."
Section Three . Whereas , many of the vehicles
covered by this Act have not been licensed and the
present license charges work a hardship upon the
owners thereof , an emergency is declared to exist
and this Act shall be effective from and after its pas-
sage and approval by the Governor .
Approved by the Governor : April 21 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 4 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Definitions . The following terms shall be
construed for purposes of this Act as having the
meaning hereinafter set out , unless clearly used
otherwise :
" Taxpayer " shall mean any person made liable
by law to file a return or pay tax .
"Person " shall mean an individual , partnership ,
association , corporation , bailee , bank , fiduciary or
other person , including any public official or a public
or municipal corporation .
" Fiduciary " shall mean a guardian , trustee , ex-
ecutor , administrator , receiver , conservator , or any
person , individual , or corporate acting in any fidu-
ciary capacity for any other person .
" Commissioner " shall mean Commissioner of
Revenue .

1008 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
"Department " shall mean Department of
Revenue .
"Commission " shall mean Kentucky Tax Com-
mission .
" State agency " or " State agencies " shall mean
departments of the State government having admin-
istrative responsibilities , but shall not include county
or other local officials or departments .
2. Powers and Duties of Department Gen-
erally . Without limitation of other duties assigned
to it by law , the following powers , duties and func-
tions shall be vested in the Department of Revenue :
( 1 ) The Department shall have power to make
rules and regulations and to direct proceedings , ac-

ment of all tax laws of this State .
( 2 ) The Department , by representatives ap-
pointed by it in writing , shall have power to examine
witnesses under oath and take testimony or deposi-

and papers of any taxpayer or of any individual ,
partnership , company , association , corporation ,
pledgee , bailee , State or National bank or fiduciary
having knowledge concerning the tax liability of any
taxpayer , when it deems such an examination rea-
sonably necessary for purposes incident to the per-
formance of its functions . The Department may
enforce this right by application to the circuit court.

The Department may require the commonwealth

CHAPTER 4 1011
( 10 ) It shall be the duty of the Department to
arrange for an annual conference of the county tax
commissioners , or the county officers whose duty it
is to assess property for taxation . Said meeting
shall be held for the purpose of giving systematic
instructions in the fair and just valuation and as-
sessment of every kind of property subject to local
assessment and as to their practical duty in every
step in connection therewith . Said conference shall
continue not more than five days . It shall be the
duty of such officers to attend and take part in such
conference , unless prevented by illness , or by other
reason satisfactory to the Commisioner of Revenue .
Any such officer wilfully failing to attend said con-
ference may be removed from office by the circuit
court of the county wherein he was elected . One -half
of the actual and necessary expenses of any such
officer in attending said conference shall be paid by
the State upon a certification by the Department
if said officer participates in all sessions of said con-
ference , and one-half of the actual and necessary
expenses of such officer attending such conference
shall be paid by the county from which such officer
attends . Each of such officers shall make out an item-
ized statement showing his actual and necessary ex-
penses ; and , if the same shall be found regular and
supported by proper receipts , it shall be approved
by the Department before payment . All other func-
tions formerly vested in the State Tax Commission
shall vest in and be exercised by the Department of
Revenue .
§ 3. Functions of the Kentucky Tax Commis-
sion . The functions of the Kentucky Tax Commis-
sion as an agency of the Department shall be :
(1 ) To assess property insofar as the Depart-
ment may be authorized by law to make such assess-
ments .

1012 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 2 ) To hear and determine appeals from find-
ings by the Department and to review local assess-
ments as provided by law .
(3 ) To promulgate rules and regulations gov-
erning the procedure before the Commission and
authorized for administration of the tax laws .
The commission shall have power to summon and
compel witnesses to appear and give testimony and
to order and compel the production of records ,
books , papers and documents relating to any mat-
ter which the Commission shall have power to in-
vestigate or determine , in the same manner in which
such procedure is effected in circuit courts , and like-
wise to cause the depositions of witnesses residing
within or without the State or absent therefrom to
be taken upon notice to the interested party , if any ;
provided , that if any person or persons shall fail
or refuse to obey the summons or order of the Com-
mission to appear and testify or to produce records ,
the Commission may apply to the Franklin Circuit
Court for proper action to enforce its summons or
order , which court is hereby vested with authority
to enforce said summons or order ; or the Commis-
sion may certify the facts to the commonwealth at-
torney or county attorney of the county of the resi-
dence of the offending party , who shall apply to the
circuit court of his county , which is also vested with
authority to enforce the summons or order . No per-
son shall be excused from testifying or from produc-
ing any book , document , paper , record or account
at any inquiry by or hearing before the Commission
or its referee when ordered to do so by the Com-
mission, upon the ground that the testimony or evi-
dence , book , document , paper , record or account re-
quired of him may tend to incriminate him or sub-
ject him to a penalty or forfeiture . No person shall
be excused from testifying or from producing any

CHAPTER 4 1013
book, document , paper , record or account when sub-
poenaed as a witness or when ordered to produce
same in any court of competent jurisdiction , upon
the ground that the testimony or evidence , book ,
document, paper, record or account may tend to in-
criminate or subject him to a penalty or forfeiture .
No person shall be prosecuted , punished or subjected
to any forfeiture or penalty for or on account of any
act , transaction , matter or thing concerning which
he shall have been compelled under oath to testify or
compelled to produce documentary evidence ; pro-
vided , however , that no person so testifying shall be
exempt from prosecution or punishment for any per-
jury committed by him in his testimony . The words
"prosecuted , " " punished ," and " penalty" and each
of them, as used in this section shall be construed to
apply only to criminal prosecution , punishment or
penalty , and shall in no event be construed to apply
to any civil liability for additional taxes or liability
resulting from the application of any civil penalty
for delinquency or failure to file reports or pay
taxes as and when required by law. In addition ,
said failure or refusal to obey the summons or order
of the Commission to appear and testify or to pro-
duce records shall be deemed a misdemeanor and
punishable by fine of not less than twenty -five
dollars ($ 25.00 ) or more than five hundred dollars
($500.00 ) .

§ 4. Assessment by Kentucky Tax Commission .
Any taxpayer shall have thirty days from the date
of notice of the Commission's tentative assessment
in which to pay the tax or to ask a change thereof
by written protest, in conformity with regulations
provided therefor . In the event no protest is filed ,
the tentative assessment shall become final in thirty
days after date of notice of such tentative assess-
ment to the taxpayer . In the event a protest has

1014 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
been filed, the Commission shall give due considera-
tion to and dispose of such protest and the taxes
determined to be owing shall be due and payable
thirty days after date of notice of final assessment
to the taxpayer . Provided , nothing in this section
shall prevent an assessment by the Commission
from becoming final immediately and without tenta-
tive notice upon agreement thereto by the taxpayer .
In the event the taxpayer has requested a hearing
with his protest, the Department shall notify the
taxpayer of the time set for such hearing . A con-
feree may , in the discretion of the Commissioner of
Revenue , be appointed to conduct any hearing , re
ceive evidence and hear argument , and embody same
in a report to the Commission .
§ 5. Appeal from Tax Assessment , Order , Rul-
ing or Finding . Any aggrieved taxpayer may peti-
tion the Department for a review by the Commission
of any tax assessment , order , ruling or finding of
any officer or agent of the Department within fifteen
days from date of notice thereof . The Commission
shall review said petition and shall grant a hearing
or conference if an opportunity to be heard is re-
quested in the taxpayer's petition . If hearing is
granted, the Department shall notify the taxpayer of
the time set therefor , and promptly thereafter shall
notify such taxpayer of the Commission's decision .
Within fifteen days after the mailing of notice of
final Commission decision , if the aggrieved taxpayer
has petitioned for the aforesaid review as set out in
the preceding paragraph , he may appeal from any
such decision , tax assessment , order , ruling or find-
ing on any question of law , including adequacy of
evidence , to the Franklin Circuit Court . Two copies
of the taxpayer's petition shall be filed with the
Department on the day it is filed withthe Clerk of
the Franklin Circuit Court . The aggrieved taxpayer

CHAPTER 4 1015
shall file as a part of his petition to the Franklin
Circuit Court the record before the Department , in-
cluding all the pleadings , orders and such parts of
the evidence as the taxpayer believes necessary to
develop the points involved . The Commonwealth
may certify other portions of the evidence . To facili-
tate adjudication of the taxpayer's claims , the De-
partment is hereby required , on payment of a rea-
sonable fee therefor , to be fixed by the Department ,
to copy or cause to be copied such parts of the record
as the taxpayer may require , and supply a certified
copy of the same to the said taxpayer . The case
shall then be heard on said record by said court, but
either party shall have the right to introduce evi-
dence of fraud .
"Section 5A. Code Provisions Adopted . Except
as otherwise expressly provided in this Act , the pro-
visions of the Kentucky Civil Code of Practice shall
govern procedure hereunder involving appeals from
any assessment , order , ruling , or finding of the Ken-
tucky Tax Commission . "
§ 6. Refund . Subject to the limitations , terms
and conditions hereinafter in this section provided ,
when money has been paid into the State Treasury
in payment of any State taxes , except ad valorem
taxes , whether payment was made voluntarily or
involuntarily , the Department hereby is authorized
and directed to refund or cause to be refunded to
the person who paid same , or to his heirs , personal
representatives or assigns , ( a ) any overpayment ;
(b) payment where no tax was due ; and , ( c) when
a bona fide controversy exists between the Depart-
ment and the taxpayer as to the liability of the tax-
payer for the payment of tax claimed to be due by
the Department , the taxpayer may pay the amount
claimed by the Department to be due , and if an ap-
peal be taken by the taxpayer from the ruling of the

1016 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Department within the time provided by section 4 of
this Act and it is finally adjudged that the taxpayer
was not liable for the payment of the tax or any part
thereof , the Department shall make such refund as
the court may direct.
Refunds shall be made in the same manner as
other claims on the State Treasury are paid .
In the event refunds result from error on the part
of the Department or its agents , but not otherwise ,
refunds shall be paid with interest at the rate of six
per cent (6% ) per annum . The refunds authorized
by this section shall not be charged against any ap-
propriation but shall be deducted from tax receipts
for the current fiscal year.
Nothing in this section shall be construed to au-
thorize the Department to make or cause to be made
any refund except within two years from the date
the money was paid into the State Treasury . Noth-
ing in this section shall be construed as requiring
the Department to make or cause to be made any re-
fund to a taxpayer without demand from the tax-
payer , if in the opinion of the Department the cost
to the commonwealth of making such refund is
greater than the amount which should be refunded .
The term " overpayment " and expression " pay-
ment where no tax was due " as used in this section
shall be construed as referring to tax liability under
the term of the Statutes without reference to the con-
stitutionality of the Statute or Statutes . It is not
intended that either of said terms or expressions
shall relate to or apply to any case or circumstance
wherein the Statute or Statutes may be held to be
unconstitutional , either in whole or in part . In cases
in which a Statute has been held unconstitutional ,
taxes paid thereunder may be refunded to the extent
provided by section 162 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-

CHAPTER 4 1017
utes , Baldwin's 1936 Edition , and by the Statute
held unconstitutional .
$ 7. Payment before assessment . If any tax be
paid before the taxpayer's liability has been ascer-
tained or before the taxpayer is notified thereof,
the acceptance and deposit into the State Treasury
of such remittance by the Department shall not be
deemed to imply that the payment was the correct
amount due , nor preclude assessment and collection
of additional taxes found to be due or refund of any
part of the amount paid which may be in excess of
that determined to be due .
§ 8. Assessment of public service companies .
Section 4077 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936
Edition , is hereby repealed , amended and re -enacted
to read as follows :
4077. Assessment of Public Service Companies .
Every railway company , gas company , water com-
pany , ferry company , bridge company , street rail-
way company , express company , electric light com-
pany , electric power company , telephone company ,
telegraph company , press dispatch company , bus line
company , common carrier trucking company , air
transport company , palace car company , dining car
company , sleeping car company , chair car company ,
tank car company , coal car company , pipe line com-
pany , and every other like company or business per-
forming any public service , shall , in addition to the
other taxes imposed on it by law , annually pay a tax
on its franchise to the State , and a local tax thereon
to the county , incorporated city, town and taxing
district , wherein its franchise may be exercised .
The property of such taxpayers shall be classified
as operating tangible , franchise , non -operating tan-
gible and non -operating intangible .
Non -operating intangibles within the taxing juris-
diction of the Commonwealth shall be taxable for

1018 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
State purposes only at the same rate as the intan-
gible property of other taxpayers not performing
public services , and tangible property whether oper-
ating or non -operating shall be subject to State and
local taxes at the same rate as the tangible property
of other taxpayers not performing public services .
The Department of Revenue shall have sole power
to value and assess all of the property , including the
franchise , of every corporation , company , associa-
tion , partnership , or person performing any public
service , including those enumerated above and all
others to whom this section may apply , whether or
not such operating or non -operating tangible or non-
operating intangible , or franchise values have here-
tofore been assessed by said Department , and shall
apportion such assessments of franchise as pro-
vided by section 4081 of the Statutes and shall cer-
tify tangible property to the counties , cities , towns
and taxing districts where said property is situated .
Provided , however , that in the case of a taxpayer
whose business is predominantly non -franchise and
the franchise or public service business in which he
is engaged is merely incidental to his principal busi-
ness , the Department shall in the exercise of its
judgment and discretion , determine from evidence
which it may have or obtain , what portion of the
capital and property is devoted to the public service
business subject to assessment by the Department
under this Act , and shall require the remainder of
the property not so engaged to be assessed by the
local taxing authorities .
$ 9. Jeopardy assessments . Whenever the De-
partment shall have reasonable grounds to believe
that any person domiciled or doing business in this
Commonwealth who may have incurred any tax lia-
bility to the Commonwealth or to any taxing dis-
tricts therein has withdrawn from this Common-

CHAPTER 4 1019
wealth or concealed its assets or a material part
thereof so as to hinder or evade the computation ,
assesment or collection of taxes , or has desisted from
any taxable activity in the Commonwealth , or has
become domiciled elsewhere , or has departed from
this Commonwealth with fraudulent intent to hinder
or evade the computation , assessment or collection
of taxes , or has done any other act tending to render
partly or wholly ineffective proceedings to compute ,
assess or collect any such taxes , or contemplates do-
ing any of the aforementioned act or acts in the
immediate future , or that any tax claim for any
other reason is being or becoming endangered , such
tax liability shall become due and payable immedi-
ately upon assessment or determination of the
amount of taxes due as hereinafter authorized .
Under any of the foregoing circumstances set out
in this section , the Department shall have the au-
thority to make a tentative assessment or determina-
tion of the taxes due , and may proceed immediately
in any manner permitted by law to bring garnish-
ment , attachment or any other legal proceedings to
collect the taxes so assessed or determined to be
due , except that no bond shall be required . Such
person may stay legal proceedings by filing a bond
in an amount sufficient in the opinion of the Depart-
ment to cover the taxes , penalties , interest and court
and other legal costs so charged . If no such pro-
ceedings have been instituted , the Department may
require such person to make bond adequate to cover
all taxes , penalties and interest so charged . On
making bond as hereinabove provided , such person
may file exception to the assessment or determina-
tion of tax liability in the same manner and time as
provided in section 4 of this Act . If no exceptions
are so filed to the tentative assessment or taxes de-
termined as due , the same shall become final .

If such person appeals as provided in section 5 of
this Act from the final assessment or determination
of taxes due , and executes bond to the Franklin Cir-
cuit Court sufficient to protect the Commonwealth
and any county , city , and other taxing district which
may be involved , the bond required by said court
shall supersede any bond previously filed with the
Department.
The Department shall have authority to require
forthwith any such person to file with it such reports
as are required by law or regulation , or such addi-
tional reports or other information as is necessary
fairly to assess the property and/or determine the
amount of tax due.
If the Department fails to exercise the authority
conferred by this section , such person shall and may
be required to report and pay all taxes due as other
wise provided by law.
10. Collection on bond . In the event any tax-
payer who is required to make bond for the pay-
ment of taxes shall fail to pay such taxes when due ,
the Department shall notify such taxpayer and his
surety or sureties by mailing notice to the last known
post office addresses of such parties , and if, after ex-
piration of raesonable time from the date of such
notice , the amount due remains unpaid, the Commis-
sioner shall proceed by suit to collect the amount
due thereunder , including the penalties , interest and
costs . It shall not be necessary to make the default-
ing taxpayer a party to any suit that may be brought
against his surety or sureties .
§ 11. Definition and nature of tax . Every tax
imposed by law and all increases , penalties and in-
terest thereon shall constitute and be a personal
debt of the person or persons liable for the payment
thereof from the time the tax becomes due until
paid .

CHAPTER 4 1021
The term "tax " as used herein shall be construed
to include all penalties and interest imposed by law
for the failure to list , report or pay same as and
when directed or required by law . In addition to all
of the remedies now provided by law for the col-
lection of taxes , penalties and interest, the collection
thereof may be enforced in the same manner as the
collection of any other debts due the Commonwealth .
The penalty prescribed by section 4263 , Kentucky
Statutes , Baldwin's Kentucky Statutes Service , Sup-
plementing Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1937 Edi-
tion, being section 4 , chapter 21 of the Acts of the
Fourth Extraordinary Session of the General As-
sembly of Kentucky of 1936-1937 , when applicable ,
shall be applied to the amount of the original tax ,
interest and penalty or penalties prescribed or im-
posed by the preceding or other Statutes , or either
of them , due the Commonwealth and/or any county ,
taxing district and/or school district , and shall be
in addition thereto .
§ 12. Penalty for failure to make reports or pay
tax. Any person who fails to make any tax report
or return or pay any tax required by law within the
time specified for making such report or return , or
paying such tax , for which a specific civil penalty
is not provided by law , shall pay a penalty of ten per
cent ( 10% ) of the tax found to be due , with interest
from the date due at one per cent ( 1 % ) a month or
part thereof , or ten dollars ( $ 10.00 ) if the penalty
and interest so computed be less than this amount .
The penalty provided in this section shall be col-
lected in the same manner provided for assessment
and collection of taxes under the particular act in-
volved , or collected in a civil action at the instance
of the Department , and in addition , any action may
be brought to compel such person to make such
re-port or return
.

14. Refusal to Accept Personal Check . The
Department may refuse to accept a personal check
as remittance in payment of taxes due or collected
by any person who has ever tendered the Common-
wealth a check which , when presented for payment ,
was not honored . Any check so refused shall be con-
sidered as never having been presented .
§ 15. Penalty for Divulging Information . It
shall be unlawful for any present or former com-
missioner or employee of the Department , member
of county board of supervisors , county tax commis-
sioner or employee thereof or any other person ,
whether enumerated herein or not , to divulge any in-
formation acquired by him of the affairs of any
person or information regarding the tax schedules ,
returns or reports required to be filed with the De-
partment or other proper officer or any information
produced by a hearing or investigation insofar as
the same may have to do with the affairs of the in-
dividual business ; provided , however , that this pro-
hibition shall not extend to information required in
prosecutions for making false reports or returns of
property for taxation or any other infraction of the
tax laws of the State ; provided , further , that this
prohibition shall not extend to any matter properly
entered upon any assessment book , roll or record , or
otherwise in any way made a matter of public rec-
ord ; nor shall it preclude furnishing any taxpayer
or his properly authorized agent information re-
specting his own return ; and provided further that
the Commissioner shall make available any data or
information for official use only to the proper officer ,
board or commission of this State , any Kentucky
city, any other state or province , or the Federal Gov-
ernment , and make available any data or informa-

tion in its possession or coming to its knowledge to
any such officer , board or commission on a confiden-
tial basis , and under reciprocal arrangements
whereby the Department shall receive similar or
useful information from such officer or body . Any
violation of the provisions of this section shall be
a misdemeanor and shall be punished by a fine not
exceeding five hundred dollars ($500.00 ) or by im-
prisonment not exceeding six months , or both , and
the guilty person shall be disqualified and removed
from office or employment .
§ 16. State Tax Commission, How Construed .
The words " State Tax Commission " or " Kentucky
State Tax Commission " , where used in the statutes ,
shall be read and construed as the " Department of
Revenue ."
$ 17. Interstate Comity . The courts of this
Commonwealth shall recognize and enforce statutes
concerning taxation constitutionally imposed by
other states which extend like comity .

CHAPTER 5 1025
CHAPTER 5 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re -enact section 77 and section
106 of House Bill 129 of the regular session of the General
Assembly of 1938. This Act shall be known as the Robinson-
Embry Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Repeal , amend and re -enact section 77 of
House Bill 129 , of the regular session of the General
Assembly of 1938 , so that when amended and re-
enacted said section shall read as follows :
Section 77. Closing Hours and Times . No prem-
ises for which there has been granted a license for
the sale of distilled spirits or wine at retail shall be
permitted to remain open for any purpose between
midnight and eight o'clock a . m., or at any time
during the twenty-four hours of a Sunday or of
an election day , provided , that if a licensee provides
a separate department within his licensed premises
capable of being locked and closed off, within which
is kept all stocks of distilled spirits and wine , and all
fixtures and apparatus connected with his business
as a licensee , and said department is kept locked
during the times mentioned above , he shall be
deemed to have complied with this section .
§ 2.2. Repeal , amend and re -enact section 106 of
House Bill 129 of the regular session of the General

CHAPTER 6 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend , and re -enact Section 4020 , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , in order to clarify the definition of certain
property subject to taxation in this Commonwealth .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 4020 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
Baldwin's 1936 Revision , is hereby repealed , amend-
ed, and re - enacted so that as re-enacted the same
shall read as follows :
Section 4020. Property subject ; tangible and in-
tangible property ; franchises . All real and per-
sonal estate within this state , and all personal estate
of persons residing in this state , and of all corpora-
tions organized under the laws of this state , whether
the same be in or out of the state , including intan-
gible property , which shall be considered and esti-
mated in fixing the value of corporate franchises as
herein provided , and the intangible property of non-
residents , including corporations not organized un-
der the laws of Kentucky if said property has ac-
quired a business situs in Kentucky , shall be subject
to taxation unless the same be exempt from taxation
by the Constitution , and shall be assessed at its fair
cash value , estimated at the price it would bring
at a fair voluntary sale ; Provided , however , that
tangible personal property located and having a tax-
able situs without the state , of persons residing in
this state , and of all corporations organized under
the laws of this state , shall not be subject to taxa-

CHAPTER 7 1027
tion : And , provided further , that the situs of in-
tangible personal property for purposes of taxa-
tion shall be at the residence of the real or beneficial
owner , and not at the residence of the fiduciary or
agent having the custody or possession of same . Pro-
vided further , that all intangible personal property
including money in hand , notes , bonds , accounts and
other credits whether secured by mortgage , pledge
or otherwise , or unsecured , and shares of stock to-
gether with all other intangible personal property
irrespective of whether or not the same has hereto-
fore been subject to taxation in the Commonwealth
of Kentucky shall have a situs and be subject to
taxation in this Commonwealth insofar as the Gen-
eral Assembly of Kentucky can subject the same to
taxation under the terms of the Constitutions of the
United States and of Kentucky . Provided further ,
that nothing herein contained shall in any way af-
fect the liability for franchise taxes now payable by
corporations organized under the laws of this state ;
nor the method of taxation of banks and trust com-
panies as provided in Section 4092 of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision ; nor the
method of taxation of building and loan associations
as provided in Sections 4019a -7 and 4019a - 8 of Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision ;
and provided further that nothing herein contained
shall alter or repeal Section 4088 of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 7.
AN ACT providing for the making of direct reduction loans by
building and loan associations , incorporated under the laws
of Kentucky , defining the rights and obligations of the bor

1028 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
rower, and providing for the method of re-payment of the
loans .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Building and Loan Associations , incorpo-
rated under the laws of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky may also make loans on the Direct Reduction
Plan . All said loans shall bear interest at such rate
and be payable in equal monthly installments , and
for such length of time as the Board of Directors
may determine . The applicant for such loans shall
subscribe for shares or fractional shares of stock ,
equal , when paid up, to the amounts of such loans .
Payment of dues and interest shall be credited upon
such loan and shares in accordance with the direct
reduction plan adopted . In consideration of making
such loans upon such plan no dividend shall be de-
clared or paid or credited upon amounts credited as
dues or principal upon such loans , but all payments ,
made on said loans shall be first applied to interest
due to date of respective payments and the balance
applied as dues on principal , and interest shall be
collected only on such balance . When the amount
paid in as dues and credited as payment on such
shares as calculated shall equal the value of a share
or shares , such shares shall be considered paid in full
and automatically canceled , but such cancellation
shall in no manner affect or reduce the stipulated
weekly or monthly installment payments provided
to be paid in the note or mortgage given to evidence
and secure the payment of said loan .
When any such intallment becomes due and re-
mains unpaid for six weeks after the same becomes
due and payable , then all the balance of such install-
ments , both due and to become due , shall immedi-
ately become due and payable at the option of the
holder of such note , and said borrower shall be noti-

CHAPTER 8 1029
fied of said delinquency , and payment of same de-
manded , in writing , by mail with postage prepaid to
the address of said borrower as same appears on
the books of the association and if said delinquent
payments of principal and interest are not paid
within thirty ( 30 ) days from the mailing of said no-
tice , then all moneys paid in as dues or principal and
such shares , may be forfeited by said association
and applied first to the payment of interest due and
the balance on principal and suit brought to enforce
payment of said note and said mortgage , according
to law. Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 8.
AN ACT authorizing the Department of Revenue to require the
use of crowns in collection of beer taxes ; authorizing rules
and regulations relating thereto ; making provisions for avoid .
ing double taxation ; defining terms ; providing for punishment
of persons who violate the act or regulations pursuant thereto ;
and for other purposes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Notwithstanding the provisions of sections
4281c - 1 to 4281c -25 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition, and particularly of section 4281c - 10 ,
the Department of Revenue is hereby authorized at
its discretion to prescribe that beer taxes be col-
lected by means of tax crowns of such distinctive de-
sign as it may prescribe , or by means of both such
crowns and stamps used as prescribed in the said
Alcoholic Beverage Tax Act , or as may be directed
by regulation . ( This shall be in lieu of the methods
prescribed in said Act . ) In the event the said De-
partment of Revenue shall authorize and require
the use of tax crowns it shall be unlawful for any
beer manufacturer ( except to the extent beer is pro-

1030 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
duced for export from Kentucky ) , distributor , or
retailer to have in his possession any beer not having
affixed thereto the appropriate crown or stamp as
may be required ; provided , however , that the De-
partment of Revenue is explicitly required to pro-
vide by regulation means whereby no person author-
ized by law to import beer into Kentucky and re-
package the same shall be under the necessity of
paying tax on both the original package as imported
into the state and also the retail container in which
said beer is packaged and distributed .
It is also explicitly required if the Department
shall order the use of tax crowns that the said De-
partment devise and enforce by regulation means
whereby the manufacturer , or manufacturers , of said
crowns shall make bond in favor of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky in an amount approved by the
Department of Revenue conditioned that the said
manufacturer , or manufacturers , will not sell or de-
liver said crowns to any person except on written
approval of the Department of Revenue , and that in
all other respects the said manufacturer , or manu-
facturers , will faithfully safeguard the handling and
distribution of said crowns so that the tax revenues
of the Commonwealth may be fully protected .
§ 2. Any violation of this Act , or of any valid
regulation issued pursuant thereto , shall be pun-
ished as provided by the Alcoholic Beverage Tax
Act ( section 4281c -22 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 edition ) .
3. The term " crown " as used in this act shall
be construed to mean such crowns as are used for
stopping bottles , lids for the closing of the cans , and
stoppers of whatever material or design for sealing
containers of beer , but shall not include adhesive
stamps such as the Department of Revenue is , by
this act , authorized to prescribe and require .

CHAPTER 9 1031
The definitions contained in the first section ( sec-
tion 4281c - 1 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edi-
tion ) of the Alcoholic Beverage Tax Act are hereby
adopted for purposes of this act so far as applicable .
This act shall be construed in appropriate relation
to the Alcoholic Beverage Act of 1936 , heretofore
cited , and to the Alcohol Control Act .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .

AN ACT relating to Workmen's Compensation and providing
for a revolving fund and limiting the assessment of employers

this Act ; repealing, amending and reenacting Section 4968-9
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 edition , and repealing sub-
section 2 of section 24 of Chapter One of the Acts of the

tion 22 of part one of House Bill One , appropriating out of the

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 4968-9 Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 edition , be and the same is hereby re-
pealed , amended and reenacted so when amended

The Board shall not be authorized to incur expense

zation Act of 1936 , being Chapter One of the Acts

CHAPTER 11 .
AN ACT amending Article 3 Chapter 85 Kentucky Statutes by
adding another section defining Optometry and regulating the
issual and revocation of licenses to practice Optometry and
to prohibit false , misleading or bait advertising with reference
to ocular examinations for eyeglasses or mountings and to
authorize the State Board of Health of Kentucky to make
rules and regulations governing the practice of Optometry and
authorizing the Court to issue temporary and permanent
injunctions for violation of any of the Kentucky Statutes in
reference to Optometry and to provide penalties for a viola-
tion of any of the provisions of this Act and to amend Sec-
tion 2618a -6 Kentucky Statutes by striking the words "sixty
(60 ) days " and substituting therefor 'thirty ( 30 ) days " so that
said section when amended will read as hereinafter set out .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the practice of optometry as used in
this Act is defined as follows : The examination of
the human eye without the use of drugs , medicines

1036 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
·
or surgery , to ascertain the presence or defect of
abnormal conditions which can be corrected by the
use of lenses , prims , or ocular exercises and their
adaptation for the aid thereof .
§ 2. It shall be unlawful for any person to :
(a ) practice optometry without first procuring
or having a license so to do , in this State .
(b) falsely impersonate an Optometrist of like
or different name .
(c ) practice , offer or hold himself out as au-
thorized to practice optometry or use in connection
with his name any designation tending to imply that
he is a practitioner of optometry if not licensed
to practice under the provisions of this Act , or in
accordance therewith .
(d ) advertise by printed matter , radio , display
or any other means the quotation of prices for a dis-
count on or specific terms of payment for eyeglasses ,
spectacles or accessories thereto , ophthalmic lenses ,
frames or mountings or the phrases , " free examina-
tions " , " moderate prices " , " low prices " , " clinic
prices " , "guaranteed glasses ", "satisfaction guar-
anteed " or any variation thereof or words of similar
import .
(e) publish , directly or indirectly , any fraudu-
lent, false or misleading statement as to the skill or
method of practice of any person engaged or about
to engage , in the practice of optometry or with refer-
ence to any article , or the sale thereof , used in con-
nection therewith .
§ 3. The State Board of Health may refuse to
issue the certificate provided in this Act for any of
the causes listed in Section 2615 Kentucky Statutes
and may suspend or revoke certificates for any of
the causes for which it may refuse to grant licenses
under the provisions of this Act and in addition , it
may revoke a certificate under the provisions of Sec-

CHAPTER 11 1037
tion 2615 Kentucky Statutes of any holder guilty of
gross immorality , false statement or fraud or if
any such holder has employed , hired , procured or
induced any person not holding such certificate to
practice any branch of the healing art in this State
including optometry or that said licensee aids or
abets in the practice of any branch of the healing
art including optometry any person not holding a
certificate entitling him to practice , or who adver-
tises superiority of performance of professional
service in the practice , or who attempts to secure
patronage by advertising prices for eyeglasses , spec-
tacles , ophthalmic lenses , frames , mountings or op-
tometric devices , or who seeks patronage by adver-
tising free optometric services or free examinations ,
or any words or phrases implying such services or
who advertises or claims to guarantee any optomet-
ric service of any eyeglasses , lenses , frames or
mountings , or who seeks to attract patronage by
means of solicitors , canvassers or agents for the
purpose of obtaining patronage , or shall publish di-
rectly or indirectly any false, fraudulent or mislead-
ing statement as to skill or method of practice of
any person engaged in the practice of any branch
of the healing art including optometry or with refer-
ence to the sale thereof or use in connection there-
with .
Section 2618a - 6 Kentucky Statutes is amended by
striking the words " sixty ( 60 ) days " and substitut-
ing the words " thirty ( 30 ) days " therefor and said
section when so amended shall read as follows :
The State Board of Health is authorized to revoke ,
upon thirty ( 30 ) days notice , and after a fair and
impartial hearing , the certificate of any Optometrist
found guilty of practicing as an itinerant or peddling
from door to door , or the establishment of temporary
offices or the practice of Optometry outside or away

1038 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
from his regular office or place of business . Holders
of Optometrist's certificate may , however, establish
a branch office or offices provided each such branch
office be fully equipped with the instruments neces-
sary to make an optometric examination and is in
charge of an Optometrist holding a certificate au-
thorizing him to practice .
4. The State Board of Health shall have power
to make such rules and regulations not inconsistent
with law or with this act as may be necessary for
the regulation of the practice of optometry and shall
have the right to institute in its own name an action
or actions to enjoin the violations of any of the
provisions of this Act or regulations or rules pro-
mulgated in accordance therewith and in such con-
nection a temporary injunction may be obtained or
granted as well as a permanent injunction against
any offender thereof
§ 5. Upon application the Board may in its dis-
cretion reissue a license to practice optometry that
has been revoked but such application shall not be
made or license reissued prior to one year after
revocation and shall be made in such manner and
form as the Board may prescribe .
§ 6. That any person living in this State , or who
may hereafter come into this State who corrects or
attempts to correct defects of the eye by mechanical
or any other means , except upon the prescription of
a legally qualified physician , for reward or compen-
sation without complying with the provisions of this
Act or who opens an office for such purpose or an-
nounces to the public a readiness to so practice , shall
upon conviction , be fined fifty dollars ($50.00 ) and
upon each and every subsequent conviction shall
be fined one hundred dollars ($ 100.00 ) and impris-
oned thirty ( 30 ) days or either or both in the dis-
cretion of the court or jury trying the case and each

CHAPTER 12 1039
day any person violates any of the provisions there-
of shall constitute a separate offense and in no case
where any provision of this law has been violated.
shall the person so violating be entitled to receive
any compensation for the service rendered . To open
an office for such purpose or to announce to the
public a readiness to practice optometry shall be
deemed to engage in the practice of optometry with-
in the meaning of this Act ; provided this Act shall
not apply to persons who sell spectacles , eyeglasses ,
or lenses only as merchandise in a duly established
mercantile establishment .
§ 7. If any provision of this Act or part thereof
shall be held invalid , the remainder of the Act and
the application of the provisions thereof shall not
be affected nor impaired thereby .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 12 .
AN ACT to provide approved high school service for all pupils .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That it shall be the duty of the board of education
of each county to provide approved high school serv-
ice within the county for all children of high school
grade residing in the county district , either by main
taining the school or by contract with an independ-
ent district within the county , and the duty of each
independent district board of education to provide
such service within its district , or by contract with
a city or other independent district within the coun-
ty, for all children of high school grade residing
within such district ; provided that if the number of
such pupils of either race is not sufficient in any
county or independent district to maintain a high

1040 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
school , the board shall provide it by transporting
them daily to an approved high school in another
county or district , or in lieu of transportation shall
provide for their board and room within reasonable.
walking distance of such school if it is found more
feasible or more economical to do so , said mainte-
nance in no case to cost more than one hundred dol-
lars ($ 100.00 ) per year for any pupil .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 13.
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re -enact Subsections ( a) and (b )
of Section 3 , and Subsections (b) and (f) of Section 8, and to
repeal Subsection (a ) of Section 8 of Chapter 145 of the 1934
Acts of the General Assembly relating to public utilities and
the Public Service Commission of Kentucky , being Sections
3952-8 , 3952-9 , 3952-53 , 3952-57 and 3952-52 , respectively , of
Baldwin's 1936 Revision of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ; pro-
viding for the appointment of the chairman of the Public
Service Commission of Kentucky , repealing all inconsistent
laws .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Subsection ( a ) of Section 3 of Chapter
145 of the Acts of the 1934 Session of the General
Assembly , being Section 3952-8 of Baldwin's 1936
Revision of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , be and the
same is hereby repealed and re-enacted so that the
same as re -enacted shall read as follows :
The Commission shall appoint a secretary who
shall hold office during its pleasure , who shall devote
his entire time to the duties of his office and who
shall receive an annual salary of four thousand five
hundred ( $4,500.00 ) dollars , payable in the same
manner as that of the members of the Commission .
It shall be the duty of the Secretary to keep a full

CHAPTER 13 1041
and true record of all the proceedings of the Com-
mission , of all books , maps , documents , and papers
ordered filed by the Commission , and of all orders
made by the Commission or approved and con-
firmed by it, and ordered filed , and he shall be re-
sponsible to it for the safe custody and preservation
of all such documents at it office . Under the direc-
tion of the Commission , the secretary shall have gen-
eral charge of its office , superintend its clerical busi-
ness , and perform such other duties as the Commis-
sion may prescribe . He shall have power and au-
thority to administer oaths in all parts of the state ,
so far as the exercise of such authority is properly
incidental to the performance of his duties or that
of the Commission . The secretary shall designate
from time to time one of the clerks appointed by the
