2. That Subsection (b ) of Section 3 of Chapter
145 of the Acts of the 1934 Session of the General
Assembly, being Section 3952-9 of Baldwin's 1936
Revision of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , be and the
same is hereby repealed and re -enacted so that the
same as re -enacted shall read as follows :
The Attorney General of this Commonwealth
shall , subject to the approval of the Commission , ap-
point an additional assistant attorney general whose
sole duty shall be to act as legal counsel for the
Commission . Said Assistant Attorney General shall
be of at least thirty years of age , and shall have been
a regular practicing attorney for more than eight
years , and shall be paid his salary of four thousand
five hundred ( $ 4,500.00 ) dollars per annum , payable
in the same manner as that of the members of the
Commission. It shall be the duty of the Attorney

1042 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
General , together with said Asistant Attorney Gen-
eral , to represent and appear for the Commission in
all actions and proceedings involving any question.
arising under any law relating to the Commission
or under any order or act of the Commission .
§ 3. That Subsection ( b ) , Section 8 , Chapter 145
of the 1934 Acts of the General Assembly , being Sec-
tion 3952-53 of Baldwin's 1936 Revision of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , be and the same is hereby re-
pealed and re -enacted and the same as re - enacted
shall read as follows :
For the purpose of maintaining the Commission ,
including the payment of salaries , traveling ex-
penses , including hotel bills , printing , rent , light ,
heat , water , telephone and all other expenses , and
the expenses of regulation by the Commission of the
utilities subject to its jurisdiction , the Commission
shall on or before July 1 , 1938 , and on or before
July 1 of each year thereafter , apportion among and
assess the cost thereof upon said utilities in propor-
tion to the gross earning or receipts of said utilities.
derived from intrastate business in Kentucky for
the next preceding calendar year in which the as-
sessments are made ; provided , however , that the
total amount so assessed shall not in any year exceed
$130,000 . All such fees or assessments for the main-
tenance of the Commission shall be covered through
the Department of Finance into the Treasury of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky on or before the 1st
day of July , 1938 , and on or before the 1st day of
July of each year thereafter ; provided , however, the
Commission shall be free to use and expend any
money heretofore collected by it for its maintenance .
§ 4. Notwithstanding any provision of any exist-
ing law the Governor of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky shall appoint a member of the Commission to
act as its Chairman .

CHAPTER 14 1043
§ 5. Subsection ( f ) of Section 8 , Chapter 145 of
the 1934 Acts of the General Assembly , being Section
3952-57 of Baldwin's 1936 Edition of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , be and the same is hereby repealed
and re - enacted so that the same shall read as fol-
lows :
Should any amount of the sums paid by such utili-
ties for the maintenance of the Commission remain
unexpended in the treasury at the end of any fiscal
year, such amount so unexpended shall remain cred-
ited to the account of the Commission and may be
used to defray the expenses of the regulation by and
maintenance of the Commission during any suc-
ceeding year or years .
§ 6. Subsection ( a ) of Section 8 , Chapter 145
of the 1934 Acts of the General Assembly , being Sec-
tion 3952-52 of Baldwin's 1936 Revision of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , be and the same is hereby re-
pealed . All other statutes or laws , or parts thereof,
in conflict with any provision or provisions of this
Act are hereby repealed .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 14 .
AN ACT appropriating money for the 1938 Special Sessions of
the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and
declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated out of the
general expenditure fund for the payment of sal-
aries and other necessary expenses of the 1938 Spe-
cial Sessions of the General Assembly , the sum of
Sixty Thousand Dollars ( $ 60,000.00 ) .
§ 2. Whereas, the funds heretofore appropriated

1044 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
for the 1938 General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky are practically exhausted , and
Whereas , the work of the 1938 General Assembly
is not completed , an emergency is declared to exist
and this Act shall become effective upon its passage

CHAPTER 15 .
AN ACT providing for the purchase of property in Paintsville
Kentucky , known as " The Mayo College property " , and for the
establishment thereon of a State Vocational School ; providing
for the maintenance and operation of said school , and making
appropriations therefor.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The Kentucky Real Estate Board is hereby
authorized and empowered , in accordance with the
Governmental Re -organization Act , to purchase cer-
tain properties in Paintsville , Kentucky , known as
the "Mayo College property ", for the purpose of
establishing thereon a State Vocational School ,
known as the Mayo State Vocational School .
§ 2. There is hereby recognized as established
and maintained the Mayo State Vocational School
for white persons , to be located at Paintsville , Ken-
tucky, and to begin operations on and after the 1st
day of July , 1938. The managament and control
of the said Mayo State Vocational School shall be
vested in the State Board of Education , which is
hereby vested with all the duties , rights , powers , pur-
poses and responsibilities of the institution , and
shall be in possession and control , in accordance with
the Governmental Re -organization Act of 1936 , of
all properties , moneys , funds , appropriations , lands

CHAPTER 15 1045
and other properties , real or personal , and all rec-
ords , equipment and supplies that now, or may be
hereafter provided for the benefit of said institu-
tion .
3. The said Mayo State Vocational School is
hereby authorized to conduct , maintain and operate ,
under the control and supervision of the State Board
of Education , a State Vocational School , the pur-
pose of which shall be to make available facilities
for vocational training for white persons , to be car-
ried on in accordance with State plans for vocational
education . The curriculum shall be established and
approved by the State Board of Education .
4. For the purpose of operating and maintain-
ing the boarding and rooming departments , student
activities , and other necessary operation and main-
tenance expenses of the Mayo State Vocational
School , there are hereby appropriated for the fiscal
year of 1938-1939 , and the fiscal year 1939-1940 all
fees and other operating receipts which shall be
covered into the State Treasury promptly after the
close of each month , and placed to the credit of the
revolving fund of the said institution . Withdrawals
for the use and benefit of the institution may be
made from the said revolving fund maintained in
the State Treasury when and as needed upon requi-
sition of the governing authority of the institution ,
properly approved by the Commissioner of Finance .
§ 5. There is hereby appropriated out of the
General Expenditure Fund the sum of not exceeding
twenty -five thousand dollars ( $25,000 . ) for the pur-
pose of purchasing the property aforesaid for the
location of the said Mayo State Vocational School .
For the payment of salaries and other expenses
incident to the maintenance and operation of the
said Mayo State Vocational School , excepting the
purchase of real estate authorized by this Act , there

1046, ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
is hereby appropriated out of the General Expendi-
ture Fund the sum of twenty-five thousand ( $25 ,-
000.00 ) dollars for the fiscal year 1938-1939 .
For the payment of salaries and other expenses
incident to the maintenance and operation of the
said Mayo State Vocational School , there is hereby
appropriated out of the General Expenditure Fund
the sum of nineteen thousand dollars ( $ 19,000 ) for
the fiscal year 1939-1940 .
Approved by the Governor : April 14, 1938.
CHAPTER 16 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend , and re-enact section twenty -two (22)
of House Bill one hundred twenty nine ( 129 ) of the General
Assembly of 1938 , and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section twenty -two ( 22 ) of House Bill
one hundred twenty nine ( 129 ) of the General As-
sembly of 1938 be and the same is hereby repealed ,
amended and re -enacted so that as amended and re-
enacted said section shall read as follows :
22. Transactions Permitted and Prohibited to
Distillers , Rectifiers , and Vintners . Sales and de-
liveries of alcoholic beverages may be made at
wholesale , and from the licensed premises only , ( 1 )
by distillers to licensed rectifiers , licensed vintners ,
holders of special non -beverage alcohol licenses so
far as they are authorized to make the purchases , or
other licensed distillers ; by rectifiers to licensed
vintners , or to licensed distillers provided distilled
spirits sold to distillers are packaged in retail con-
tainers ; by vintners to licensed rectifiers or other
licensed vintners , or to the holders of special non-
beverage alcohol licenses ; or ( 2 ) by distillers , recti-

CHAPTER 16 1047
fiers or vintners to licensed wholesalers ; or ( 3 ) by
licensed distillers , rectifiers or vintners for export
out of the Commonwealth ; provided , no distiller ,
rectifier or vintner , shall sell or contract to sell , give
away or deliver any alcoholic beverages to any per-
son, who is not duly authorized by the law of the
State of his residence and of the Federal Govern-
ment if located in the United States , to receive and
possess said alcoholic beverages ; and in no event
shall he sell or contract to sell , give away or deliver ,
any of his products to any retailer or consumer in
Kentucky .
Distillers may purchase distilled spirits only from
other licensed distillers in this Commonwealth or in
another state , territory or province ; except that dis-
tillers may purchase from rectifiers licensed in Ken-
tucky, distilled spirits which are packaged in retail
containers .
Rectifiers may purchase distilled spirits or wine
only from distillers or vintners licensed under this
Act ; or from non -residents duly authorized by the
law of the State of their residence and by the Fed-
eral Government , if located in the United States , to
make the sales .
Vintners may purchase distilled spirits or wine
only from distillers or vintners licensed under this
Act or from non -residents duly authorized by law of
the State of their residence and by the Federal Gov-
ernment , if located in the United States to make the
sales .
Provided nothing in this Act shall be construed
to prohibit the purchase or sale of warehouse re-
ceipts by any person or persons but this provision
shall not authorize the owner of any such receipt
to accept delivery of any distilled spirits unless the
owner is qualified under this law to receive the same.
Since this act amends House Bill one hun-2 .

1048 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
dred twenty -nine ( 129 ) , which was passed at the
last regular session of the Legislature and which is
now in effect , an emergency is declared to exist and
this act shall become a law and be effective on its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 17.
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Sections 1772 of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision ; and declaring an
emergency to exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 1772 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
Baldwin's 1936 Revision , be amended so that when
so amended the same shall read as follows :
In counties having a population of seventy -five
thousand or over , the jailer shall , after the term
of the present incumbent has expired , receive an an-
nual salary of five thousand dollars ( $ 5,000 ) ; and
the number of his deputies , guards and turnkeys and
the salaries allowed to each , shall be fixed by the
judge of the county court , the mayor of the city
where jail is located , and the judge of the criminal
division of the circuit court , but the salary of the
chief deputy shall not exceed eighteen hundred dol-
lars ( $ 1,800 ) per annum , nor shall the annual salary
each of the other deputies , guards or turnkeys ,
exceed twelve hundred ( $ 1,200 ) per annum .
order fixing the salaries and numbers of deputies ,
guards and turnkeys shall be signed by a majority
of said persons and spread upon the order book of
the county court and a certified copy thereof , as well
as of any subsequent changes made therein , shall
be filed when made with the auditor of public ac-
The

CHAPTER 18 1049
counts ; provided , however , that in counties having a
population of 200,000 or over , the Jailer may ap-
point , without the necessity of approval of any
court , board, committee or any other officer , such
deputies , guards and turnkeys as he may deem nec-
essary and proper , and shall be liable on his official
bond for the conduct of such deputies , guards and
turnkeys . He shall also fix , without the necessity of
approval of any such court , board, committee or
any other officer , the salaries to be allowed to such
deputies , guards and turnkeys , but the salary of the
chief deputy , who may also be the chief bookkeeper
of the Jail , shall not exceed thirty -six hundred dol-
lars ( $ 3,600 ) per annum , nor shall the annual salary
each of the other deputies , guards or turnkeys ,
exceed eighteen hundred dollars ( $ 1,800 ) per an-
num . The order of the Jailer fixing the salaries and
numbers of deputies , guards and turnkeys , and his
order of appointment to said respective positions ,
shall be signed by him and acknowledged before a
Notary Public and shall be filed by him with the
County Court , and by it spread upon its Order Book ,
and a certified copy thereof, as well as of any subse-
quent changes made therein , shall be filed when
made with the auditor of public accounts .
Whereas the provisions of this Act are necessary
for the proper administration and efficiency of jails
in counties containing a city of the first class , an
emergency is hereby declared to exist and this Act
shall become effective immediately after its passage
and signature of the Governor .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 18.
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re-enact Section 199a -8, Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision , and declaring an
emergency to exist.

1050 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 199a - 8 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
Baldwin's 1936 Revision be and the same is hereby
repealed , amended and re -enacted so that when
amended and re-enacted the same shall read as fol-
lows :
When in the opinion of any burial or cemetery as-
sociation or corporation in this Commonwealth , or-
ganized for the purpose of furnishing to the public ,
a burial or cemetery site or ground , it may condemn
under the right of eminent domain , if it desire , after
a resolution reciting such need and desire for said
purposes shall have been recorded in the County
Clerk's Office of such county , order the condemna-
tion of such land or site and a sufficient passway or
roadway not to exceed 100 feet in width for ingress
and egress to and from such sites . Wherever any
land or passway belonging to any burial or cemetery
association or corporation is needed and required
for State Highway purposes and where by the build-
ing of any State Highway would necessitate a change
in the entrance to any cemetery , such burial or ceme-
tery association or corporation may likewise con-
demn under the right of eminent domain such other
adjacent land as may be necessary for a roadway
and entrance to such land not to exceed 100 feet in
width ; and such burial or cemetery association or
corporation may likewise condemn under the right
of eminent domain such site or land as may neces-
sary for a chapel site adjacent to or connected with
any land now used or which may hereafter be used
for cemetery purposes , such site not to exceed five
acres . "Said burial or cemetery association or cor-
poration shall not be authorized to condemn for the

CHAPTER 19 1051
purposes herein set out more than forty acres at
any one time. ”
Whereas the Department of Highways of the
State of Kentucky is at the present time taking the
entrance and the other real estate belonging to cer-
tain burial and cemetery associations for the pur-
pose of highways , and
Whereas , such burial and cemetery associations
do not have proper entrance to their burial grounds
and cannot under the present law condemn property
for this purpose , an emergency is hereby declared to
exist , and this act shall become effective from and
after its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved by the Governor : April 14 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 19 .
AN ACT to establish the office of physician to the Jail in coun-
ties containing a population of 150,000 , or more ; providing for
his appointment , and removal ; prescribing his duties , and
fixing his compensation ; and repealing Chapter 27 of the Acts
of 1898 of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky , now Sections 2237a -1 to 2237a -3 , inclusive , of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision ; and declaring
an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That in counties having a population of
150,000 or more , the Jailer of any such county may
appoint , without the necessity of approval of any
court , board , committee or other officer , a physician
to the Jail of such county , who shall, upon his ap-
pointment , enter upon the discharge of the duties
of said office , and shall hold said office at the pleasure
of such Jailer . The order of the Jailer appointing
said physician , and any removal from office of said.
physician , shall be signed by him and acknowledged

1052 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
before a Notary Public and shall be filed by him
with the County Court , and by it spread upon its
Order Book .
§ 2. It shall be the duty of said physician to at-
tend upon and prescribe for all persons who may
be confined in said Jail by order of the Courts of the
State.
§ 3. Said physician shall receive as compensa-
tion , for his services the sum of Twenty -four Hun-
dred Dollars ( $2400.00 ) per annum to be paid by the
county. In counties containing cities having a sepa-
rate government the county may require the city
government to pay its pro rata part of such com-
pensation .
§ 4. Chapter 27 of the Acts of 1898 of the Gen-
eral Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
now Sections 2237a -1 to 2237a - 3, inclusive , of Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision
shall be and the same is and are hereby repealed .
Whereas , the administration and efficiency of
jails in counties having a population of 150,000 or
more , and the health and welfare of the inmates of
such jails are dependent upon this Act , an emergency
is hereby declared to exist and this Act shall become
effective immediately after its passage and signa-
ture of the Governor .
Approved by the Governor : April 12 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 20 .
AN ACT providing for matrons for jails and station houses in
cities of the first class ; providing for their appointment , their
duties , their removal , their compensation , and repealing Chap-
ter 8 of the Acts of 1912 of the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , now embraced in Sections 2877a -1 to
2877a-14 , inclusive , of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's
1936 Revision , and declaring an emergency .

CHAPTER 20 1053
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Station Houses for Female Prisoners to be
Designated : That within ninety days after the ad-
journment of the present General Assembly , the
Mayor of each city for the first class shall designate
one or more station houses within the said city for
the detention of all female prisoners who may be
properly detained in a station house while awaiting
trial , and said Mayor may thereafter change the
station house or station houses so designated ; Pro-
vided , however , That at least one station house shall
always remain as a place in which female prisoners
shall be detained , until they shall have been set at
liberty , or removed by order of a competent court .
§ 2. Jail Designated .-Where there shall be a
county jail in said city of the first class , the Mayor
of said city shall also designate it as a place in
which female prisoners may be detained awaiting
trial , or after trial , until removed or set at liberty
by the order of a competent court.
3. Matrons to Look After Female Prisoners
To Be Appointed .—Immediately upon such designa-
tion of such station house or station houses , and
County Jail , if there be a jail in such city , the County
Jailer shall appoint without the necessity of ap-
proval of any Court , board , committee or any other
officer , two respectable women to care for and have
supervision over the female prisoners in such Coun-
ty Jail , subject to the orders of said Jailer . One of
the women so appointed shall be called Police Ma-
tron , and she shall be stationed at the County Jail ,
and the other shall be called Assistant Police Ma-
tron , and she shall also be stationed at the County
Jail . Said Jailer may also so appoint a colored
respectable woman as an Assistant Police Matron

1054 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
for the Jail to take care of and have supervision
over the colored women prisoners subject to the

lice Matrons for each of the station houses other

CHAPTER 20 1055 .
§ 5. Term of Office of Matrons .-Neither the
Police Matron nor Assistant Police Matrons shall
be appointed for any definite term , but they shall
hold their positions until removed , which may be
done at any time for the good of the service , and they
may be removed by the appointing power .
§ 6. Hours of Service and Duties of Matrons.-
The hours of service of the police matron and the
assistant matrons at the county jail shall be so ar-
ranged by the jailer for the county jail and the
Chief of Police for the city , that at least one of them
shall at all hours of the day and night be on duty
at each of the places in which there shall be one or
more female prisoners detained . The Police Matron
shall have , subject to the control of the jailer , the
entire care of women and children under arrest in
the county jail , which may be designated for female
prisoners , and subject to the control of the Chief of
Police of women and children under arrest at station
houses , and she may call upon a police officer or a
jailer , or his deputy for assistance .
§ 7. Rooms to be Provided for Matrons and
Female Prisoners at the County Jail .-In every jail
in which a police matron or an assistant police ma-
tron serves , sufficient and proper accomodation
shall be provided therein for women confined under
arrest . There shall also be provided at said jail
designated for female prisoners a comfortable and
suitable room or rooms which the police matron or
the assistant police matron , as the case may be, may
occupy , and if, in the opinion of such matron the
accomodations are not sufficient , or proper at the
county jail , she shall notify the jailer , and he shall
provide the necessary and proper accomodations ;
the expense necessary therefor to be paid by the
proper county authorities out of the County
Treasury .

1056 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
$ 8. How Provided for by the City.- The As-
sistant Police Matrons stationed at any station house
or houses that may be designated for the care and
keep of women prisoners as herein provided other
than the County Jail , shall be under the control of
the Chief of Police , and he shall provide proper ac-
comodations for them at any such station house or
houses . For the appointment of such Assistant Po-
lice Matrons for station houses other than the Coun-
ty Jail , the Mayor shall be notified by the Chief of
Police of the necessity for making such appointment ,
and if any vacancies should occur at any time , the
Chief of Police shall notify the Mayor that such
vacancy exists .
9. Matron to be Notified When Female or Child
Incarcerated .-Whenever in any city , where a police
matron should be appointed under this act , a woman
or child is taken to the station house or jail in which
a police matron or an assistant police matron is at-
tached , and she shall not be present at the time said
woman or child is received , the keeper of said sta-
tion or jail shall immediately notify her that such
female , or child , is detained at said place .
-§ 10. Matron to Have Charge of Assistant Ma-
trons . The police matron shall , subject to the Jail-
er at the County Jail , have charge of the assistant
police matrons stationed therein , and the Chief of
Police shall have charge of the assistant police ma-
trons stationed at the station house or houses as
herein provided other than the County Jail . The
police matron and Chief of Police shall respectively
instruct the assistants subject to them as to their
duties , and shall see that they give proper care to
the female prisoners under their charge .
§ 11. Matrons to Attend Criminal Court- When .
-The Police matron or one of the assistant police
matrons shall attend the Circuit Court and Police

CHAPTER 20 1057
Courts in said city whenever a female prisoner is
brought before said courts , and she shall have
charge of said women , subject to the orders of the
court.
12. "Police Station " , "Woman " , Meaning of
Words . The words " police station " shall be con-
strued under this Act to mean any place where per-
sons are temporarily under arrest ; the word
"Women " shall include any person of the female
sex.
§ 13. Matron to be Present When Female
Searched - Salary of Matron and Assistants- Ex-
penses , How and by Whom Paid . Whenever it be-
comes necessary to search a female prisoner , it shall
be done by or in the presence of the police matron or
an assistant police matron . The salary of the police
matron shall be one hundred dollars ( $100.00 ) per
month , and that of the assistant police matrons one
hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) per month . The expenses
necessary for providing the proper accomodations
for the police matron and assistant police matrons
at the jail , and for sufficient and suitable accomo-
dations for the female prisoners detained therein ,
as provided for in this Act , and the salary of the
police matron and the assistant police matrons at
the county jail , shall be borne by the County in which
said jail is located , and the expense necessary for
providing a suitable room or rooms at each of the
station houses designated for the detention of female
prisoners and the salary of each of the assistant
matrons at said station houses , and the expense of
providing suitable accomodations for said female
prisoners , shall be borne by the city of the first class
as other expenses in the police department are pro-
vided .
§ 14. Acts Repealed .- Chapter 8 of the Acts of
1912 of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
Acts-34

1058 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of Kentucky , now embraced in Sections 2877a - 1 to
2877a - 14 inclusive, of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
Baldwin's 1936 Revision , is hereby repealed .
Whereas , this Act is necessary to the proper ad-
ministration and efficiency and to the health and
welfare of the inmates of jails and station houses in
Cities of the First Class , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this Act shall become effective
immediately after its passage and the signature of
the governor . Approved by the Governor : April 12 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 21 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , repealing, amending
and re -enacting sections 4149 , 4151-2 , 4153 , 4154 , 4158 , 5162 ,
4073 , 4074 , 4130 , 4251 , 4239f and 4021a -1, Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1936 Edition , and repealing sections 4076 and 4149a ,
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , and Article III of
Chapter 22 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1906 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Sections 4021a - 1 , 4076 and 4149a , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , and Article III of
Chapter 22 of the Acts of the General assembly of
1906 be and the same are hereby repealed .
§ 2. Repeal , amend and re-enact section 4149 ,
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , so that
when amended and re-enacted it shall read as fol-
lows :
As soon as any taxpayer becomes delinquent , the
sheriff or the collector of the county where the prop-
erty is liable shall distrain sufficient personal prop-
erty of such delinquent , if found in the county , to
satisfy all taxes , penalties and interest due ; or , he
shall levy on a sufficient quantity of the delinquent's
real property for that purpose . A sheriff or tax

CHAPTER 21 1059
collector shall sell for cash any property belonging
to the delinquent taxpayer so levied upon or as much
thereof as will pay the taxes due , penalties , interest ,
costs and his commission , in the same manner that
property is sold under execution , except that the
land shall not be valued and shall be advertised by
posting for fifteen ( 15 ) days before the sale , a
written or printed notice at the court house door ,
and by publication once a week for two consecutive
weeks prior to the day of sale in a newspaper of
general bona fide circulation in the county , or by
printed hand bills posted for fifteen ( 15 ) days be-
fore the sale at the court house door and in three or
more conspicuous places in the taxing district ; pro-
vided , that said notices shall contain such a descrip-
tion of the property as will reasonably identify it ;
and he shall, not less than fifteen ( 15 ) days before
the sale , send a registered letter to the delinquent ,
addressed to his place of residence or place of busi-
ness , if such can be ascertained , notifying him of the
time and place of sale . As compensation for his
services the sheriff or collector shall have , in addi-
tion to his regular commission , one dollar ( $ 1.00 )
for each person whose property is advertised , to be
paid by the delinquent , but in no event to be paid
by the state , county or taxing district . Any ex-
penses incurred under the provisions of this act
which must be paid before the sale of the property
shall be paid by the county , and the county shall
have a first lien on the property for any sums so
paid.
§ 3. Repeal , amend and re -enact section 4151-2 ,
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , so that
when amended and re - enacted it shall read as fol-
lows :
When real property is sold for taxes , if no one
will bid for and purchase such real property at the

1060 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
amount of tax, penalty , interest , cost , including the
cost for advertising and the sheriff's or collector's
commission , it shall be the duty of the sheriff or col-
lector to purchase the same for the state , county and
taxing districts having taxes against the delinquent,
for the amount of taxes due , penalties , interest , costs
and commissions thereon , and he shall make return
to the county court clerk, as provided in this act ,
and the county court clerk shall certify same to the
Auditor of Public Accounts until the first Monday
in January , 1940 , and thereafter to the Department
of Revenue , and the sheriff or collector shall have
credit for the amount of such tax due the state in the
settlement with the Auditor of Public Accounts or
with the Department of Revenue . The owner of such
real property , his representatives , heirs or assigns ,
shall have the right to redeem the same from the
state , county and district at any time prior to the
actual sale of the land by the Commissioner of
Revenue or his proper designated agent , as pro-
vided in section five of this act , or from a purchaser
other than the state , county and district at any time
prior to the expiration of the six months notice re-
quired of such purchaser, as provided in section four
of this act , by paying the purchase money , with in-
terest at the rate of six per cent (6 % ) per annum
plus the costs , and in addition , when the state , county
and other district is the purchaser , the taxes , pen-
alties and interest assessed against same as provided
in sections five and twelve of this act .
The state , county and district shall have the right
to possession of lands purchased by them at any
time after the expiration of three ( 3 ) years from
the date of the sale provided for in section two of
this act , by giving the notice provided for in section
four of this act ; a purchaser other than the state ,

demption from five to three years shall not affect
any sales of property for taxes made before the
effective date of this Act .
In the redemption of lands sold to the state , county
and district for delinquent taxes , at any time within
the redemption period, the county court clerk is
hereby vested with the authority to receive pay-
ment of such delinquent taxes , penalties , interest ,
costs and commissions as are prescribed by law , and
he shall make report thereof to the Auditor of Public
Accounts until the first Monday in January, 1940 ,

treasurer of the county , and the proper officer of
the district as often as the latter officers may re-
quire , and not less than once in every thirty days ;
and he shall pay into the state treasury all moneys
collected by him due the state , and the county treas-
ury all moneys due the county , and all moneys due
the district to the proper officer thereof, as herein
authorized , less five per cent ( 5% ) commission for
the collection which he shall retain for his services ,
on the first day of every month and oftener if re-
quired by the Auditor of Public Accounts , or the
Department of Revenue after the first Monday in
January , 1940 , or either of the other officers men-
tioned .
Said report of the county court clerk shall show
the name of each owner and each piece of property
redeemed , the year's taxes for which delinquent , the
assessed valuation for the same for each year , and
the total amount paid to the particular taxing dis-
trict to which payment is being made .

CHAPTER 22.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation ; providing for the
classification , assessment and taxation of all items of direct or
contingent indebtedness of customers to brokers making pur-
chases or sales of securities for them in margin transactions ;
levying an annual ad valorem tax thereon exclusively for State
purposes , and providing for the collection thereof ; providing
also a method for the assessment of securities purchased by
brokers for customers in margin transactions , for the report
thereof by brokers to the State Tax Commission for assess-
ment by it, and for the collection of the tax from the customer
by the broker so reporting , and payment thereof to the State ;
providing a penalty for failure of the broker to make the
report required ; providing compensation to the broker for
services rendered in making such report , collection and pay .
ment; providing for the distribution of funds derived from
taxation under this Act ; providing for a referendum if desired ;
and repealing all laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. An annual tax is hereby levied for the year
1938 , based upon the assessed value as of July 1 ,
1937 , and thereafter , as of July 1 in each year , at
the rate of ten cents ( 10c ) on each one hundred dol-
lars ( $ 100 ) of the fair cash value of all items of
direct or contingent indebtedness of customers to
brokers making purchases or sales of securities for
them in margin transactions , which tax shall be paid
by such brokers . Such tax shall be for State pur-
poses only, and in lieu of all other ad valorem taxes
by the State or by any county , city, town or other
taxing district on such indebtedness to brokers .
The assessment , the date thereof, the equalization
and the collection of the tax imposed by this section

1074 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall be governed by the general laws applicable to
intangible personal property other than that
re-
fered to in Section
2hereof
.

CHAPTER 22 1075
count of the customer , who shall in that event have
no claim against the broker by reason of the pay-
ment of such tax charged to the customer's account .
Failure to make such report as herein required
shall subject the broker to a penalty of not less than
Two Hundred and Fifty ($250.00 ) Dollars nor more
than One Thousand ( $ 1,000.00 ) Dollars , to be recov-
ered , if so ordered by the Department of Revenue ,
by penal action on behalf of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky in the Franklin Circuit Court .
Upon the filing of such report with it in the year
1938 and each subsequent year , the Kentucky Tax
Commission shall assess the stocks , bonds and other
securities therein reported for taxation, at their fair
cash value insofar as subject to taxation in Ken-
tucky , and shall fix the amount of tax due thereon to
the State of Kentucky , applying the rate of taxation
fixed by law , and shall render to the broker a tax
bill covering the full amount of taxes due to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky upon the stocks , bonds
and other securities so reported . Such taxes shall
become due and payable in thirty days after the
rendition of such statement by the Department of
Revenue , and it is hereby made the duty of the
broker to pay , through the Department of Revenue ,
to the State Treasurer of Kentucky , within said
thirty day period , the taxes so levied , subject to the
same rate of discount provided in Section 4148 Ken-
tucky Statutes , and the broker shall have the right
to charge to and collect from each customer his por-
tion of the tax so levied upon any and all stocks ,
bonds or other securities so held or carried for
him .
In case the broker shall fail to pay all taxes due
hereunder , as and when same become due and pay-
able , the broker shall become liable for interest
thereon at the rate of six per cent per annum , and

1076 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
an additional penalty of ten ( 10) per cent upon the
amount of said taxes with interest .
As compensation for services rendered pursuant
to this section , the broker reporting and paying
taxes as therein provided , shall be entitled to deduct
from the amount of taxes due , a sum squal to four
(4 ) per cent thereof, which shall be allowed as a
credit thereon for such services so rendered .
§ 3. All taxes collected under the terms of this
Act shall be paid into the State treasury , to the
credit of the General Expenditure Fund .
§ 4. In event a referendum is desired upon this
Act, then such referendum shall be conducted in all
respects in the manner provided in Sections 4019b - 1
to 40196-6 Kentucky Statutes , Edition of 1936 .
§ 5. If any provision of this Act is held invalid ,
it shall not affect in any way the other provisions of
the Act, but they shall remain in full force and
effect .
§ 6. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Approved by the Governor : April 21, 1938 .

AN ACT to amend and re-enact Section 2573 , Carroll's Kentucky

WHEREAS , mercantile establishments , theatres and newspapers
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , as a result of such Court

" Provided , however , that the words " lottery or

CHAPTER 25 1079
CHAPTER 24 .
AN ACT appropriating money for the operation , maintenance ,
support , and functionings of the Department of Finance ,
Division of Purchases and Public Properties .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. There is hereby appropriated , out of the
General Expenditure Fund for the fiscal year begin-
ning July 1 , 1937 , and ending June 30 , 1938 , to the
Department of Finance , Division of Purchases and
Public Properties to supplement the appropriation
made by the Budget Session of the 1936 General As-
sembly, the sum of $ 38,500.00 .
§ 2. Whereas , the Division of Purchases and
Public Properties has during the current year been
called upon to repair public properties in order to
house divisions of the State government , and
Whereas , the Division , due to provisions of the
Reorganization Act , has been forced to assume addi-
tional expenses connected with public properties ,
which expense was not properly anticipated in the
appropriation made during the Budget Session of
the 1936 General Assembly , and
Whereas , the appropriation made is insufficient
to operate on for the remainder of the fiscal year
ending June 30 , 1938 , an emergency is declared to
exist and this ACT shall become effective on and
after the date of passage and signature of the Gover-
nor of the Commonwealth .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 25.
AN ACT repealing, amending , and re -enacting Section Twenty-
Eight ( 28 ) , Chapter Seven (7 ) of the Acts of the Third
Extraordinary Session of the 1936 General Assembly, codified
as Section 4281b -28 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition ,
and declaring an emergency .

1080 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section Twenty -Eight (28 ) , Chapter Seven ( 7) of
the Acts of the Third Extraordinary Session of the
1936 General Assembly, now codified as Section
4281b - 28 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936 Edition ,
be and the same is hereby repealed , amended and
re -enacted so that amended and re-enacted it shall
read as follows :
" The full amount of the tax payable by any tax-
payer as the same shall appear from the face of the
return , shall be paid to the Department at the time
fixed herein for filing returns ; provided , however ,
that any taxpayer who desires to do so may defer
payment of one-third of the amount shown by the
return to be due until the fifteenth day of the seventh
month following the end of the taxable year and an
additional one-third until the fifteenth day of the
eleventh month following the end of the taxable
year , and such deferred payments shall not be sub-
ject to any interest charge provided said payments
are received within the times specified above . In
the event additional amounts are found to be due
after the return has been filed , such amounts shall
be subject to interest at six per cent ( 6% ) per annum
from the time the return was originally due until
payment is actually made to the Department ."
Whereas , the date for the filing of income tax
returns is near at hand , and ,
Whereas , it has become apparent that interest
accruals of deferred payments of income tax are
burdensome , unfair , and out of harmony with the
Federal income tax , an emergency is hereby declared
to exist, and this Act shall become a law and be effec-
tive upon
itspassage and approval
bythe Governor
.
Approved by the Governor
: April
14, 1938
.

CHAPTER 27 1081
CHAPTER 26 .
AN ACT to enbale the Department of Welfare to receive money
from the United States of America and /or any of its agencies ,
and to enable the Department of Welfare to expend money so
received .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The Department of Welfare is and is hereby
authorized to accept from the United States of
America and/or any of its agencies , money under
any grant agreement entered into by the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and/or the Department of Wel-
fare , and the Department of Welfare is and is hereby
authorized upon the approval of the Department of
Finance and the Governor of the Commonwealth to
expend the money so received for the purchase of
land and the erection of buildings for the use of the
Department of Welfare for the confinement of con-
victs and the establishment of hospitals for the in-
sane , feeble -minded and epileptic wards of the state .
Provided , however , that any unexpended balance of
funds so received in any fiscal year shall be carried
as a credit to the Department of Welfare and be
available for expenditure during the next or succeed-
ing fiscal years for the purposes set out in this Act ,
subject to the approval of the Commissioner of
Finance . Approved by the Governor : April 12, 1938 .
CHAPTER 27 .
AN ACT to amend and re-enact Section 4618-83 , Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision , relating to Kentucky
Disabled Ex-Service Men's Board .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 4618-83 , Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision , be amended and

1082 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
re-enacted by striking therefrom the following
language :
"This section shall not be effective after June 30 ,
1938."
So that when said language is stricken from said
section and same is amended and re -enacted it shall
read as follows :
" The functions heretofore performed by the Ken-
tucky Disabled Ex - Service Men's Board , provided
by Chapter twenty-eight ( 28 ) , Acts of the General
Assembly of 1934 , shall, beginning July 1 , 1936 , be
continued by the veteran's division of the Depart-
ment of Military Affairs . The division shall pre-
pare in proper form , present , and prosecute the
claims of disabled ex- service men and women and
their dependents with the United States Veterans '
Administration and other federal agencies ; secure
and expedite action on said claims ; keep in contact
with the United States Veterans ' Administration so
that the status of any claim may be known at any
time ; continue to survey the State to make certain.
that no disabled veteran dr his dependents are
neglected ; and perform such other duties as the Ad-
jutant General may direct .
The words , " veterans " and " ex-service men and
women , " as used in this Act, include any individual
who served on active duty in the army, navy, or
marine corps of the United States and who has re-
ceived an honorable discharge from such service .
General administration of the work of this division
shall be lodged in the Adjutant General who shall
direct the work in the light of advice from the Ken-
tucky Disabled Ex- Service Men's Board of three
members . Said Board shall be composed of the
Lieutenant Governor , who shall act as Chairman of
the board ; the State Commander of the American

CHAPTER 29 1083
Legion , Department of Kentucky ; and the Adjutant
General , all ex-officio members . The members of
the Board shall serve without compensation , but
they shall be reimbursed for necessary travel ex-
penses incident to their official duties under this
section . "'
§ 2. That all laws or parts of laws in conflict
herewith are hereby repealed .
Approved by the Governor : April 4 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 28 .
AN ACT to amend and re- enact Section 1061a -1 of Carroll's Ken .
tucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision , and providing a
stenographer for county judges , and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That in all counties in this Commonwealth having
a population of two hundred thousand ( 200,000 ) or
over , and in all counties containing a city of the sec-
ond , third or fourth class , there is hereby created the
office of stenographer or clerk to the county judge ;
said stenographer or clerk shall be appointed by the
county judge for a term of four years , but may be
removed at any time by said county judge .
An emergency is hereby declared to exist and this
act shall become effective immediately upon its pas-
sage and signature of the Governor .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 29.
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Sections 4527-59 to 4527-81 ,
inclusive , of the Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision ,
relating to higher education for negroes ; changing the name of
the Kentucky State Industrial College to "Kentucky State Col-
lege for Negroes " ; discontinuing the West Kentucky Indus-

1084 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
trial College ; establishing the West Kentucky Vocational
Training School in place thereof, providing for its mainte
nance , operation and control, and making an appropriation
therefor.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby recognized as established
and maintained Kentucky State College for Negroes ,
located at Frankfort . The said college for colored
persons shall be maintained by the Commonwealth
with such endowments , incomes , buildings , and
equipment as shall enable them to do work such as is
done in other institutions of corresponding rank and
kind .
§ 2. It shall be the purpose of the Kentucky
State College for Negroes to train teachers for
schools for colored children , and to give such indus-
trial and general college training to young men and
women of the negro race as the State Board of Edu-
cation deems necessary or advisable .
$ 3. There shall be maintained in Kentucky
State College for Negroes a department for the edu-
cation of colored students in agriculture and the
mechanic arts , and for said purpose said board
shall be entitled to receive an equitable division of
the moneys arising from the sale of public lands and
appropriated to the Commonwealth of Kentucky
by an Act of Congress approved August 30 , 1890 ,
entitled "An Act to apply a portion of the proceeds
of public lands to the more complete endowment and
support of the college for the benefit of agriculture
and the mechanic arts established under the provi-
sions of the Act of Congress approved July 2 , 1862."
§ 4. The management and control of Kentucky
State College for Negroes shall be vested in the
State Board of Education . Said Board of Education
shall succeed to and is hereby invested with all the

CHAPTER 29 1085
·
duties , rights , powers , purposes , responsibilities and
jurisdiction of the present Board of Trustees of said
College , not inconsistent with this Act , and shall be
in possession and control of all records , books ,
papers , offices , equipment , supplies , moneys , funds ,
appropriations , lands and other property , real or
personal now or hereafter held for the benefit of said
institution .
§ 5. There is hereby recognized as established
and maintained the West Kentucky Vocational
Training School for Negroes , located at Paducah ,
which shall succeed on the 1st day of July , 1938 , to
all the buildings , properties and funds now owned
and used by the West Kentucky Industrial Col-
lege , except as hereinafter provided . The manage-
ment and control of West Kentucky Vocational
Training School for Negroes shall be vested in the
State Board of Education , which is hereby invested
with all the duties , rights , powers , purposes and re-
sponsibilities of the institution , and shall be in pos-
session and control of all records , books , papers , of-
fices , equipment , supplies , moneys , funds , appro-
priations , lands and other property , real or personal ,
now or hereafter held for the benefit of the said
institution .
§ 6. All records papers transcripts of credit ,
files , reports and books of every kind , except library
books , of West Kentucky Industrial College are
hereby transferred to Kentucky State College for
Negroes .
§ 7. The West Kentucky Vocational Training
School for Negroes is hereby authorized to conduct ,
maintain and operate under the control and super-
vision of the State Board of Education a State Voca-
tional Training School , the purpose of which shall
be to make available facilities for vocational train-
ing for colored children to be carried on in accord-

1086 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ance with the State plans for vocational education .
The curriculum shall be established by the State
Board of Education and shall be of the character
and rank given by approved schools of this type .
8. The State Board of Education acting as a
board of control for Kentucky State College for
Negroes and West Kentucky Vocational Training
School for Negroes shall be a body corporate , shall
have perpetual succession , with power to contract
and be contracted with , to sue and to be sued, to
plead and to be impleaded , to receive by any legal
mode of conveyance property of any description
and to have and to hold and to enjoy the same , also
to make and use a corporate seal with power to alter
the same ; to adopt bylaws , rules and regulations for
the government of said schools , their officers , agents
and employees ; provided such bylaws shall not con-
flict with the Constitution of the United States and
with the Constitution of Kentucky and the laws of
Kentucky .
§ 9. Said board of education shall have power to
purchase , sell , and hold real and personal property
in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for
the use and benefit of each of said schools , and to
condemn real estate in the manner provided by law
for condemnation of land for railroad purposes . It
shall have power to build and construct improve-
ments and to do all things deemed by it necessary
in carrying out the purposes for which said schools
are maintained. Any revenue accruing by reason of
the sale or conveyance of any real estate or build-
ing now owned by it, shall be reinvested in other
real estate and buildings for use of said school .
§ 10. It shall be illegal for any officer , board
member or any other person in any way connected
with either of the schools for colored persons to con-
tract any obligations for or on behalf of either insti-

CHAPTER 29 1087
tution , where there is no money or insufficient money ,
in the treasury or has been no money appropriated
for the purpose for which said contract or obligation
was made , and any of said persons who fail to com-
ply with this law shall be fined in each case not less
than two hundred and fifty dollars ( $250 . ) nor more
than two thousand dollars ( $ 2,000 . ) or confined not
less than ten days in jail nor more than six months ,
or both so fined and imprisoned .
§ 11. The board shall appoint a president for
Kentucky State College for Negroes and West Ken-
tucky Vocational Training School for Negroes , and
determine his salary and the tenure of office , the
term of which is not to exceed four years . The presi-
dent may be removed for cause .
§ 12. The president shall be the executive agent
of the State Board of Education in its administra-
tion of the affairs of the institutions and shall have
general supervision , subject to the control of the
board , of all educational and business affairs of the
institutions and of all disciplinary problems which
may arise.
13. The State Board of Education shall , upon
recommendation of the president , appoint and re-
move all officers , teachers , and such other employees
as are needed for the successful operation of the said
schools and shall determine their salaries , duties ,
and official relations ; provided that all executive of-
ficers , teachers , or other employees of the board of
trustees in office or under contract at the time this
act goes into effect shall serve out the terms for
which elected , unless removed for cause .
§ 14. No president , officer , or teacher shall be
removed except for incompetency , neglect of or re-
fusal to perform his duty , or for immoral conduct ,
nor shall such president , officer , or teacher be re-
moved until after ten days ' notice in writing stating

1088 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the nature of the charges preferred ; and such per-
son shall have an opportunity to make defense be-
fore the board by counsel or otherwise , and shall be
allowed to introduce testimony which shall be heard
and determined by the board.
§ 15. The State Board of Education shall adopt
such rules and regulations for the government of
said schools and for the guidance of the employees
and students of each of the said schools for colored
persons as it may deem proper . It may require of
the president, the teachers , or other officers such
reports as it may deem necessary ; provided , that
all existing rules and regulations made by the pre-
ceding boards shall remain in force until repealed
by order of the State Board of Education .
16. The State Board of Education shall have
the power to invest the faculty or a committee of the
faculty with power to suspend or expel any pupil
for disobedience of the rules and regulations of the
schools , for insubordination , or immoral conduct.
In every case of suspension or expulsion of a stu-
dent by the faculty or committee of the faculty , the
person so suspended or expelled shall be allowed an
appeal from the decision of the faculty to the State
Board of Education , but the decision of the State
Board of Education shall be final .
§ 17. The State Board of Education shall have
power to determine from time to time the number of
departments of study of each school , and shall pre-
scribe the curricula for each of the said schools for
colored persons . It shall have the power to issue
certificates or diplomas of graduation to students
completing such curricula as may be provided for.
$ 18. The State Board of Education shall have
power to maintain in connection with the Kentucky
State College for Negroes a model and practice
school under the supervision of trained teachers for

CHAPTER 29 1089
the purpose of giving observation and practice work
to the student teachers .
19. Tuition in said schools for colored students
shall be free to all colored residents of Kentucky .
The board shall fix the rate of tuition and the con-
ditions on which pupils who are not residents of
Kentucky may be admitted . The board shall fix such
incidental fees as it deems necessary to meet such
incidental expenses as may be incurred .
$ 20. The secretary of the State Board of Edu-
cation shall serve as secretary of the board in its
administration of the two schools for colored per-
sons . It shall be the duty of the secretary to keep
and prepare all records , books , and papers belong-
ing to the board in its administration of said schools .
He shall keep a journal of the proceedings of the
board , in which if requested by any member of the
board of education , the " ayes " and the " nays " of
all questions shall be entered . He shall prepare , un-
der the direction of the board, all reports and esti-
mates .
§ 21. The president of Kentukcy State College
for Negroes and the president of West Kentucky
Vocational Training School for Negroes shall make
to the State Board of Education written reports dur-
ing the month of August of each year , which shall
contain a full account of all receipts of money from
appropriations , tuitions , fees , and other sources ,
and the disbursement thereof, and for what purpose ,
and the condition of said school .
§ 22. The State Treasurer shall be the treasurer
of the State Board of Education in its administra-
tion of Kentucky State College for Negroes and
West Kentucky Vocational Training School for
Negroes .
§ 23. The State Board of Education shall meet
at least once each year at the school plant of each of
Acts- 35

1090 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the schools herewith placed under its management
and control for the purpose of inspecting the plant
and observing the work and the needs of the school .
$ 24. All appropriations made by the General
Assembly for the support of the said schools for
colored persons or for the benefit thereof , and all
grants , bequests , or donations by any individual or
corporation for specified use shall be applied to such
use or uses and no other .
§ 25. In respects other than those specifically
mentioned herein , the State Board of Education , in
its administration of the said schools for colored
persons , shall be guided by existing legislation re-
specting its organization , duties , powers and
activities .
§ 26. The provisions of this act are severable ,
and if any of its provisions shall be held unconsti-
tutional the decisions of the courts shall not affect
or impair any of the remaining provisions of this
act . It is hereby declared as a legislative intent that
this act would have been adopted had such uncon-
stitutional provisions not been included therein , and
any school service prohibited or impaired by such
provisions shall be administered by the laws in ef-
fect at the time this act became operative .
§ 27. For the payment of salaries and other ex-
penses incident to the maintenance and operation of
the West Kentucky Vocational Training School for
Negroes there is hereby appropriated to said school ,
out of the general expenditure fund , the sum of forty
thousand dollars ( $40,000 .) for the fiscal year
1938-39 .
For the payment of salaries and other expenses
incident to the maintenance and operation of the
West Kentucky Vocational Training School for Ne-
groes there is hereby appropriated to said school ,
out of the general expenditure fund , the sum of

CHAPTER 29 1091
thirty-nine thousand dollars ( $ 39,000 . ) for the fiscal
year 1939-40 .
§ 27 -a For the purpose of operating and main-
taining the boarding and rooming departments , ath-
letics and student activities , for instructional equip-
ment and supplies , and other necessary operation
and maintenance expenses of the West Kentucky
Vocational Training School for Negroes there are
hereby appropriated , for the fiscal year 1938-1939 all
fees and other operating receipts which shall be cov-
ered into the State Treasury promptly after the
close of each month and placed to the credit of the
revolving fund of the said institution . Withdrawals
for the use and benefit of the institution may be
made from the said revolving fund , maintained in
the State Treasury , when and as needed , upon requi-
sition from the governing authority of the institu-
tion , properly approved by the Commissioner of
Finance .
For the purpose of operating and maintaining the
boarding and rooming departments , athletics and
student activities , for instructional equipment and
supplies , and other necessary operation and main-
tenance expenses of the West Kentucky Vocational
Training School for Negroes there are hereby ap-

other operating receipts which shall be covered into
the State Treasury promptly after the close of each
month and placed to the credit of the revolving fund
of the said institution . Withdrawals for the use and
benefit of the institution may be made from the said
revolving fund , maintained in the State Treasury ,.
when and as needed , upon requisition from the gov-
erning authority of the institution , properly ap-
proved by the Commissioner of Finance .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .

1092 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 30 .
AN ACT to promote , develop and encourage better methods of
production , packing and marketing of strawberries , apples ,
peaches and other fruits ; providing for scientific and practical
studies of diseases , insects and parasites affecting fruit crops ;
providing for the publication and dissemination of information
concerning the results of such studies and of such other facts
and experiences as may be helpful to fruit growers ; imposing
certain duties in respect thereto upon the Agricultural Experi-
ment Station of the University of Kentucky ; and , making an
appropriation therefor .
WHEREAS , the soil and climatic conditions in Kentucky are
most favorable for the production of fine quality strawberries
and other berries , peaches , apples and various other fruits ;
and ,
WHEREAS , Kentucky is a leading State in the production of
the highest grade strawberries ; and ,
WHEREAS , fine orchards have been developed extensively in
certain sections of Kentucky ; and ,
WHEREAS , the growing and marketing of strawberries , peaches ,
apples and other fruits now are and should be of major impor-
tance to the prosperity and the development of the resources
of Kentucky ; and
WHEREAS , substantial appropriations have heretofore been
made by the General Assembly of Kentucky for the studies ,
work and service now being carried on through the Kentucky
Agricultural Experiment Station of the University of Kentucky
in aid of the promotion and development of better methods of
production and marketing of other principal crops in Ken-
tucky ; and ,
WHEREAS , the present facilities of the Sub -stations of the Ken-
tucky Agricultural Experiment Station, which are located in
Princeton and in Quicksand respectively , can be used
effectively for the additional service provided for in this Act ;
Now , Therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The Kentucky Agricultural Experiment Sta-
tion of the University of Kentucky shall appoint a
horticulturist whose duty it shall be to carry out the
purposes of this Act, and who shall devote his full
time to the performance of said duties . The horti-

CHAPTER 30 1093
culturist shall be scientifically trained in the field of
horticulture and a graduate of the College of Agri-
culture of the University of Kentucky or of some
other agricultural college of equal rating , and he
shall have had at least three years of practical horti-
cultural experience . The horticulturist shall be
provided with an office and suitable quarters at the
Princeton Sub-station , at which place he shall be
stationed .
The Kentucky Agricultural Experiment Station
shall also employ such additional research workers ,
clerical, stenographic and other help , and purchase
such supplies and equipment , and provide for travel-
ing expenses , in accordance with the regulations of
the University of Kentucky , as may be necessary for
carrying out the purposes of this Act .
The facilities of the Kentucky Agricultural Ex-
periment Station and of the Sub -stations at Prince-
ton and Quicksand , shall be available for the carry-
ing on of the research and investigations herein con-
templated and provided for .
§ 2. It hereby is made the duty of the University
of Kentucky , through the Kentucky Agricultural
Experiment Station and specifically through the
Princeton and Quicksand Sub- stations , to give spe-
cific and special consideration , attention and action
to the following :
(a ) To discover and demonstrate the best known
methods for the culture , packing and marketing of
strawberries , peaches , apples and other fruits ;
(b ) To discover and demonstrate the most effec-
tive methods for the prevention and control of dis-
eases and insects affecting fruit crops ;
(c ) To make or cause to be made studies of the
various types of pruning and cultural practices , and
"

1094 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
1
a thorough testing of the most promising new and
approved varieties of fruits and plants ;
(d ) To obtain and disseminate scientific and
practical findings relating to the foregoing ;
(e ) To arrange and promote meetings whereby
growers of fruits may exchange ideas and receive.
information relating to improved methods of grow-
ing , packing and marketing of fruits , and to obtain
and provide for outstanding specialists to attend
and address such meetings ;
(f) To provide for the publication and distribu-
tion of bulletins giving the results of scientific and
practical studies , of market information , and of such
other facts and experiences as may be helpful to
producers ;
(g) To provide for displaying fruit exhibits for
the purpose of advertising the State's strawberry ,
peach , apple and other fruit production , when in the
opinion of the horticulturist such displays should be
made to promote the best interests of the develop-
ment of such production in Kentucky ;
(h ) To carry on such other investigations and
studies as will assist the horticultural interests of
the State .
§ 3. There hereby is appropriated to the Ken-
tucky Agricultural Experiment Station for the uses
and purposes specified in this Act, from the General
Fund , or from any monies not otherwise appro-
priated , the following amounts :
(a) The sum of Ten Thousand ($ 10,000 ) Dollars
for the period beginning upon the effective date of
this Act and ending on June 30 , 1939 ; and
(b) The sum of Ten Thousand ($10,00 ) Dollars
for the period beginning July 1 , 1939 , and ending
June 30 , 1940 .
The appropriation herein made shall be used ex-

CHAPTER 31 1095
clusively for the uses and purposes provided by this
Act , and shall be in addition to any other money
available for use by the Kentucky Agricultural Ex-
periment Station in horticultural work now being
done .
§ 4. This Act shall be known and may be cited
as the " Melton -Cleveland Bill . "
§ 5. Whereas , it is necessary that the work
authorized by this Act be started promptly in order
to be of service to the horticultural interests of the
State this year ; and whereas , there is immediate
need for said work and service , an emergency hereby
is declared to exist and this Act shall be effective
immediately upon its passage and approval by the
Governor . Approved by the Governor : April 21 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 31 .
AN ACT relating to county finance repealing , amending and
re -enacting Section 2514 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936
Edition , and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Definitions .-Whenever used in this Act un-
less the context requires otherwise :
( 1 ) " Fund " means the receipts from any tax
levy or part thereof which must be or is maintained
separately for a specific county purpose .
( 2 ) " Unit " means any one of the several offices ,
agencies or functional activities of any county to
which a specific allocation of county money is made .
(3) " Appropriation account " means each sub-
division of a unit to which a specific allocation of
county money is made for any particular object or
objects . It is the intention to define " appropria-
tion accounts " so that they shall be used to identify

1096 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the classification of budgetary allotments as nearly
as practicable by organization unit , function , object
and character .
(4) "Classification " means the arrangement of
appropriation accounts in an orderly and workable
manner so as to permit accurate accounting and
facilitate expenditure and budgetary analysis
(5) . " Fiscal court " means the judge of the coun-
ty court and the justices of the peace or the judge
of the county court and the commissioners of the
county .
(6) " State Local Finance Officer " or " Local
Finance Officer " means the Commissioner of Rev-
enue or his agent designated in writing with the ap-
proval of the Governor .
(7) " Commission' means the county Debt Com-
mission as hereinafter established .
( 8) " Road and bridge bonds " or " road and
bridge funded indebtedness " means county road and
bridge warrants , bonds , or re -issues thereof of the
same character , authorized by Section 157a of the
Constitution of Kentucky .
§ 2. Administrative organization .-The Com-
missioner of Revenue , with the advice of the State
Local Finance Officer , if there be one appointed ,
shall have authority within the limits prescribed by
Section 4618-90 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1936
Edition , to employ , discharge , fix the compensation
of and authorize the payment of all legal and neces-
sary expenses of such office and field staff as he
deems necessary to administer the provisions of this
Act . The State Local Finance Officer shall have au-
thority to delegate such duties and powers as are
herein imposed on him.
§ 3. County Debt Commission .-There is hereby
created the County Debt Commission . The Gover-
nor of the Commonwealth shall be Chairman of the

CHAPTER 31 1097
Commission . In the absence of the Governor from
Frankfort , Kentucky , the Lieutenant -Governor shall
be Chairman . In the absence of both the Governor
and the Lieutenant -Governor from Frankfort , Ken-
tucky , the Commissioner of Finance shall be Chair-
man . The Chairman , the Treasurer of the Com-
monwealth , the Auditor of Public Accounts , the Sec-
retary of State , the Attorney General , the Commis-
sioner of Highways , the Commissioner of Finance
and the Commissioner of Revenue shall be members
of the Commission . Each member shall serve with-
out additional compensation , and each member ex-
cept the Governor may , by advising the Governor in
writing , authorize an executive officer of the depart-
ment of which he is head to act for him as a member
of the Commission .
The Commission is hereby authorized by majority
vote of the quorum present to adopt rules and regu-
lations appropriate for the enforcement of this Act ,
to hear and dispose of appeals from rulings of the
State Local Finance Officer and to perform such
other duties as are imposed upon it by this Act .
The Chairman and four other members of the
Commission shall constitute a quorum . The Chair-
man may , at his discretion , order the members of
the Commission to assemble and designate the time
and place of the meeting .
The Commission may hold public hearings on any
question before it, or the Chairman may appoint ,
under instructions to report any findings or recom-
mendations to it , a committee thereof or a referee
for such purpose . The officials or taxpayers of a
county or the creditors thereof having a material
interest in any hearing before the Commission shall ,
upon demand , be heard by the Commission , either
directly or by a committee or referee designated for
the purpose .

and make such special inquiries or investigations
as are necessary for the proper exercise of

CHAPTER 31 1099
pects of the county do not warrant a reasonable ex-

expenditures of the county ; if, in his discretion the
issue or re- issue of bonds will not serve the best

or the issuance thereof will be invalid .
The State Local Finance Officer shall give at least
two weeks ' notice of the hearing hereinabove re-
quired to the county judge of the county proposing
to issue such bonds , and any party having a material
interest shall have an opportunity to be heard and
to present evidence . The State Local Finance Of-

Local Finance Officer .
The Commission shall not pass upon the question
until and unless the Attorney General has certified
to the Commission that the issue of bonds proposed
is valid in all respects . In order to have all the
facts for such decision , the Attorney General shall
use the records of the proceedings and findings of
the State Local Finance Officer and such other in-

the State Local Finance Officer . The findings and
decision of the Attorney General shall be added to
the record.
If the Attorney General shall certify to the Com-

Commission , on the basis of the record prepared by
the State Local Finance Officer and submitted to the
Attorney General and any additional substantial
evidence which may be introduced , shall pass on the
decision of the State Local Finance Officer .

1100 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Within thirty ( 30 ) days after a decision by the
Commission , including a refusal to reveiw the deci-
sion of the State Local Finance Officer because the
Attorney General has given his opinion that the
proposed bond issue is invalid , any interested party
or taxpayer of the county , upon filing bond for costs ,
may appeal to the Franklin Circuit Court by filing
a copy of the record of the proceedings and findings
of the State Local Finance Officer , the opinion of the
Attorney General , and the decision of the Commis-
sion with the Clerk of the Franklin Circuit Court
and the Commission shall be made party defendant .
To facilitate adjudication , the State Local Finance
Officer is hereby required , on payment of a reason-
able fee therefor , to be fixed by the State Local Fi-
nance Officer , to supply a certified copy of such rec-
ords to such party . The case shall be advanced on
the docket of the Franklin Circuit Court and shall
be heard and decided as in the case of a declaratory
judgment, except that all findings of fact of the
Commission shall be final if supported by any sub-
stantial evidence . An appeal may be taken from the
Franklin Circuit Court to the Court of Appeals in
the same manner as the law now permits appeals in
declaratory judgment cases , except that such appeal
must be taken within thirty ( 30 ) days from the entry
of the judgment . In case no appeal is taken from an
approval of the bond issue by the State Local Fi-
nance Officer or the Commission , as provided in this
Act , the decision as to the legality of such bond issue
shall be res adjudicata in any subsequent case or
cases raising such question of legality . When bonds.
are approved as provided in this Act, they shall not
be required to be approved as provided in Sections
186c -6 and 186c - 7 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 Edition.
Record of the approval of bonds as provided in

CHAPTER 31 1101
this Section shall be made in the minutes of the next
meeting of the fiscal court of the county issuing the
bonds so approved .
§ 5. Assistance to counties .-A county shall ,
upon petition of the fiscal court , be entitled as a
matter of right to the assistance of the State Local
Finance Officer in formulating a plan for reorganiz-
ing its debt structure . Whenever the fiscal court of
any county shall have so petitioned the State Local
Finance Officer or shall have received the approval
of any issue or re -issue of county bonds voluntarily
submitted for approval as provided in this Act , all
bonds thereafter issued or re -issued by such county
must be approved as provided in this Act .
When any county ( a ) is in default on the principal
or interest of any funded or unfunded indebtedness ,
(b) gives evidence of becoming unable to meet prin-
cipal or interest maturities on any funded indebted-
ness within a reasonable time , or ( c ) has an indeb-
tedness which might, with advantage to the county ,
be funded or refunded , the State Local Finance Of-
ficer may investigate the debt situation and credit
standing of such county and negotiate with the offi-
cials and creditors thereof for the purpose of formu-
lating a plan for issuing or re -issuing bonds .
§ 6. Sale of county bonds .-The order of the
fiscal court of any county authorizing the issue or
re-issue and sale of any bonds shall specify ( a ) the
maximum amount to be issued , ( b ) the denomina-
tions in which the bonds shall be issued , ( c) the
maximum interest rate to be paid on the bonds , ( d )
the terms of bonds (if serial , a fully detailed princi-
pal maturity schedule shall be shown ) , and such
other information as the Commission may by regu-
lation require . A certified copy of such order shall
be submitted to the State Local Finance Officer , who

1102 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall expedite approval or disapproval if requested
or required by this Act.
All county bonds offered for sale shall be adver-
tised for sealed competitive bids at least once during
each of two consecutive weeks in a newspaper of
general bona fide circulation in Kentucky and in the
county offering the bonds for sale , and , if deemed
advisable , twice in a periodical having general cir-
culation among bond buyers and shall be sold by
the fiscal court to the bidder at such sale offering the
price most advantageous to the county, and the State
Local Finance Officer may offer to purchase the
bonds , under an agreement to re -sell , by submitting
a bid at the same time and in the same manner as
other bids ; provided, a county may exchange

§ 8. County sinking funds .- On or before the

shall be delivered to the judge of each county along
