County Sinking Fund rendered the county judge for
the fiscal year during which the coupons or bonds.
were paid and cancelled .
§ 9. Investment of County Sinking Fund .- The
State Local Finance Officer , with the approval of the
Commission , shall have authority to invest any funds
accrued to the County Sinking Fund and the fiscal
court shall have authority to invest any funds ac-
crued to and maintained in a county sinking fund

1106 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
which are not needed to meet interest and principal
maturities on county debts within the next succeed-
ing twelve ( 12 ) months in obligations secured by
the credit of the United States Government , or in
warrants of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, or in
road and bridge bonds of any county of the Com-
monwealth which have been approved by the Com-
mission for such investment , or in other bonds of
the issue not exceeding the amount of the sinking
fund deposits for the payment of principal and in-
terest of such issue ; provided that such county bonds
so purchased may be cancelled and interest thereon
shall cease ; provided further that all county bonds
thus purchased shall mature before the money with
which they were purchased shall be required for
meeting obligations of the Sinking Fund ; and pro-
vided also that any county bonds acquired must be
purchased at a price not exceeding that offered in
the open market , if any , and not exceeding par
value plus accrued interest if the bonds mature
within three years , or one hundred five per cent
( 105 % ) par value plus accrued interest if the bonds
do not mature within three (3 ) years.
Any earnings or losses of the County Sinking
Fund shall be charged or credited as of July 1 of
each year among the counties maintaining deposits
therein in the same ratio as the amount of their
deposits remaining therein for a period of more than
six (6) months during the twelve ( 12 ) months ' pe-
riod preceding the allocation bears to the total de-
posits therein .
10. Place of payment of bonds .-The State
Local Finance Officer shall specify the place or places
of payment of all principal and interest of county
bonds approved as provided in this Act .
§ 11. Section 2514 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,

CHAPTER 31 1107
1936 Edition , is hereby repealed , amended and re-
enacted so that it will read as follows :
Section 2514. Actions that must be brought with-
in fifteen years after accrual of right ; extension of
time of liens .- Civil actions , other than those for the
recovery of real property , shall be commenced with-
in the following periods, after cause of action has
accrued , and not after : An action upon a judgment
or decree of any court of this State or of the United
States , or of any state or territory thereof , the period
to be computed from the date of the last execution
thereon ; an action or suit upon a recognizance , bond
or written contract ; an action upon the official bond
of a sheriff , marshal , sergeant , clerk, constable or
any other public officer , or any commissioner , receiv-
er , curator personal repesentative , guardian , com-
mittee , or trustee appointed by a court or authority
of law ; an action upon an appeal bond , or bond given
on a supersedeas , attachment , injunction , order of
arrest , or for the delivery of property , or for the
forthcoming of property , or to obey or perform an
order or judgment of court in an action , or upon a
bond for costs , or any other bond taken by a court
or judge or by an officer pursuant to the directions
of a court or judge , in an action , or after judgment
or decree , or upon a replevin , sale or delivery bond
taken under execution , decree or warrant of dis-
tress , upon an indemnifying bond taken under a
statue , or upon a bond to suspend a proceeding , or
upon a bond or obligation for the payment of money
or property , or for the performance of any under-
taking , shall be commenced within fifteen years after
the cause of action first accrued ; and no promise , or
acknowledgment , or payment of money by any per-
son bound on any bond or obligation for the pay-
ment of money secured by a lien shall operate as a
prolongation or extension of the time within which

1108 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
such lien may be enforced as against purchasers or
creditors , unless the promisor and the holder of the
lien , before fifteen years after the maturity of the
debt , shall enter a memorandum on the margin of
the record of the deed or mortgage , attested by the
clerk , showing that the debt is extended , and for
what time it is extended , and the amount still due
thereon . ( The payee shall pay the clerk a fee of
twenty -five cents ( 25c ) for his services . )
Provided , however , that a cause of action existing
against any county , by virtue of any bonded obliga-
tion for the payment of money by any county, after
the said county shall have in good faith offered to
redeem such obligations , which shall have matured ,
by the payment in full of all principal and interest
due, shall expire and become ineffective at the end
of three ( 3 ) years thereafter or at the end of three
(3 ) years from the effective date of this Act , which-
ever be the longer period .
§ 12. Registration of bonds .-Registration of
bonds , when required , shall be with the State Local
Finance Officer , who shall be authorized to require
the registration of any bonds approved as provided
in this Act . The Commission shall have power to
prescribe appropriate rules and regulations govern-
ing the registration of bonds .
§ 13. Not retroactive .- Nothing contained in
this Act shall affect any former procedure or order
made prior to the effective date of this Act by the
fiscal court of any county or any court of competent
jurisdiction respecting the sale or validity of any
county bond heretofore authorized .
14 (a ) . If the proposed county budget fails to
comply with any requirements of law , the State Lo-
cal Finance Officer , who is hereby authorized to ap-
prove or disapprove the same , shall advise the cour-
ty judge of such lack of compliance and may refuse

CHAPTER 31 1109
to approve the budget until he is authorized by the
county budget commission to make such changes as
he deems to be required by law.
§ 14. ( b) Within fifteen (15 ) days after the
adoption by the fiscal court , the county judge shall
certify to the State Local Finance Officer a copy of
the original budget as approved by the State Local
Finance Officer in which all changes made by the
fiscal court are clearly indicated .
§ 14 ( c ) . The fiscal court , with the approval of
the budget commission , may make provision for the
expenditure of receipts unanticipated in the original
budget by preparing an amendment to its budget ,
which shall show the source and amount of the
unanticipated receipts and shall specify the appro-
priation account or accounts which are to be in-
creased thereby ; and said amendment shall be sub-
mitted to the State Local Finance Officer in the same
manner and subject to the same provisions as the
original budget .
§ 14 ( d ) . The fiscal court shall have authority to
borrow money in anticipation of revenue receipts
to meet the current expenses of the county as they
accrue and to pay interest and/or principal on bond-
ed debts as they become due , provided that such
current borrowing shall not exceed seventy five per
cent (75 % ) of the unrealized and unencumbered
revenue estimated in the budget to be derived from
a tax levy for the then current fiscal year from which
the principal of the borrowed money and the in-
terest thereon may be paid ; provided , however , that
bonded debt duly authorized and incurred shall not
be considered to be in contravention of this para-
graph .
§ 14 ( e ) . No county may , during the first half of
the 1941-42 fiscal year , or any fourth fiscal year
thereafter , encumber or expend more than sixty five

1110 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
per cent ( 65 % ) of all its current funds , taken as a
unit , budgeted for such fiscal year , excepting and
excluding budgetary allotments for and payments
of principal and interest of bonded indebtedness ,
except in the case of an emergency respecting which
the fiscal court , the budget commission and the State
Local Finance Officer unanimously agree and so
state in writing .
§ 14 (f ) . Counties may be classified for prescrib-
ing accounts as in the case of budgets .
§ 14 (g ) . For the three months ending Septem-
ber 30 , 1938 , and each third month thereafter , the
clerk of the fiscal court shall prepare or cause to be
prepared a statement showing for the current fiscal
year to date actual receipts from each county rev-
enue source , the totals of all encumbrances and ex-
penditures charged against each budget fund , the
unencumbered balance of the fund , any transfers
made to or from the fund , and such other informa-
tion as the State Local Finance Officer shall pre-
scribe . Said statement , made within fifteen ( 15 )
days following the close of the period which it cov-
ers , shall be posted by the clerk of the fiscal court
in a conspicuous place in the courthouse near the
front door for at least ten (10 ) consecutive days , a
copy shall be transmitted to the State Local Finance
Officer and to the county judge , and said statement
shall be read by the clerk of the fiscal court to the
fiscal court at the next meeting following its release .
$ 15 Officers and employees may not hold
county bonds .- Ownership of obligations of Ken-
tucky counties shall disqualify the Commissioner of
Revenue or any of his appointees from serving in
any capacity incident to the administration of this
Act , and any official or employee performing any
act in violation of this section shall be removed from
office or employment and made ineligible for reap-

CHAPTER 31 1111
pointment in the same or similar capacity , and shall ,
upon conviction in a court of competent jurisdiction ,
be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and punished by
a fine of not more than five hundred dollars ( $ 500.00 )
or ninety ( 90 ) days imprisonment , or both .
§ 16. Penalties .-Any county or state official
who shall knowingly do any act hereinabove pro-
hibited , or who shall knowingly fail to do any act
hereinabove required , shall, in addition to the specific
liabilities imposed by this Act , be guilty of a mis-
demeanor and upon conviction thereof, shall have
his office declared vacant and may be punished by a
fine of not more than five hundred dollars ( $500.00 )
or ninety ( 90 ) days imprisonment , or both .
17. Laws repealed .-All laws in conflict with
the provisions of this Act are hereby repealed to the
extent of such conflict .
§ 18. Severability .-If any provision , section ,
paragraph or phrase hereof, or if the application
thereof to any person , circumstance or condition
shall be held invalid , such decision shall not affect
or impair the remainder of the Act , it being hereby
declared the legislative intent that the remainder of
this Act would have been passed by the General As-
sembly without the invalid section , paragraph or
phrase , or that the General Assembly would have
passed this Act without intending the application of
it to such person , circumstance or condition .
19. This Act shall be cited as the " County
Debt Act ."
§ 20. Declaring an emergency . The distressed
condition of the counties of the Commonwealth ,
which will make it impossible for an increasing num-
ber to meet their obligations , is declared to consti-
tute an emergency , and this Act, except when other-

1112 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
wise specifically provided , shall become a law and be
effective on its passage and approval by the
Governor . Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 32 .
AN ACT appropriating $35,000 for the establishment and main-
tenance of an exhibit at the "New York World's Fair 1939 ".
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
There is hereby appropriated to the Department
of Conservation for the fiscal year beginning July 1 ,

way Fund, the sum of thirty -five thousand ( 35,000 )

CHAPTER 33 .
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Chapter 42 , Acts of 1932 , page
322 , being Section 415b -6 ( 2 ) relating to Confederate pensions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter 42 , Acts of 1932 , page 322 , now sec-
tion 415b - 6 ( 2 ) of the 1936 Edition , Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , be amended and re -enacted , so that
when so re - enacted it will read as follows :
Any Confederate veteran who has been an actual
bona fide resident of the State since January 1 ,
1915 , and who did actual military service in the
confederate army from the date of his enlistment

CHAPTER 33 1113
to the close of the war , or until he was honor-
ably discharged as an officer or private soldier or
sailor in the military and naval service of the con-
federate states of America in the war of 1861 to
1865 , shall be paid out of the state treasury the sum
of fifty ( $50.00 ) dollars per month upon proof of the
fact according to forms and regulations prescribed
by this act and conditions therein . Provided , that
any officer or private soldier or sailor who was pre-
vented from so serving until the close of the war , or
until he was honorably discharged by reason of
wounds or disease contracted in the line of duty ,
which rendered him unable to perform further mili-
tary service , shall be entitled to the benefits of this
act as though he had served until the close of the
war , or until honorably discharged ; and provided
further , that any officer or private soldier or sailor
who deserted or was dishonorably discharged , shall
not be entitled to the benefits of this act unless he re-
enlisted or returned to the service in the confederate
army , and served until the close of the war , or until
he was honorably discharged or was prevented from
so serving by wounds received or disease contracted.
in line of duty which rendered him unable to per-
form further military service . Provided also , that
the widow of any such officer , private soldier or
sailor who would have been entitled to the pension
provided in this Act and which widow was living
with him as his legal wife at the time of his death ,
and has not re-married again since the death of her
veteran husband , and who has been an actual bona
fide resident of the State since January 1 , 1915 , shall
be paid out of the State Treasury the sum of Fifty
dollars ( $ 50.00 ) per month if married to such vet-
eran prior to January 1 , 1875 ; forty dollars ( $40.00 )
per month if married to such veteran prior to Jan-

1114 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
uary 1 , 1895 , and subsequent to January 1 , 1870 ;
thirty dollars ( $ 30.00 ) per month if married to such
veteran prior to January 1 , 1915 , and subsequent to
January 1 , 1890. Payments hereunder are to be
made upon proof of the facts according to forms and
regulations prescribed by this Act and regulations
therein . Since many of the veterans and widows
of veterans are of such an advanced age that they
may not receive this expression of appreciation
unless this Act is effective immediately , such is de-
clared to constitute an emergency and this Act shall
become a law and be effective on its passage and
approval by the Governor .
Approved by the Governor : April 21 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 34 .
AN ACT repealing , amending and re-enacting section 2741L- 1,
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision , relating
to the acquisition or construction of water works and sewer-
age systems by second , third , fourth , fifth and sixth class
cities .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 2741L -1 , Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision , be and the same
is hereby repealed , amended and re -enacted so that
said section as amended and re -enacted will read as
follows :
" That cities of the second , third , fourth , fifth and
sixth class whether organized under the general law
or special charter law , and including cities where
the commission form of government has been
adopted , are hereby authorized and empowered to
purchase , establish , erect , maintain and operate
water works and/or sewerage system , and necessary
appurtenances thereto , within or without the corpo-

CHAPTER 35 1115
rate limits under the provisions of this Act , for the
purpose of supplying such city or town and inhabi-
tants thereof with water and/or sewerage system .
Whenever any city of the fourth , fifth and sixth
classes determines to acquire or construct a sewer-
age system under the provisions of this act , and
property , real or personal , located either within or
without the boundaries of said city is necessary to
complete either the plant or system to be acquired
or constructed , and an ordinance has been adopted
and published determining and declaring said neces-
sity , then if the compensation for the sale of said
property can not be agreed upon by the city and the
owner or owners thereof , said city is hereby author-
ized to condemn and take possession of such prop-
erty for said purpose in the same way and manner
as is now provided by law for the condemnation of
real estate for railroad purposes ."
§ 2. That all acts and parts of acts in conflict
with this act are hereby repealed to the extent of the
conflict . Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 35.
AN ACT to authorize the judges of police courts in cities of the
second class to postpone rendition of judgment , to probate the
offenders , and to make rules and regulations for the arrest of
defendants and enforcement of judgment rendered in certain
criminal cases ; to provide for the appointment , duties and
salary of a probation officer, and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. In prosecutions for crime , in the police
courts in cities of the second class , except as herein-
after provided , where the defendant has pleaded or
been found guilty , and the defendant has never
before been convicted of a felony either in this State

1116 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or elsewhere , and it appears to the satisfaction of
the court that the character of the defendant and
circumstances of the case are such that he is not
likely again to engage in an offensive course of con-
duct , and the public good does not demand or require
that he shall suffer the penalty imposed by law ,
such court may postpone the rendition of judgment
on such terms and conditions as the court may deem
proper . Whenever in the opinion of the court the
sentence should no longer be postponed , the court
shall have power to cause a warrant to issue for the
defendant and he may thereupon on his arrest be
sentenced and such sentence carried into immediate
execution .
2. To properly carry out the provisions of this
act there is hereby created the office of probation
officer in the cities hereinbefore stated , whose duty
it shall be to act in cooperation with the police
department of said cities and to see that all probated
offenders do not violate the law during their term of
probation and to check the circumstances of the case
to be probated and the character of the defendant
and to do any other duty properly requested by the
court . Said probation officer shall have all powers
now imposed upon police officers . He shall be ap-
pointed by the police judge . His salary shall not
be less than twelve hundred ( $1200 ) dollars nor more
than eighteen hundred dollars ( $ 1800 ) a year and
shall be fixed by the court . Said salary to be paid
out of the fines and forfeitures imposed and collected
by said police court and no part of said salary shall
be paid by the city except as herein stated .
Provided , however , the Board of Commissioners
or the City Council , may by ordinance provide that
one of the regular police force of the city , to be
designated by the Police Judge , shall be assigned to
perform said duties , without additional pay .

CHAPTER 36 1117
§ 3. Whereas it is necessary that some imme-
diate action be taken with reference to first - time ,
minor offenders , therefore , it is declared that an
emergency exists and this act shall take effect imme-
diately upon its passage and approval by the Gov-
ernor .
§ 4. All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Approved by the Governor : April 9, 1938 .
CHAPTER 36 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend and reenact Section 4135 , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1936 revision , and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .
" That Section 4135 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1936 Revision , be repealed , amended , and re - enacted
so that said section as amended and re -enacted will
read as follows " :
The outgoing sheriff , as soon as his successor
has been qualified and inducted into office and his
official bond approved , shall immediately vacate his
office , deliver to his successor all books , papers , rec-
ords , and other property held by virtue of his office
and shall make a full and complete settlement of his
accounts as sheriff . Except that the outgoing
sheriff upon the execution , as herein provided , a new
revenue bond , shall keep in his possession all unpaid
tax bills and shall collect and account for same as
provided by law , together with any and all other
taxes due upon the levy for the preceding year ,
whether certified prior or subsequent to his qualifica-
tion as such collector , and shall have until the first
day of June after his term of office has expired to
make his settlement with the auditor of public ac-

1118 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
counts and the fiscal court of his county and to re-
cive his quietus , and immediately thereafter he shall
deliver these records to his successor . On the
failure of any outgoing sheriff for ten days to
comply with this provision of the section , he shall be
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and , on conviction ,
be fined in a sum not less than fifty dollars ( $50.00 )
nor more than five hundred dollars ($ 500.00 ) and be
liable on his bond for any default .
Whereas , all taxes levied for the year 1937 and
certified after the first Monday in January , 1938 ,
have no designated officer under the present law to
collect the same and it is necessary to so designate
such officer so that said taxes may be paid and col-
lected at once as authorized by this amendment , an
emergency is declared to exist and this act shall take
effect upon approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 37 .
AN ACT repealing , amending and re -enacting section 331e-3
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes and relating particularly to the
appointment and compensation of Probation Officers in the
various counties of the Commonwealth and to the appointment
and compensation of Probation Officers in Counties wherein
is located a city of the First Class , and for the creation of a
Merit Board of the Juvenile Court in counties wherein there
is a city of the first class of four appointed members with the
County Judge as a member ex officio , with power to vote and
the appointment , qualification , terms of office , removability
of the members of said Merit Board ; for definding the rights ,
powers and duties of said Merit Board and providing for the
organization and administration of said Merit Board and fixing
the time when the changes provided for herein shall become
effective , providing for a merit system of employment of pro-
bation officers and employees in the Juvenile Court ; prohibit-
ing the making or soliciting of political contributions by
employees in positions subject to the provisions of this Act ;

CHAPTER 37 1119
providing for maintaining the validity of the remainder of the
Act in case any part shall be declared invalid and providing
for the repeal of any Acts or parts of Acts in conflict herewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 331e -3 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes is hereby repealed , amended , and re -enacted , so
that when so amended and re -enacted the said Sec-
tion shall read as follows :
1. The County Courts of the several Counties of
this State shall have authority to appoint and desig-
nate one or more discreet persons of good moral
character to serve as volunteer Probation Officers
during the pleasure of the Court without any com-
pensation whatsoever . In Counties having a city of
the second class the County Court may appoint one
probation officer , who shall be designated as Chief
Probation Officer and who shall receive a salary of
not exceeding twenty -four hundred ( $2,400.00 ) dol-
lars per annum , to be fixed by the County Judge ; one
or more assistant probation officers , one of whom
shall receive a salary of not exceeding twelve hun-
dred ( $ 1,200.00 ) dollars per annum , and all other
assistants so appointed shall receive a salary of not
exceeding one thousand ( $ 1,000.00 ) dollars per an-
num , such salaries to be fixed by the Judge of the
County Court . Such salaried probation officers
shall be allowed their actual and necessary expenses
incurred in the performances of their duties ; said
salaries and expenses to be paid as provided herein .
All such salaried probation officers shall have all the
powers of sheriffs and County Patrolmen in making
arrests .
In Counties having a city of the first class the
Judge of the County Court may appoint one proba-
tion officer , who shall be designated as Chief Proba-
tion Officer , and who shall receive a salary of not less

1120 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
than four thousand ( $4,000.00 ) dollars nor more
than five thousand ($5,000.00 ) dollars per annum , to
be fixed by the Fiscal Court ; one or more assistant
probation officers , two of whom shall receive a salary
of not exceeding twenty-four hundred ( $ 2,400.00 )
dollars per annum and all other assistant probation
officers so appointed shall receive a salary of not
exceeding eighteen hundred ($1,800.00 ) dollars per
annum , such salaries to be fixed by the Fiscal Court .
Such salaried probation officers shall be allowed
their actual and necessary expenses incurred in the
performance of their duties , said salaries and ex-
penses to be paid as provided herein. All such
salaried probation officers have the powers of sher-
iffs and county patrolmen in making arrests . The
salaries and the reimbursements for expenses for
the probation officers provided for herein shall be
paid out of the available funds of the County and
the salaries shall be paid in equal monthly install-
ments .
2. In all counties wherein there is located a city
of the first class , there shall be established a Merit
Board composed of four members appointed by the
County Judge from a list of fifteen reputable , social-
ly minded citizens to be submitted by the Council of
Social Agencies of the county , within thirty days
after this Act takes effect or a vacancy occurs .
Should said list not be submitted in the prescribed
time , the County Judge shall appoint four reputable
and socially minded citizens of his own choice . The
County Judge of such County shall be a member ex
officio of said Board , and in case of a tie on any
matter before the Board , the County Judge shall
cast the deciding vote . Not more than two members
of said Merit Board shall be registered Independent
or be members of any one political party. Each

CHAPTER 37 1121
member of said Board appointed hereunder shall
serve without compensation , and shall serve for a
term of four years except for those first appointed
under this Act which shall be for terms of one , two ,
three , and four years , respectively . Provided
further , however , that any member of said Board
appointed to fill the vacancy for any unexpired term
shall be appointed only for the remainder of such
unexpired term . Any member of said Board ap-
pointed according to the provisions of this Act shall
be eligible for re -appointment . Members of the
Merit Board of the Juvenile Court shall be at least
twenty-five ( 25 ) years of age and shall have been
residents and voters in the County for at least three
years , and no member of the said Board shall be eli-
gible for any appointive or elective office of the said
county or any city in said county during the time he
serves as a member of the said Board . The County
Judge shall appoint said Board within ten days
after the list of recommendations above provided
for is submitted . Said Board shall meet to organize
and function immediately upon appointment . This
Board shall formulate rules and regulations for
carrying out the provisions of this Act. The Board
shall hold quarterly meetings . Special meetings
may be called by the Chairman . The reasonable
expenses necessary for the Merit Board to carry out
the provisions and purpose of this Act shall be ap-
propriated by the Fiscal Court of said County.
3. The Board from time to time shall conduct
competitive examinations for the positions of Chief
Probation Officer , two first assistant probation of-
ficers , all other assistant probation officers , and all
other employees , and such examination shall be both
written and oral to establish the fitness and merit
of applicants for positions as probation officers and
Acts- 36

1122 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
employees . The Board shall fix standards and
qualifications for eligibility of candidates for
said competitive examinations and shall choose for
the respective positions the candidates receiving the
highest grades in the examinations.
In the event of an unexpected vacancy in any of
the probation officer positions , the Merit Board may
make an emergency appointment to fill said vacancy
for a period sufficient for them to hold the regular
examination for applicants for said position , such
emergency appointment to last not longer than
ninety days .
4. Dismissal or demotion or promotion of em-
ployees in positions subject to the provisions of this
Act may be only by the Chief Probation Officer to-
gether with the approval of the Merit Board of the
Juvenile Court . Nothing in this Act shall permit
the dismissal or demotion of any probation officer
subject to the provisions of this Act on account of
religious faith or political affiliation . All probation
officers now serving under appointment in the Juve-
nile Court shall be eligible to examination and re-
commendation on the same terms as the other appli-
cants . No probation officer shall hold office under
an appointment by the appointive authority who
has not qualified as provided herein and been certi-
fied as eligible for appointment as probation officer
by the Merit Board of the Juvenile Court .
No probation officer shall directly or indirectly
solicit or remit funds or in any manner be concerned
with the soliciting for or remitting funds to any
political party or any candidate for public office ; nor
shall any probation officer take any active part in
political campaigns or be a member of any campaign
committees or governing committees of any political
organization . Any violation of this section shall

CHAPTER 38 1123
operate to forfeit the office or position held by the
probation officer violating the same .
5. Should any section , paragraph , sentence ,
clause or phrase of this Act be declared unconstitu-
tional or invalid for any reason , the remainder of
said Act shall not be affected thereby .
6. It appearing that a great number of boys in
the City just over 17 years of age-the juvenile age
-are being brought into the Criminal Court on
charges of serious and grave felonies , and that a
great number of boys and girls under juvenile age
are rapidly becoming confirmed in criminal tenden-
cies which will likely result in their becoming con-
firmed criminals whose care in institutions will cause
great expense to the City , County and State . There-
fore an emergency is declared to exist and this action
shall take effect immediately upon its passage and
approval by the Governor .
Approved by the Governor : April 14 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 38 .
AN ACT relating to the establishment of state licensed ware-
houses for the storage of grain on farms in the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and providing, but not by way of limitation , for
the establishment of state licensed warehouses for the storage
of grain on farms in the Commonwealth of Kentucky and
providing for the issuance of negotiable and non -negotiable
storage certificates for grain stored on the farm ; for general
supervision by the Board of Agriculture , hereinafter referred
to as the Board of Agriculture , of the administration of the
provisions of this act , including , but not by way of limitation ,
the right and duty to make rules and regulations for the
enforcement of the provisions of this act ; for fees to the
Board of Agriculture and their disposition ; for local super-
visory boards , including , but not by way of limitation , the
membership thereof , the duties and powers thereof , the licens
ing of said board by the Board of Agriculture , the provisions
of said license , the qualifications of the members of said

1124 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
board , the oath and term of the members thereof and the
mode and manner of filling vacancies therein ; for officers of
said board ; for oath and bond of certain officers thereof ; for
funds to defray the expenses and costs of said board , includ .
ing maximum and minimum fees to be charged to the owner
of the stored grain and for the disbursement of expenses from
such fund ; for compensation to members of the board ; for
refunds ; for a local sealer , the authority of such sealer, the
oath and bond of the sealer , the duties of the sealer , the right
of the sealer to enter the premises whereat the grain is stored ,
the fees of the sealer , the type of seals and locks to be used ;
making it unlawful for unauthorized persons to break , tamper
or interfere with any seal or lock and prescribing penalties
therefor ; making unlawful the unauthorized delivery of grain
out of possession of owner knowing that a negotiable certifi
cate is outstanding and providing penalties therefor ; making
it unlawful to sell , mortgage , pledge , hypothecate , incumber ,
remove or attempt to sell, mortgage , pledge , hypothecate ,
incumber or. remove stored grain after the issuance of a cer-
tificate for grain in storage and providing penalties therefor ;
providing for the application of existing or hereafter passed
laws ; providing for the issuance of certificates and the form
and contents of such certificates ; providing for the amounts of
certificates ; prohibiting limitation of certain liability of the
owner from being included in said certificates ; prohibiting and
providing penalties for the fraudulent issuance of certificates ;
prohibiting , in certain cases , and providing penalties for the
improper issuance of duplicate certificates ; providing for the
issuance of "board duplicate " certificates ; providing for non-
negotiable certificates ; providing for negotiability of certain
forms of certificates ; providing for duplicate certificates and
the recording thereof with the County Court Clerk and the
indexing thereof and fees to be received therefor by the County
Court Clerk , the assignment and release thereof and the fees
of the County Clerk therefor ; providing for the duty of the
owner to deliver the stored grain to the holder of the certifi-
cate and for the burden of proof of justifying a refusal to
deliver ; providing for decisions of the local supervisory board ,
for appeal therefrom to the Board of Agriculture , for the
finality of its decision ; providing for complaints direct to the
Board of Agriculture , for hearings before the Board of Agri-
culture, for decisions and orders of the Board of Agriculture
and for the payment of costs and expenses of hearings before
the Board of Agriculture ; providing for a liberal construction
of the act ; providing that if any provision of this act is held

CHAPTER 38 1125
invalid , it shall not affect the other provisions thereof; and
repealing any and all acts or parts of acts and any and all
parts of laws in conflict herewith and declaring an emergency
to exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. This act may be known , cited and referred
to as the " Unbonded Farm Agricultural Warehouse
Act " of 1938 .
§ 2. Wherever the words " Board of Agricul-
ture " shall appear in this act it shall refer to the
Board of Agriculture , of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky .
The word " board " shall refer to any local super-
visory board of individual producers appointed by
the Board of Agriculture under the provisions of
this act .
The word " sealer " shall refer to any person
whose duty it shall be under the provisions of this
act to seal any granary , crib , bin or other receptacle
for grain .
The word " certificate " shall refer to any certifi-
cate or receipt evidencing the storage of grain under
the provisions of this act and any rules or regula-
tions promulgated thereunder .
The word " owner " shall refer to and include any
person or persons , ( whether individuals , copartner-
ships , or corporations ) who shall either personally
or as trustee have title to or the right of possession
of any grain stored under the provisions of this act.
The word " grain in storage " shall refer to any
grain stored under the provisions of this act .
Where the word " owner " is used in this act , it
shall be construed to be used in the same connection
as the word " warehouseman " is used in the existing
or hereafter passed warehouse act .
Where the word " certificate " is used in this act,

1126 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
it shall be construed to be used in the same connec-
tion at the word " receipt " is used in the existing
or hereafter passed warehouse act .
3. The Board of Agriculture shall have gen-
eral supervision of the administration of the provi-
sions of this act . It shall :
1. Make and promulgate such rules and regula-
tions , not inconsistent herewith , as shall be neces-
sary or desirable effectually to carry out the pur-
poses hereof.
2. Make such reasonable regulations with respect
to the construction and maintenance of granaries ,
cribs, bins , or other receptacles as may be necessary
to protect the grain to be stored therein under the
provisions of this act .
3. Prepare and have printed under the same con-
ditions as other state printing the necessary blanks ,
forms , and other printed matter shall make such
charges to persons desiring such printed matter as
shall meet the cost of production thereof .
§ 4. The Board of Agriculture shall receive for
services rendered under the provisions of this act ,
three ( 3 ) dollars for each license issued .
§ 5. All moneys received by the Board of Agri-
culture from fees and other sources in connection
with the administration of the provisions of this act
shall be paid into the state treasury and may be
drawn upon by him for the purposes thereof , subject
to the provisions of the law applicable to disburse-
ments by the Board of Agriculture .
§ 6. A local supervisory board consisting of not
less than three nor more than seven members shall
be appointed by the Board of Agriculture in any
county upon the application of one or more citizens
as hereinafter provided for the purpose of super-
vising grain in storage and the issuing of certificates
against such grain , and generally and under the di-

CHAPTER 38 1127
rection of the Board of Agriculture for carrying out
the purposes and enforcing the provisions of this
act .
§ 7. Any person may make application to the
Board of Agriculture for the appointment of a board
in and for the county in which he resides or the
Board of Agriculture may make such appointments
upon its own initiative . When any such application
is made the Board of Agriculture shall as soon as
practicable investigate the situation and determine
upon the advisability or otherwise of making the
appointment requested .
§ 8. Upon the appointment and qualification of
the members of such board the Board of Agriculture
shall immediately issue a license to it , and prescribe
the duties of its officers and the records they shall
keep . Each license shall be numbered and specify
the territory which shall be under the jurisdiction
of the board and within which certificates may be
issued , and such certificates shall bear the names
and the license number of the board . It shall also
have printed thereon such other directions , rules ,
and regulations as the Board of Agriculture shall
make or promulgate and deem necessary to set forth
upon such license .
§ 9. A suitable name and number shall be given
to such board by the Board of Agriculture .
10. The members of such boards shall at the
time of their appointment be producers of agricul-
tural products , preferably grain , in the state and
residents in the county thereof .
§ 11. Members of such boards shall qualify by
taking the oath similar to that required of public
officials .
§ 12. They shall continue in office until their suc-
cessors are appointed by the Board of Agriculture .
In the event of vacancies arising by reason of the

1128 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
resignation or upon removal from the district or
death of any member or members , such vacancies
shall be filled in manner and form as in the case
of original appointments .
§ 13. Each board shall elect one of its own mem-
bers as its secretary -treasurer and shall also elect a
president and vice president from its own member-
ship and their duties shall be those of such officers
in similar organizations .
§ 14. Each secretary -treasurer shall furnish a
corporate surety bond for the faithful performance
of his duties in such amount as shall be determined
by the Board of Agriculture , but in no event shall
such bond be in an amount less than one thousand
( 1,000 ) dollars . Such bonds shall in every case be
subject to the approval of the Board of Agriculture
and be deposited with it . The premium thereon shall
be payable out of any funds in the hands of the
board.
15. For the purposes of defraying the expenses
of supervision , the owner shall pay to the board at
the time of sealing, an amount which shall not ex-
ceed one cent per bushel of grain inspected and
sealed by the sealer . In no case shall the cost to the
owner of the grain , housed in a single warehouse ,
be less than one dollar nor more than twenty dol-
lars . Out of the fund thus created the compensa-
tion of the sealer , as fixed by the board , subject to
the approval of the Board of Agriculture and all
other expenses shall be paid .
§ 16. No compensation shall be paid to members
of the board except by the express authorization and
approval of the Board of Agriculture , and then only
in case such payments may be made without over-
drawing upon or unduly depleting the funds in the
hands of the board .
§ 17. Surplus funds remaining in the treasury

CHAPTER 38 1129
of the local warehouse board October first of each
year shall be refunded to the owners as the board
may direct providing however that the procedure
and time and amount of such refund is approved by
the Board of Agriculture and providing that where
the amount paid by the owner was insufficient to
meet the costs incurred in sealing , no refund shall
be made to such owners .
18. The board shall submit to the Board of
Agriculture the name of some person or persons ,
none of whom shall be members of said board , who
shall , subject to the approval of the Board of Agri-
culture , act as the local sealer or sealers , and every
such sealer shall have the same authority with re-
spect to the provisions of this act and the rules and
regulations promulgated thereunder , and the en-
forcement thereof, as any officer of the peace .
19. Each sealer shall furnish a corporate sure-
ty bond for the faithful performance of his duties
in such amount as shall be determined by the Board
of Agriculture , but in no event shall such bond be
in an amount less than one thousand ( 1,000 ) dollars .
Such bonds shall in every case be subject to the ap-
proval of the Board of Agriculture and be deposited
with it, the premium thereon shall be payable out of
any funds in the hands of the board . Such sealer
shall also qualify by taking an oath similar to that
required of public officials .
§ 20. It shall be the duty of the sealer under the
direction of the Board of Agriculture to :
1. Supervise the storage of grain .
2. Ascertain the amount stored by each owner
who shall desire to avail himself of the provisions
of this act .
3. Determine , so far as possible , the grade and
quality thereof .
4. Ascertain , prior to the issuance of any cer-

1130 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tificate and/or certificate of re-inspection that the
granary , crib , bin or other receptacle in which the
grain is stored is satisfactory for the storage of such
grain , and that such receptacle conforms to the reg-
ulations applicable thereto promulgated by the
Board of Agriculture .
He shall , before delivering certificate and/or cer-
tificate of re -inspection to the owner, ascertain that
there are no other certificates outstanding upon the
grain and shall seal the granary , crib , bin or other
receptacle in which the grain is stored in the manner
hereinafter provided , and thereafter make periodic
inspections of the granaries , cribs , bins or other re-
ceptacles so sealed at such times and in such manner
as the Board of Agriculture may determine , but in
no event less frequently than ninety day intervals ,
rendering to the Board of Agriculture with reference
to each such subsequent inspection and to the owner
when requested report or affidavit , in such form as
may be required , in regard to the amount and con-
dition of the grain under seal and the condition of
the structure within which it is stored .
The sealer shall at the request of any borrower ,
issue a certificate of re -inspection on forms pre-
scribed by the Board of Agriculture for the purpose
of enabling said borrower to obtain the renewal or
extension of an outstanding obligation secured by
the pledge of a farm warehouse certificate . The
fees for such re - inspection shall be determined and
collected in the same manner as hereinbefore pro-
vided for original sealing fees .
§ 21. The sealer shall have authority at all
times to enter upon any premises for the purpose
of inspecting grain in storage or the granary , crib,
bin or other receptacle in which it shall have been
stored and the acceptance of a certificate by the
owner shall be deemed consent to entry and inspec-

CHAPTER 38 1131
tion by the sealer or any person duly authorized
thereunto by the Board of Agriculture .
§ 22. In the exercise of his powers and functions
as an officer of the peace in connection with the pro-
visions of this act , the sealer shall be entitled to
the same fees as are provided by law for the per-
formance of similar duties , but not to exceed three
dollars per day or fraction thereof for each full
day's work . All fees and compensation of the sealer
shall be subject to the approval of the board .
§ 23. Seals , locks or other fastenings employed
shall be in accordance with specifications furnished
by the Board of Agriculture .
§ 24. Any person unlawfully removing , breaking
or in any manner interfering or tampering with any
seal , lock or other fastening placed upon any gran.
ary , crib , bin or other receptacle for grain under the
provisions of this act, except when such removal
shall be rendered imperative to prevent the damage ,
loss , or destruction of grain stored therein , shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor , and shall be punished by a
fine of not less than one hundred dollars nor more
than five hundred dollars or by imprisonment in the
county jail for not more than six months or by both
such fine and imprisonment.
§ 25. An owner or any officer , agent or servant
of an owner , who delivers grain out of the posses-
sion of such owner , knowing that a negotiable cer-
tificate , the negotiating of which would transfer the
right to the possession of such grain , is outstanding
and uncanceled , without obtaining the possession
of such certificate at or before the time of such de-
livery , shall , except when ordered by the court , as
hereinbefore provided , be found guilty of a misde-
meanor , and on conviction shall be punished for each
offense by imprisonment in the county jail not ex-
ceeding one year, or by a fine not exceeding one

1132 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
thousand dollars or by both such imprisonment and
fine .
§ 26. Any owner who shall, after the issuance of
a certificate for grain in storage , take , sell , mort-
gage , pledge , hypothecate or otherwise incumber or
attempt to take , sell , mortgage , pledge or otherwise
incumber the said grain or who shall take or remove
it from the receptacle where standing , shall be guilty
of a misdemeanor and upon conviction thereof shall
be subject to a fine of not less than one hundred
dollars nor more than one thousand dollars or be
imprisoned in the county jail for not more than one
year or be punished by both such fine and imprison-
ment .
$ 27 . The provisions of the laws of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky now existing or hereafter
passed , relative to the negotiation , transfer , sale or
indorsement of warehouse receipts shall , so far as
applicable , apply to the negotiation , transfer , sale
or indorsement of the certificates provided for
herein .
§ 28. Certificates (for farm stored grain ) shall
be upon forms approved by the Board of Agricul-
ture and every such certificate must embody within
its written or printed terms :
1. The name and license number of the board
under which such certificates are issued ,
2. The consecutive number of the certificate .
3. The date of issue of the certificate .
4. A particular description of the granary , bin ,
crib or other receptacle in which the grain is stored ,
and of the premises upon which it is located .
5. A description of the grain .
6. The name of the owner or owners , whether
ownership is sole , joint, or in trust and the condi-
tions of such ownership and in the case of tenants
the date of termination of the lease .

CHAPTER 38 1133
7. A statement of any loans or other indebtedness
made to or owing by the owner which in any man-
ner constitutes a lien , whether statutory or con-
tractual , including both mortgage and landlord's
lien, upon the grain , which statement shall be signed
by the owner or his agent .
8. A form of waiver of liens which may be signed
by the lienholder .
9. A statement whether the goods received will
be delivered to bearer , to a specified person or to a
specified person or his order and at what place it
will be delivered .
10. A facsimile signature of the Board of Agri-
culture by its proper officer and the countersignature
of the sealer .
11. If the owner is married , a waiver by the
spouse of any claim of exemption and a consent to
the instrument .
12. Statement that no other certificates are out-
standing on the grain represented thereby .
§ 29. The sealer shall issue to the owner one or
more certificates as herein provided , but the aggre-
gate amount of the grain represented by such cer-
tificate or certificates shall in no event exceed the
amount of grain stored and sealed by the sealer and
each certificate shall cover a separate granary , crib
or bin.
$ 30 . No term or condition shall be inserted in
any certificate , whether negotiable or otherwise ,
which shall in any manner purport to relieve the
owner from exercising that degree of care in the
safekeeping of the grain in storage which a reason-
ably prudent man would exercise with regard to
similar property of his own .
§ 31. An owner , the agent , or servant of an own-
er, or any member of any board or any sealer , who
fraudulently issues or aids in fraudulently issuing

1134 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
a certificate for grain , knowing that it contains any
false statement , shall be guilty of a misdemeanor
and upon conviction shall be punished for each of-
fense by imprisonment in the county jail not ex-
ceeding one year , or by a fine not exceeding one
thousand dollars , or by both .
§ 32. An owner , or any officer , agent or servant
of any owner, who issues or aids in issuing a dupli-
cate or additional negotiable certificate for grain ,
knowing that a former negotiable certificate for the
same grain , or any part of it , is outstanding and
uncanceled , without plainly placing upon the face
thereof the word " duplicate , " except in the case of
a lost or destroyed certificate after approval thereof
by the board and the Board of Agriculture , shall be
guilty of a felony and upon conviction shall be pun-
ished for each offense by imprisonment in the peni-
tentiary not exceeding two years , or by a fine not
exceeding one thousand dollars , or by both such im-
prisonment and fine .
$ 33 . The sealer shall file with the secretary of
the board a duplicate of all certificates delivered
by him, and the secretary shall keep an accurate rec-
ord thereof in a book provided by the Board of Agri-
culture for the purpose . Such duplicates shall have
plainly printed upon the face thereof, " board dupli-
cate, no value."
$ 34. A certificate in which it is stated that the
grain stored shall be released or delivered to the
owner or to any other specified person , is a non-
negotiable certificate .
§ 35. A non -negotiable certificate shall have
plainly printed or written upon its face , " non -nego-
tiable " or " not negotiable ” .
36. A certificate in which it is stated that the
grain stored will be delivered to the bearer , or to
the order of any person named in such certificate ,

CHAPTER 38 1135
•
is a negotiable certificate . No provision shall be in-
serted in a negotiable certificate that is non -nego-
tiable . Such provisions , if inserted , shall be void .
§ 37. Before or at the time the owner negotiates
the original warehouse certificate he shall file or
cause to be filed a duplicate copy of said certificate
in the office of the county court clerk of the county
in which the grain is located , which duplicate shall
remain in the custody of the county court clerk ex-
cept as hereinafter provided .
Said owner shall then deliver to the assignee said
original certificate bearing the stamp of the county
court clerk, showing the date , time and number of
filing the duplicate thereof .
§ 38. When a duplicate is filed in the office of
the county court clerk , he shall index the same in
the chattel mortgage index or other suitable index
book showing the date of the certificate , the num-
ber thereof, to whom issued , kind , quantity and loca-
tion of the grain and stamp the original thereof
showing the date , time and number of filing . He
shall collect ten ( 10 ) cents for each certificate in-
dexed . The filing and indexing of such certificate
shall impart the same notice as the filing and in-
dexing of a chattel mortgage .
§ 39. When the owner or holder of a certificate
makes written assignment thereof after said instru-
ment is filed , the county court clerk shall on request
of the assignee enter a copy of such assignment upon
the duplicate in his office , and enter upon his index
book the date of the assignment and the names of
the assignor and the assignee . He shall collect ten
( 10 ) cents for such assignment entered .
§ 40. The owner may secure the cancellation of
a certificate by delivering the original to the Board
of Agriculture or secretary of the local board ,
through which it was issued with the request that it

1136 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
be canceled . The Board of Agriculture or secretary
of the local board shall stamp the original " can-
celed , " with the date of such cancellation and retain
same . Upon notice in writing from the Board of
Agriculture or the secretary of the local board
through which the certificate was issued that it has
been canceled , the county court clerk shall release
the duplicate filed of record without charge .
§ 41. The owner shall, in the absence of some
lawful excuse provided by this act , deliver the grain
stored upon demand made by the holder of a certifi-
cate for the grain or for such part thereof as is
represented by the certificate if such demand is ac-
companied by :
1. A showing that all such liens as may appear
upon the certificate and which shall subsist upon the
date of the demand have been waived or satisfied .
2. An offer to surrender the certificate if nego-
tiable , with such indorsements as would be neces-
sary for the negotiation of certificate .
3. A readiness and willingness to sign, when the
goods are delivered , an acknowledgment that they
have been delivered , if such a signature is requested
by the owner .
42. In case the owner refuses or fails to deliver
the goods in compliance with a demand by the holder
of a certificate so accompanied , the burden shall be
upon the owner to establish the existence of a lawful
excuse for such refusal .
§ 43. The privileges of this act shall be open to
all owners upon the same conditions . Any owner
desiring to place his grain in storage and have a
certificate or certificates issued against it shall make
application therefor to the board.
§ 44. Any owner aggrieved by any ruling or de-
cision of the board may appeal to the Board of
Agriculture whose decision shall be final .

CHAPTER 38 1137
§ 45. If any person shall feel aggrieved by any
action of the board or of the sealer or any other
official , he may submit his complaint in writing to
the Board of Agriculture and the Board of Agricul-
ture shall , as soon thereafter as possible , set the
matter down for hearing before the Board of Agri-
culture , at such a place as shall be desirable and
proper , having regard to the character of the con-
troversy and the locality of the grain and residence
of the parties involved . Likewise , the board may
present to the Board of Agriculture any proper com
plaint against any owner and the procedure shall be
as nearly as practicable the same as that in the case
of charges filed by owners .
46. The Board of Agriculture shall, upon final
hearing , make and enter such orders as it shall deem
proper for the correction of improper practices , and
may suspend the license of the board offending un-
til such orders are obeyed . But such suspension
shall in no manner relieve the board or owners of
any liability previously incurred under the provi-
sions of this act .
§ 47. The costs and expenses of such hearings
shall be defrayed by the parties thereto , and shall
be apportioned by the Board of Agriculture in such
a manner as it shall deem just and equitable .
§ 48. This act shall be liberally construed to ef-
fectuate the purpose thereof .
§ 49. If any provision of this act , or the applica-
tion of such provision to any person , body or cir-
cumstance , shall be held invalid the remainder of
this act , or the application of such provisions to per-
sons , bodies or circumstances , other than those as
to which it is held invalid , shall not be affected
thereby .
§ 50. All acts and parts of acts and all statutory
provisions and all laws and parts of laws in con-

1138 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
flict with the provisions of this act or any part of
this act are hereby expressly repealed .
§ 51. Whereas , farmers and producers of grain
in other states have the benefit of similar legisla-
tion and whereas , under certain acts of Congress
governmental agencies are empowered to make ad-
vances or loans against warehouse certificates is-
sued regarding farm stored grain , and whereas ,
Kentucky farmers should be entitled to the same
advantages as farmers of other states , an emer-
gency is hereby declared to exist and this act shall
become a law and be effective from and after its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 39 .
AN ACT to make uniform the law of warehouse receipts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1
PART I
THE ISSUE OF WAREHOUSE RECEIPTS .
Persons Who May Issue Receipts . Ware-
house receipts may be issued by any warehouseman .
$ 2 Form of Receipts. Essential Terms .
Warehouse receipts need not be in any particular
form , but every such receipt must embody within its
written or printed terms-
(a) The location of the warehouse where the
goods are stored ,
(b) The date of issue of the receipt ,
(c) The consecutive number of the receipt ,
(d ) A statement whether the goods received will
J

CHAPTER 39 1139
be delivered to the bearer , to a specified person , or
to a specified person or his order ,
(e) The rate of storage charges ,
(f) A description of the goods or of the packages
containing them ,
(g ) The signature of the warehouseman , which
may be made by his authorized agent ,
( h ) If the receipt is issued for goods of which
the warehouseman is owner , either solely or jointly
or in common with others , the fact of such owner-
ship, and
(i ) A statement of the amount of advances made
and of liabilities incurred for which the warehouse-
man claims a lien . If the precise amount of such
advances made or of such liabilities incurred is , at
the time of the issue of the receipt , unknown to the
warehouseman or to his agent who issues it , a state-
ment of the fact that advances have been made or
liabilities incurred and the purpose thereof is suf-
ficient .
A warehouseman shall be liable to any person in-
jured thereby , for all damage caused by the omission
from a negotiable receipt of any of the terms herein
required .
§ 3. Form of Receipts . What Terms May Be
Inserted . A warehouseman may insert in a receipt ,
issued by him , any others terms and conditions , pro-
vided that such terms and conditions shall not :
(a) Be contrary to the provisions of this act .
(b) In any wise impair his obligation to exercise
that degree of care in the safe -keeping of the goods
entrusted to him which a reasonably careful man
would exercise in regard to similar goods of his own .
§ 4. Definition of Non -Negotiable Receipt, A
receipt in which it is stated that the goods received

1140 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
will be delivered to the depositor, or to any other
specified person, is a non -negotiable receipt .
§ 5. Definition of Negotiable Receipt . A receipt
in which it is stated that the goods received
will be delivered to the bearer , or to the order of
any person named in such receipt is a negotiable
receipt .
No provision shall be inserted in a negotiable
receipt that is non -negotiable . Such provision , if
inserted, shall be void .
$ 6. Duplicate Receipts Must Be So Marked .
When more than one negotiable receipt is issued for
the same goods , the word " duplicate " shall be plain-
ly placed upon the face of every such receipt , except
the one first issued . A warehouseman shall be
liable for all damage caused by his failure so to do
to any one who purchased the subsequent receipt for
value supposing it to be an original , even though the
purchase be after the delivery of the goods by the
warehouseman to the holder of the original receipt.
§ 7. Failure to Mark " Not Negotiable " . A non-
negotiable receipt shall have plainly placed upon its
face by the warehouseman issuing it " non- negoti-
able ", or " not negotiable " . In case of the ware-
houseman's failure so to do , a holder of the receipt
who purchased it for value supposing it to be nego-
tiable , may , at his option , treat such receipt as im-
posing upon the warehouseman the same liabilities
he would have incurred had the receipt been nego-
tiable .
This section shall not apply , however , to letters ,
memoranda , or written acknowledgments of an in-
formal character .

CHAPTER 39 1141
PART II
OBLIGATIONS AND RIGHTS OF WAREHOUSEMEN UPON
THEIR RECEIPTS .
$ 8. Obligation of Warehouseman to Deliver . A
warehouseman , in the absence of some lawful excuse
provided by this act , is bound to deliver the goods
upon a demand made either by the holder of a receipt
for the goods or by the depositor , if such demand is
accompanied with :
(a ) An offer to satisfy the warehouseman's lien ,
(b) An offer to surrender the receipt if negoti-
able , with such indorsements as would be necessary
for the negotiation of the receipt , and
(c) A readiness and willingness to sign, when
the goods are delivered , an acknowledgment that
they have been delivered , if such signature is re-
quested by the warehouseman .
In case the warehouseman refuses or fails to
deliver the goods in compliance with a demand by
the holder or depositor so accompanied , the burden
shall be upon the warehouseman to establish the
existence of a lawful excuse for such refusal .
§ 9. Justification of Warehouseman in Delivery .
A warehouseman is justified in delivering the goods ,
subject to the provisions of the three following sec-
tions , to one who is :
(a ) The person lawfully entitled to the posses-
sion of the goods , or his agent ,
(b) A person who is either himself entitled to
delivery by the terms of a non -negotiable receipt
issued for the goods , or who has written authority
from the person so entitled either indorsed upon the
receipt or written upon another paper , or
(c) A person in possession of a negotiable re-
ceipt by the terms of which the goods are deliverable
to him or order or to bearer , or which has been

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
1142
endorsed to him or in blank by the person to whom
delivery was promised by the terms of the receipt
or by his mediate or immediate indorsee.
§ 10. Warehouseman's
Liability for Misdelivery .
Where a warehouseman
delivers the goods to one
who is not in fact lawfully entitled to the possession
of them , the warehouseman
shall be liable as for con-
version to all having a right of property or posses-
sion in the goods if he delivered the goods otherwise
than as authorized
by subdivisions
(b) and (c ) of
the preceding section and though he delivered the
goods as authorized
by said subdivisions
he shall be
so liable, if prior to such delivery he had either :
Been requested , by or on behalf of the per-
(a)
son lawfully entitled to a right of property or pos-
session
in the goods , not to make such delivery , or
(b) Had information that the delivery about to
be made was to one not lawfully entitled to the pos-
session of the goods
§ 11. Negotiable Receipts Must be Canceled
When Goods Delivered . Except as provided in Sec-
tion
36, where a warehouseman
delivers goods for
which
he had issued a negotiable
receipt , the negotia-
tion ofwhich would transfer the right to the posses-
sion ofthe goods , and fails to take up and cancel the
receipt
,he shall be liable to any one who purchases
for
value in good faith such receipt, for failure to
deliver
the goods to him, whether such purchaser
acquired
title to the receipt before or after the
delivery
of the goods bythe warehouseman
.
12. Negotiable
Receipts
Must be Canceled or
Marked
When Part of Goods Delivered
. Except as
provided
in Section 36, where a warehouseman
de-
vers
part ofthe goods for which he had issued a
egotiable
receipt and fails either to take up and
Cancel
such
receipt , or to place plainly upon it a

CHAPTER 39 1143
statement of what goods or packages have been
delivered he shall be liable , to any one who pur-
chases for value in good faith such receipt , for
failure to deliver all the goods specified in the re-
ceipt , whether such purchaser acquired title to the
receipt before or after the delivery of any portion
of the goods by the warehouseman .
§ 13. Altered Receipts . The alteration of a
receipt shall not excuse the warehouseman who is-
sued it from any liability if such alteration was :
( a ) Immaterial ,
(b) Authorized , or
(c ) Made without fraudulent intent .
If the alteration was authorized , the warehouse-
man shall be liable according to the terms of the
receipt as altered . If the alteration was unauthor-
ized , but made without fraudulent intent , the ware-
houseman shall be liable according to the terms of
the receipt , as they were before alteration .
Material and fraudulent alteration of a receipt
shall not excuse the warehouseman who issued it
from liability to deliver , according to the terms of
the receipt as originally issued , the goods for which
it was issued , but shall excuse him from any other
liability to the person who made the alteration and
to any person who took with notice of the alteration .
Any purchaser of the receipt for value without no-
tice of the alteration shall acquire the same rights
against the warehouseman which such purchaser
would have acquired if the receipt had not been
altered at the time of the purchase .
Where a14. Lost or Destroyed Receipts .
negotiable receipt has been lost or destroyed , a court
of competent jurisdiction may order the delivery of
the goods upon satisfactory proof of such loss or
destruction and upon the giving of a bond with suffi-

1144 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cient sureties to be approved by the court to protect
the warehouseman from any liability or expense ,
which he or any person injured by such delivery may
incur by reason of the original receipt remaining
outstanding. The court may also in its discretion
order the payment of a warehouseman's reasonable
costs and counsel fees .
The delivery of the goods under an order of the
court as provided in this section , shall not relieve
the warehouseman from liability to a person to whom
the negotiable receipt has been or shall be negotiated
for value without notice of the proceedings or of the
delivery of the goods .
§ 15. Effect of Duplicate Receipts . A receipt
upon the face of which the word " duplicate " is
plainly placed is a representation and warranty by
the warehouseman that such receipt is an accurate
copy of an original receipt properly issued and un-
canceled at the date of the issue of the duplicate , but
shall impose upon him no other liability .
§ 16. Warehouseman Cannot Set Up Title in
Himself. No title or right to the possession of the
goods , on the part of the warehouseman , unless such
title or right is derived directly or indirectly from
a transfer made by the depositor at the time of or
subsequent to the deposit for storage , or from the
warehouseman's lien , shall excuse the warehouseman
from liability for refusing to deliver the goods ac-
cording to the terms of the receipt .
$ 17. Interpleader of Adverse Claimants . If
more than one person claims the title or possession
of the goods , the warehouseman may , either as a
defense to an action brought against him for non-
delivery of the goods , or as an original suit , which-
ever is appropriate , require all known claimants to
interplead .

CHAPTER 39 1145
$ 18. Warehouseman has Reasonable Time to
Determine Validity of Claims . If some one other
than the depositor or person claiming under him has
a claim to the title or possession of the goods , and
the warehouseman has information of such claim ,
the warehouseman shall be excused from liability
for refusing to deliver the goods , either to the deposi-
tor or person claiming under him or to the adverse
claimant , until the warehouseman has had a reason-
able time to ascertain the validity of the adverse
claim or to bring legal proceedings to compel all
claimants to interplead .
§ 19. Adverse Title is no Defense Except as
Above Provided . Except as provided in the two
preceding sections and in sections 9 and 36 , no right
or title of a third person shall be a defense to an
action brought by the depositor or person claiming
under him against the warehouseman for failure to
deliver the goods according to the terms of the
receipt.
§ 20. Liability for Non -Existence or Misdescrip-
tion of Goods . A warehouseman shall be liable to
the holder of a receipt , issued by him or on his behalf
by an agent or employee the scope of whose actual
or apparent authority includes the issuing of ware-
house receipts , for damages caused by the non-
existence of the goods or by the failure of the goods
to correspond with the description thereof in the
receipt at the time of its issue . If, however , the
goods are described in a receipt merely by a state-
ment of marks or labels upon them , or upon pack-
ages containing them, or by a statement that the
goods are said to be goods of a certain kind , or that
the packages containing the goods are said to con-
tain goods of a certain kind , or by words of like
purport , such statements , if true , shall not make

1146 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
liable the warehouseman issuing the receipt , al-
though the goods are not of the kind which the marks
or labels upon them indicate , or of the kind they
were said to be by the depositor .
§ 21. Liability for Care of Goods . A ware-
houseman shall be liable for any loss or injury to the
goods caused by his failure to exercise such care in
regard to them as a reasonably careful owner of
similar goods would exercise , but he shall not be
liable , in the absence of an agreement to the con-
trary, for any loss or injury to the goods which could
not have been avoided by the exercise of such care .
$ 22. Goods Must be Kept Separate . Except as
provided in the following section , a warehouseman
shall keep the goods so far separate from goods of
other depositories , and from other goods of the same
depositor for which a separate receipt has been is-
sued , as to permit at all times the identification and
re -delivery of the goods deposited .
§ 23. Fungible Goods May be Commingled , if
Warehouseman Authorized . If authorized by agree-
ment or by custom , a warehouseman may mingle
fungible goods with other goods of the same kind
and grade . In such case the various depositors of
the mingled goods shall own the entire mass in com-
mon and each depositor shall be entitled to such por-
tion thereof as the amount deposited by him bears to
the whole
$ 24. Liability of Warehouseman to Depositors
of Commingled Goods . The warehouseman shall
be severally liable to each depositor for the care and
re -delivery of his share of such mass to the same
extent and under the same circumstances as if the
goods had been kept separate .
§ 25. Attachment of Levy Upon Goods for
Which Negotiable Receipts has Been Issued . If

CHAPTER 39 1147
goods are delivered to a warehouseman by the owner
or by a person whose act in conveying the title to
them to a purchaser in good faith for value would
bind the owner , and a negotiable receipt is issued for
them , they can not thereafter , while in the possession
of the warehouseman , be attached by garnishment or
otherwise , or be levied upon under an execution ,
unless the receipt be first surrendered to the ware-
houseman , or its negotiation enjoined . The ware-
houseman shall in no case be compelled to deliver up
the actual possession of the goods until the receipt
is surrendered to him or impounded by the court .
§ 26. Creditors ' Remedies to Reach Negotiable
Receipts. A creditor whose debtor is the owner of
a negotiable receipt shall be entitled to such aid from
courts of appropriate jurisdiction , by injunction and
otherwise , in attaching such receipts or in satisfying
the claim by means thereof as is allowed at law or
in equity, in regard to property which cannot readily
be attached or levied upon by ordinary legal process .
27. What Claims are Included in the Ware-
houseman's Lein . Subject to the provisions of Sec-
tion 30 , a warehouseman shall have a lein on goods
deposited or on the proceeds thereof in his hands ,
for all lawful charges for storage and preservation
of the goods ; also for all lawful claims for money
advanced , interest , insurance , transportation , labor ,
weighing, coopering and other charges and expenses
in relation to such goods ; also for all reasonable
charges and expenses for notice , and advertisements
of sale , and for sale of the goods where default has
been made in satisfying the warehouseman's lein .
§ 28. Against What Property the Lien May Be
Enforced . Subject to the provisions of Section 30
a warehouseman's lien may be enforced :
(a ) Against all goods , whenever deposited , be-

1148 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
longing to the person who is liable as debtor for the
claims in regard to which the lien is asserted , and
(b) Against all goods belonging to others which
have been deposited at any time by the person who
is liable as debtor for the claims in regard to which
the lien is asserted if such person had been so en-
trusted with the possession of the goods that a
pledge of the same by him at the time of the deposit
to one who took the goods in good faith for value
would have been valid .
§ 29. How the Lein May Be Lost. A warehouse-
man loses his lien upon goods :
(a) By surrendering possession thereof , or
(b ) By refusing to deliver the goods when a de-
mand is made with which he is bound to comply un-
der the provisions of this act .
30. Negotiable Receipt Must State Charges
For Which Lein Is Claimed . If a negotiable re-
ceipt is issued for goods , the warehouseman shall
have no lien thereon , except for charges for storage
of those goods subsequent to the date of the receipt ,
unless the receipt expressly enumerates other
charges for which a lien is claimed . In such case
there shall be a lien for the charges enumerated so
far as they are within the terms of Section 27 , al-
though the amount of the charges so enumerated
is not stated in the receipt .
31. Warehouseman Need Not Deliver Until
Lein Is Satisfied . A warehouseman having a lien
valid against the person demanding the goods may
refuse to deliver the goods to him until the lien is
satisfied .
$ 32. Warehouseman's Lein Does Not Preclude
Other Remedies . Whether a warehouseman has or
has not a lien upon the goods , he is entitled to all
remedies allowed by law to a creditor against his
debtor , for the collection from the depositor of all

CHAPTER 39 1149
charges and advances which the depositor has ex-
pressly or impliedly contracted with the warehouse-
man to pay .
§ 33. Satisfaction of Lein by Sale . A ware-
houseman's lien for a claim which has become due
may be satisfied as follows :
The warehouseman shall give a written notice to
the person on whose account the goods are held , and
to any other person known by the warehouseman to
claim an interest in the goods . Such notice shall
be given by delivery in person or by registered let-
ter addressed to the last known place of business
or abode of the person to be notified . The notice
shall contain :
(a ) An itemized statement of the warehouse-
man's claim , showing the sum due at the time of the
notice and the date or dates when it became due ,
(b) A brief description of the goods against
which the lien exists ,
( c) A demand that the amount of the claim as
stated in the notice , and of such further claim as
shall accrue , shall be paid on or before a day men-
tioned , not less than ten days from the delivery of
the notice if it is personally delivered , or from the
time when the notice should reach its destination , ac-
cording to the due course of post , if the notice is
sent by mail , and
(d) A statement that unless the claim is paid
within the time specified the goods will be advertised
for sale and sold by auction at a specified time and
place .
In accordance with the terms of a notice so given,
a sale of the goods by auction may be had to satisfy
any valid claim of the warehouseman for which he
has a lien on the goods . The sale shall be had in
the place where the lien was acquired , or, if such
place is manifestly unsuitable for the purpose , at the

1150 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
nearest suitable place . After the time for the pay-
ment of the claim specified in the notice to the de-
positor has elapsed , an advertisement of the sale ,
describing the goods to be sold , and stating the name
of the owner or person on whose account the goods
are held , and the time and place of the sale , shall be
