published once a week for two consecutive weeks
in a newspaper published in the place where such
sale is to be held . The sale shall not be held less
than fifteen days from the time of the first publica-
tion . If there is no newspaper published in such
place , the advertisement shall be posted at least ten
days before such sale in not less than six conspicu-
ous places therein .
From the proceeds of such sale the warehouse-
man shall satisfy his lien , including the reasonable
charges of notice , advertisement , and sale . The bal-
ance , if any , of such proceeds shall be held by the
warehouseman , and delivered on demand to the per-
son to whom he would have been bound to deliver
or justified in delivering the goods .
At any time before the goods are so sold any
person claiming a right of property or possession
therein may pay the warehouseman the amount nec-
essary to satisfy his lien and to pay the reasonable
expenses and liabilities incurred in serving notices
and advertising and preparing for the sale up to the
time of such payment . The warehouseman shall de-
liver the goods to the person making such payment
if he is a person entitled , under the provisions of
this act , to the possession of the goods on payment
charges thereon . Otherwise the warehouseman shall
retain possession of the goods according to the terms
of the original contract of deposit .
$ 34. Perishable and Hazardous Goods . If goods
are of a perishable nature , or by keeping will de-
teriorate greatly in value , or by their odor , leakage ,

CHAPTER 39 1151
inflammability , or explosive nature , will be liable to
injure other property , the warehouseman may give
such notice to the owner , or to the person in whose
name the goods are stored , as is reasonable and pos-
sible under the circumstances , to satisfy the lien
upon such goods , and to remove them from the
warehouse , and in the event of the failure of such
person to satisfy the lien and to remove the goods
within the time so specified , the warehouseman may
sell the goods at public or private sale without ad-
vertising . If the warehouseman after a reasonable
effort is unable to sell such goods , he may dispose
of them in any lawful manner , and shall incur no
liability by reason thereof .
The proceeds of any sale made under the terms of
this section shall be disposed of in the same way
as the proceeds of sales made under the terms of the
preceding section .
§ 35. Other Methods of Enforcing Liens . The
remedy for enforcing a lien herein provided does not
preclude any other remedies allowed by law for the
enforcement of a lien against personal property nor
bar the right to recover so much of the warehouse-
man's claim as shall not be paid by the proceeds of
the sale of the property .
$ 36. Effect of Sale . After goods have been law-
fully sold to satisfy a warehouseman's lien , or have
been lawfully sold or disposed of because of their
perishable or hazardous nature , the warehouseman
shall not thereafter be liable for failure to deliver
the goods to the depositor , or owner of the goods ,
or to a holder of the receipt given for the goods
when they were deposited , even if such receipt be
negotiable .

1152 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
PART III
NEGOTIATIONS AND TRANSFER OF RECEIPTS .
$ 37. Negotiation of Negotiable Receipts by De-
livery . A negotiable receipt may be negotiated by
delivery :
(a) Where , by the terms of the receipt , the ware-
houseman undertakes to deliver the goods to the
bearer , or
(b) Where , by the terms of the receipt the ware-
houseman undertakes to deliver the goods to the
order of a specified person , and such person or a
subsequent indorsee of the receipt has indorsed it
in blank or to bearer .
Where , by the terms of a negotiable receipt , the
goods are deliverable to bearer or where a negotia-
ble receipt has been indorsed in blank or to bearer ,
any holder may indorse the same to himself or to
any other specified person , and in such case the re-
ceipt shall thereafter be negotiated only by the in-
dorsement of such indorsee .
$ 38 Negotiation of Negotiable Receipts by In-
dorsement . A negotiable receipt may be negotiated
by the indorsement of the person to whose order the
goods are , by the terms of the receipt , deliverable .
Such indorsement may be in blank, to bearer or to
a specified person . If indorsed to a specified per-
son, it may be again negotiated by the indorsement
of such person in blank, to bearer or to another
specified person . Subsequent negotiation may be
made in like manner .
§ 39. Transfer of Receipts . A receipt which is
not in such form that it can be negotiated by delivery
may be transferred by the holder by delivery to a
purchaser or donee .
A non-negotiable receipt cannot be negotiated , and

CHAPTER 39 1153
the indorsement of such a receipt gives the trans-
feree no additional right .
§ 40. Who May Negotiate A Receipt . A nego-
tiable receipt may be negotiated by any person in
possession of the same , however such possession may
have been acquired , if , by the terms of the receipt ,
the warehouseman undertakes to deliver the goods
to the order of such person or if at the time of nego-
tiation the receipt is in such form that it may be
negotiated by delivery .
§ 41. Rights of Person to Whom A Receipt Has
Been Negotiated . A person to whom a negotiable
receipt has been duly negotiated acquires thereby :
(a ) Such title to the goods as the person nego-
tiating the receipt to him had or had ability to con-
vey to a purchaser in good faith for value , and also
such title to the goods as the depositor or person to
whose order the goods were to be delivered by the
terms of the receipts had or had ability to convey
to a purchaser in good faith for value , and
(b) The direct obligation of the warehouseman
to hold possession of the goods for him according to
the terms of the receipt as fully as if the warehouse-
man had contracted directly with him.
§ 42. Rights of Person to Whom A Receipt Has
Been Transferred . A person to whom a receipt has
been transferred but not negotiated , acquires there-
by , as against the transferor , the title of the goods ,
subject to the terms of any agreement with the
transferor .
If the receipt is non -negotiable such person also
acquires the right to notify the warehouseman of
the transfer to him of such receipt , and thereby to
acquire the direct obligation of the warehouseman
to hold possession of the goods for him according to
the terms of the receipt .
Prior to the notification of the warehouseman by
Acts- 37

1154 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
:
the transferor or transferee of a non -negotiable
receipt , the title of the transferee to the goods and
the right to acquire the obligation of the warehouse-
man may be defeated by the levy of an attachment
or execution upon the goods by a creditor of the
transferor , or by a notification to the warehouseman
by the transferor or a subsequent purchaser from
the transferor of a subsequent sale of the goods by
the transferor .
§ 43. Transfer of Negotiable Receipt Without
Indorsement . Where a negotiable receipt is trans-
ferred for value by delivery , and the indorsement of
the transferor is essential for negotiation , the trans-
feree acquires a right against the transferor to com-
pel him to indorse the receipt , unless a contrary
intention appears . The negotiation shall take effect
as of the time when the indorsement is actually
made .
$ 44. Warranties on Sale of Receipt . A person
who for value negotiates or transfers a receipt by
indorsement or delivery , including one who assigns
for value a claim secured by a receipt , unless a con-
trary intention appears , warrants :
(a) That the receipt is genuine ,
(b ) That he has a legal right to negotiate or
transfer it, 1
(c ) That he has knowledge of no fact which
would impair the validity or worth of the receipt,
and
(d ) That he has a right to transfer the title to
the goods and that the goods are merchantable or
fitfor
aparticular purpose whenever such war-
ranties would have been implied
,
ifthe contract
of
the parties had been
totransfer without
areceipt
.
the goods represented thereby
.
$45
. Indorser Not
AGuarantor
. The indorse-
ment of
areceipt shall not make the indorser liable

CHAPTER 39 1155
for any failure on the part of the warehouseman or
previous indorsers of the receipt to fulfill their re-
spective obligations .
§ 46. No Warranty Implied From Accepting
Payment of A Debt . A mortgagee , pledgee or hold-
er for security of a receipt who in good faith de-
mands or receives payment of the debt for which
such receipt is security , whether from a party to a
draft drawn for such debt or from any other per-
son , shall not by so doing be deemed to represent
or to warrant the genuineness of such receipt or the
quantity or quality of the goods therein described .
§ 47. When Negotiation Not Impaired By
Fraud , Mistake , Or Duress . The validity of the ne-
gotiation of a receipt is not impaired by the fact
that such negotiation was a breach of duty on the
part of the person making the negotiation , or by the
fact that the owner of the receipt was deprived of
the possession of the same by loss , theft , fraud , ac-
cident , mistake , duress , or conversion , if the person
to whom the receipt was negotiated or the person
to whom the receipt was subsequently negotiated ,
paid value therefor , in good faith , without notice of
the breach of duty, or loss , theft , fraud , accident ,
mistake , duress or conversion .
§ 48. Subsequent Negotiations . Where a person
having sold , mortgaged , or pledged goods which are
in a warehouse and for which a negotiable receipt
has been issued , or having sold , mortgaged , or
pledged the negotiable receipt representing such
goods , continues in possession of the negotiable re-
ceipt , the subsequent negotiation thereof by that
person under any sale , or other disposition thereof
to any person receiving the same in good faith , for
value and without notice of the previous sale , mort-
gage or pledge , shall have the same effect as if the

1156 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
first purchaser of the goods or receipt had expressly
authorized the subsequent negotiations .
§ 49. Negotiation Defeats Vendor's Lein . Where
a negotiable receipt has been issued for goods , no
seller's lien or right of stoppage in transitu shall
defeat the rights of any purchaser for value in good
faith to whom such receipt has been negotiated ,
whether such negotiation be prior or subsequent to
the notification to the warehouseman who issued such
receipt of the seller's claim to a lien or right of
stoppage in transitu . Nor shall the warehouseman
be obliged to deliver or justified in delivering the
goods to an unpaid seller unless the receipt is first
surrendered for cancellation .
PART IV
CRIMINAL OFFENSES .
§ 50. Issue of receipt For Goods Not Received .
A warehouseman , or any officer , agent , or servant
of a warehouseman , who issues or aids in issuing a
receipt knowing that the goods for which such re-
ceipt is issued have not been actually received by
such warehouseman , or are not under his actual
control at the time of issuing such receipt, shall be
guilty of a crime , and upon conviction shall be pun-
ished for each offense by imprisonment not exceed-
ing five years , or by a fine not exceeding five thou-
sand dollars , or by both
§ 51. Issue of Receipt Containing False State-
ment . A warehouseman , or any officer , agent or ser-
vant of a warehouseman , who fraudulently issues
or aids in fraudulently issuing a receipt for goods
knowing that it contains any false statement , shall
be guilty of a crime , and upon conviction shall be
punished for each offense by imprisonment not ex-
ceeding one year , or by a fine not exceeding one
thousand dollars , or by both .

CHAPTER 39 1157
§ 52. Issue of Duplicate Receipts Not So
Marked . A warehouseman , or any officer , agent , or
servant of a warehouseman , who issues or aids in
issuing a duplicate or additional negotiable receipt
for goods knowing that a former negotiable receipt
for the same goods or any part of them is outstand-
ing and uncanceled , without plainly placing upon
the face thereof the word " Duplicate " except in the
case of a lost or destroyed receipt after proceedings
as provided for in Section 14 , shall be guilty of a
crime , and upon conviction shall be punished for
each offence by imprisonment not exceeding five
years, or by a fine not exceeding five thousand dol-
lars , or by both .
§ 53. Issue For Warehouseman's Goods of Re-
ceipts Which Do Not State That Fact . Where there
are deposited with or held by a warehouseman
goods of which he is owner , either solely or jointly
or in common with others , such warehouseman , or
any of his officers , agents , or servants who , knowing
this ownership , issues or aids in issuing a negotia-
ble receipt for such goods which does not state such
ownership , shall be guilty of a crime , and upon con-
viction shall be punished for each offense by impris-
onment not exceeding one year , or by a fine not ex-
ceeding one thousand dollars , or by both .
$ 54. Delivery of Goods Without Obtaining Ne-
gotiable Receipt . A warehouseman , or any officer ,
agent , or servant of a warehouseman who delivers
goods out of the possession of such warehouseman ,
knowing that a negotiable receipt the negotiation of
which would transfer the right to the possession of
such goods is outstanding and uncanceled , without
obtaining the possession of such receipt at or before
the time of such delivery , shall, except in the cases
provided for in Sections 14 and 36 , be found guilty
of a crime , and upon conviction shall be punished

1158 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
for each offence by imprisonment not exceeding one
year , or by a fine not exceeding one thousand dollars ,
or by both .
§ 55. Negotiation of Receipt For Mortgaged
Goods . Any person who deposits goods to which he
has not title , or upon which there is a lien or mort-
gage , and who takes for such goods a negotiable
receipt which he afterwards negotiates for value
with intent to deceive and without disclosing his
want of title or the existence of the lien or mortgage
shall be guilty of a crime , and upon conviction shall
be punished for each offense by imprisonment not
exceeding one year , or by a fine not exceeding one
thousand dollars , or by both .
PART V
INTERPRETATION .
§ 56. Cases Not Provided For In Act . In any
case not provided for in this act , the rules of law
and equity , including the law merchant , and in par-
ticular the rules relating to the law of principal and
agent and to the effect of fraud , misrepresentation ,
duress or coercion , mistake , bankruptcy , or other
invalidating cause , shall govern.
§ 57. Interpretation Shall Give Effect To Pur-
pose of Uniformity . This act shall be so interpreted
and construed as to effectuate its general purpose
to make uniform the law of those states which
enast it.
§ 58. Definitions . ( 1 ) In this act, unless the con-
text or subject matter otherwise requires :
"Action " includes counter claim , set-off, and suit
in equity
"Delivery " means voluntary transfer of posses-
sion from one person to another .
" Fungible goods " means goods of which any unit

CHAPTER 39 1159
is, from its nature or by mercantile custom , treated
as the equivalent of any other unit .
" Goods " means chattels or merchandise in stor-
age , or which has been or is about to be stored .
" Holder " of a receipt means a person who has
both actual possession of such receipt and a right
of property therein .
"Order " means an order by indorsement on the
receipt .
" Owner " does not include mortgagee or pledgee .
"Person " includes a corporation or partnership
or two or more persons having a joint or common
interest.
To " purchase " includes to take as mortgagee or
as pledgee .
"Purchaser " includes mortgagee and pledgee .
' Receipt " means a warehouse receipt .
"Value " is any consideration sufficient to support
a simple contract . An antecedent or pre -existing
obligation , whether for money or not , constitutes
value where a receipt is taken either in satisfaction
thereof or as security therefor .
" Warehouseman " means a person lawfully en-
gaged in the business of storing goods for profit .
(2 ) A thing is done " in good faith " within the
meaning of this act , when it is in fact done honestly ,
whether it be done negligently or not .
$ 59 . Act Does Not Apply To Existing Receipts .
The provisions of this act do not apply to receipts.
made and delivered prior to the taking effect of this
act .
$ 60. Inconsistent Legislation Repealed . All
acts or parts of acts inconsistent with this act are
hereby repealed and Senate Bill No. 106 passed at

1160 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Regular Session of General Assembly 1938 is hereby
expressly repealed .
§ 61. Name of Act . This act may be cited as the
Uniform Warehouse Receipts Act.
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 40.
AN ACT authorizing independent school districts to annex
county subdistricts or parts thereof.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Any independent school district may annex and
unite as a part of the independent school district any
county subdistrict or subdistricts , or parts thereof,
lying adjacent to the independent school district ,
subject to the following provisions .
The board of education of the independent school
district and the county board of education shall de-
signate the territory proposed to be annexed , and
submit the question of annexing the territory to the
independent school district to a vote of the people
residing in the territory proposed to be annexed ,
and if said proposal shall be adopted by a vote of the
majority of the people residing therein , said terri-
tory shall become a part of the independent school
district .
Provided further that if the board of education of
the independent school district or the board of edu-
cation of the county shall fail or refuse to designate
said boundary, or call said election , then if twenty-
five (25 % ) per cent of the voters residing in said
territory proposed to be annexed shall petition the
board of education to call said election , same shall be
called within sixty days from the filing of said peti-
772214

CHAPTER 41 1161
tion , and if at said election there is a majority vote
in favor of said annexation , said territory shall be-
come a part of the independent school district .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 41 .
RESOLUTION Authorizing the estate of Morgan Hacker to file
suit against the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and the State
Highway Commission , or either .
WHEREAS , on the 1937 on the State Highway
No. 21 between McKee and Manchester , in Clay County a Road
Grader owned by the State of Kentucky and at the time being
used and and operated on said State Highway by the hands
and employees of the State Highway Commission in Maintain-
ing said Highway met and ran over Morgan Hacker and killed
him through its carelessness and negligence .
NOW , in order that said estate may have its " day in court " to
determine by judicial action the question of negligence , if any ,
causing said death aforesaid .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the estate of Morgan Hacker be and it is
hereby empowered , and authorized to file and prose-
cute appropriate actions , against the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission , or
either, for the purpose of determining the liability
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State
Highway Commission , or either , for such injury and
death , if any there be ; and in the event any judg-
ment in the action is recovered by said estate or same
compromised or settled , same shall be paid by the
Auditor of Public Accounts by warrant drawn on the
State Treasurer and paid out of the General Fund .
Either party to any suit may appeal from any judg-
ment which may be entered therein as in any other
civil suit and the case may be settled and adjusted
with the consent and approval of the Attorney Gen-

1162 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
eral of Kentucky in the same way as any other civil
case .
Said action may be brought in any county of Ken-
tucky having jurisdiction of the parties and subject
matter. The limit of liability shall be $5,000.00 .
Approved by the Governor : April 20 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 42 .
A RESOLUTION authorizing the Commissioner of Highways
with the consent of the Kentucky Real Estate Board and upon
approval of the Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
to convey an easement over the right of way and the
approaches to the Catlettsburg bridge between Catlettsburg ,
Kentucky , and Kenova , West Virginia , on the West Virginia
side , to the City of Kenova for the purpose of constructing a
flood wall and to further convey all of that portion of the
Catlettsburg bridge and approaches lying and being in West
Virginia to the said State of West Virginia , and further pro-
vide for the disposal of any unused unnecessary real estate
and improvements thereon and providing that the purchase
money shall be covered into the State Treasury and placed
to the credit of the sinking fund for the retirement of Com-
monwealth of Kentucky bridge revenue bonds project No. 14 .
WHEREAS , heretofore the Commonwealth of Kentucky has
acquired all of the right , title and interest in and to the bridge
spanning the Big Sandy River between the cities of Catletts-
burg , Kentucky , and Kenova , West Virginia , and
WHEREAS , the Commonwealth of Kentucky has issued its
bridge revenue bonds for the payment of the purchase price
of said bridge and has paid to the stockholders for all the
right , title and interest in and to said bridge the sum of
$885,000.00 , and ,
WHEREAS , all of the bonds are to be retired by the tolls col-
lected in the operation of said bridge , and
WHEREAS , under the Murphy Toll Bridge Act , the Department
of Highways bears all of the cost of maintenance and opera-
tion of said bridge out of the state revenues and no part of
the tolls is applied to the maintenance and operation of said
bridge , and
WHEREAS , more than one -half of the said bridge is located
in the State of West Virginia , and

CHAPTER 42 1163
WHEREAS , the State of West Virginia and the many sub -di-
visions of the state government of West Virginia have asserted
and will continue to assert tax claims against the Common-
wealth of Kentucky on the proportional part of said bridge or
that part which lies in the State of West Virginia , and
WHEREAS , under the laws of the State of West Virginia , the
Department of Highways of said state cannot contribute to
the maintenance of said bridge so long as all of the title to
the bridge property is in the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
and
WHEREAS , the City of Kenova has suffered from flood waters
from the Big Sandy River and in order to build a flood wall ,
it is necessary that the wall be built over the rights of way
and the bridge approaches on the West Virginia side , and
WHEREAS , the State of West Virginia and the City of Kenova
and the County of Wayne in West Virginia have agreed that
if the Commonwealth of Kentucky will grant an easement to
the City of Kenova over the rights of way and approaches to
said bridge on the West Virginia side and if the Common-
wealth of Kentucky will convey to the State of West Virginia ,
subject to the easement above mentioned to the City of
Kenova , all of that portion of the bridge lying and being in
West Virginia , the Department of Highways of the State of
West Virginia will assume the maintenance of the said bridge
or that portion thereof located in West Virginia and will
cease to assert tax claims against the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky on any part of said bridge and will maintain and keep
in repair the approaches and other things necessary to the
proper operation of the bridge , all of which conveyance and
maintenance will be subject to the trust indenture entered
into by and between the Commonwealth of Kentucky and
the trustee for the bond holders on project No. 14 , and
WHEREAS , there is certain other real estate on the West Vir
ginia side and on the Kentucky side of said bridge that is
not necessary or needed to properly operate the bridge , but
which said real estate and buildings thereon can be disposed
of without materially affecting or affecting at all the proper
operation of the bridge and that said real estate and improve-
ments thereon should be disposed of, Therefore ,
Be it now Resolved by the General Assembly of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Commissioner of Highways with the con-
sent of the Kentucky Real Estate Board and upon

1164 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the approval of the Governor of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , be and he is hereby authorized to con-
vey on behalf of the Commonwealth of Kentucky an
easement to the City of Kenova , West Virginia , over
the right of way and the approaches on the West
Virginia side of the said Catlettsburg bridge cross-
ing the Big Sandy River between Catlettsburg , Ken-
tucky, and Kenova , West Virginia , said easement
for the construction of a flood wall and it shall be
stipulated in the conveyance that the flood wall shall
in no way interfere with or injure or damage the
approaches or the bridge in any manner and the said
Commissioner of Highways shall be further author-
ized to convey all of that portion of the said Catletts-
burg bridge together with necessary approaches be-
tween Catlettsburg , Kentucky and Kenova , West
Virginia , and spanning the Big Sandy River , that is
within the territorial limits of West Virginia to the
State of West Virginia and to enter into a contract
with the Department of Highways of said State of

THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY

PROCLAMATION
TO THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY :
'WHEREAS , the existing methods and equipment , including
personnel for the operation of the various state institutions for
the treatment of mentally diseased persons is inadequate , and
the personnel is insufficient , and
WHEREAS , there is a large number of persons now confined
in said institutions without proper medical care , nurses , attend-
ants , medical supplies , and equipment , and
WHEREAS , unless proper medical care , attendants , nurses ,
medical supplies , and equipment are provided for the care of said
persons they will not receive proper treatment and their lives
and health are now endangered by reason of the existing condi-
tions ;
NOW , THEREFORE , in order to improve the existing
methods and equipment , including personnel for the operation
of the various state institutions for the treatment of mentally dis-
eased persons and to supply the proper medical care , attendants ,
nurses , medical supplies and equipment and make the necessary
appropriation therefor , and in order to meet the emergency now
existing in the various state institutions for the treatment of
mentally diseased persons , and in accordance with the power
vested in me by the Constitution of Kentucky to convene the
General Assembly into Extraordinary Session , as Governor of
Kentucky , I do issue this my proclamation convening the General
Assembly of Kentucky in Extraordinary Session at the seat of
government at Frankfort , Kentucky , at one o'clock p . m ., May 23 ,
1938 , for the sole purpose of considering the following subject ,
to wit :
1. To pass an act relating to the Division of Hospitals and
Mental Hygiene of the Department of Welfare ; prescribing its
duties and functions in regard to the disposition of medical and
psychiatric care and treatment of all state non compos mentis
and other mental cases , including feeble -minded , insane , epileptic ,
pauper idiots , drug addicts , and the medical and psychiatric super-
vision of the criminal insane and non compos mentis persons ;
providing for the appointment of the director of the Division of
Hospitals and Mental Hygiene , within the Department of Welfare ,
and his assistants , including superintendents , business managers ,

1174 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
physicians , nurses and other employees for the state institutions
for the care of non compos mentis persons ; providing for the
salaries of certain officers and employees ; providing the manner
and form of receiving and transferring inmates in such institu
tions ; prescribing qualifications of all officers and employees ;
making an appropriation for the operation of the division not in
excess of three hundred thousand dollars ( $300,000 ) for the fiscal
year 1938-39 , and not in excess of five hundred thousand dollars
($500,000 ) for the fiscal year 1939-40 ; and providing for the con-
trol and supervision of pauper idiots .
You will , therefore , in accordance with this proclamation
assemble in said Extraordinary Session at the seat of govern .
ment at Frankfort , Kentucky , on the day and date above written
to consider the subjects herein mentioned , and only such sub-
jeets .
Given under my hand at 3.10 o'clock , this the 20th day of
May, 1938 .
CHAS . D. ARNETT ,,
Secretary of State .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
Governor of Kentucky .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE
GENERAL ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY
OF FRANKFORT , KENTUCKY , ON MONDAY , MAY 23 , 1938 ,
AND ENDED ON SATURDAY , MAY 28 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 1
AN ACT relating to the Division of Hospitals and Mental
Hygiene of the Department of Welfare , providing for its func-
tions and duties and prescribing its duties and func
tions in regard to the disposition of medical and psychiatric
care and treatment of all non compos mentis and other mental
cases as set out in this Act , including feeble -minded , insane ,
epileptic , pauper idiots , drug addicts , and the medical and psy
chiatric supervision of the criminal insane and non compos
mentis persons and providing for the appointment of the Direc-
tor of the Division of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene , within
the Department of Welfare , and his assistants , and further
providing for the appointment of superintendents , business
managers , physicians , nurses and other employees for the
state institutions now existing for the care of non compos
mentis persons , or such other institutions as may be hereafter
established for that purpose , including institutions that may
be established for the care of drug addicts , chronic inebriates ,
psychopathic personalities , epileptics , insane persons and
feeble -minded persons , and supervision of medical and psy-
chiatric service in institutions , maintained wholly or in part
by the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the care of dependent
children which are now or may hereafter be under

1176 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the supervision of the Department of Welfare and further
providing for the salary of the Director of the Division of
Hospitals and Mental Hygiene and certain other officers and
employees in the Division of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene
and the institutions operated under Department of Welfare
general supervision , providing for the manner of appointment ,
employment , transfer and discharge of officers and employees ,
and further providing the manner and form of receiving and
transferring of inmates in such institutions , prescribing quali
fications of all officers and employees and making an appro-
priation for the operation of the Division , and further provid .
ing for the control and supervision of pauper idiots in private
homes and otherwise , and repealing all laws in conflict here-
with and providing for an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Definitions :
For the purposes of this Act , unless otherwise
required by the context :
(a ) State institutions shall mean institutions al-
ready established , or which may hereafter be estab-
lished, which receive fifty percent (50% ) or more of
their support from state funds .
(b ) Health officer shall mean any duly appointed
person charged with the duty of enforcing the health
laws of this Commonwealth or any political sub-
division thereof ,
( c) Penal and correctional institutions shall be
construed to include all existing state penitentiaries ,
reformatories , and schools of reform , and all other
institutions , of like nature and purpose , that may
be created in the future
( d ) Central Office shall mean the Director of the
Division of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene , Assistant
Director and all clerical help and other employees in
the office of the Director .

CHAPTER 1 1177
§ 2. Functions of the Division of Hospitals and
Mental Hygiene :
The Division of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene
under the direction of the Department of Welfare
shall be charged with the administration of all state
institutions for the care and treatment of the men-
tally diseased and feeble -minded persons . This Di-
vision shall also be responsible , in cooperation with
the State Department of Health , for the develop-
ment of a State -wide mental hygiene program look-
ing toward the prevention of mental disease and
post -institutional care of persons released from
state and/or private mental institutions . In addi-
tion , the Division shall be charged with the super-
vision of the Red Cross Hospital and Home for In-
curables in Louisville , as well as of privately owned
mental hospitals which are in receipt of State aid .
In addition , the Division shall be specifically charged
with the following responsibilities :
(a) Co -ordination of all existing facilities for
medical and psychatric care and treatment .
(b) Creation of other facilities for these pur-
poses as needed.
(c) Custody, care , and medical and psychiatric
treatment , with administrative proceedings incident
thereto , of inmates in all institutions for the insane ,
feeble -minded , drug addicts , chronic inebriates , or
any other persons who are now or who may here-
after be legally adjudged as of unsound mind or non
compos mentis .
(d ) General supervision of the conditions under
which the pauper idiot act shall be carried out with
the proviso that no person shall receive any pauper
idiot into his home without a permit from the Divi-
sion of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene .
(e ) Supervision of Medical and Psychiatric
service to all penal and correctional institutions .

1178 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(f) Supervision of medical and psychiatric
services in institutions , maintained wholly or in part
by the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the care of
dependent children which are now or may hereafter
be under the supervision of the Department of
Welfare .
$ 3. Qualifications of Director of Division of
Hospitals and Mental Hygiene :
The Director of the Division of Hospitals and
Mental Hygiene shall be a graduate physician of
good character and reputation , who has had at least
five years ' experience in the institutional care and
treatment of the insane or feeble -minded .
$ 4. Duties of Director :
The duties of the Director of the Division of Hos-
pitals and Mental Hygiene shall include general su-
pervision and direction of the activities of that Di-
vision , issuing and promulgating rules and regula-
tions for the administrative procedure of State in-
stitutions , and formulating rules , regulations and
standards for the employment of persons within the
several State institutions .
$ 5. Appointment of Assistant Director of the
Division of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene , and
Superintendents of Institutions :
The Director of the Division of Hospitals and
Mental Hygiene , with the approval of the Commis-
sioner of Welfare , is hereby authorized to select an
Assistant Director of the Division of Hospitals and
Mental Hygiene , and both the Director and Assistant
Director of the Division of Hospitals and Mental
Hygiene shall make their headquarters in Frank-
fort . Upon the recommendation of the Director of
the Division of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene , the
Commissioner of Welfare shall appoint the super-
intendents of the institutions which are under the
supervision of the Division of Hospitals and Mental

CHAPTER 1 1179
Hygiene , subject to the regulations and statutory
provisions governing the qualifications of such ap-
pointees .
§ 6. Appointment of director of the Division of
Hospitals and Mental Hygiene :
The Commissioner of Welfare with the approval
of the Governor shall , as soon as this Act becomes a
law , appoint a Director of the Division of Hospitals
and Mental Hygiene , and whenever a vacancy may
thereafter exist in the position of Director of the
Division of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene , the new
Director shall be appointed by the Commissioner of
Welfare with the approval of the Governor . His
appointment shall be made in the following man-
ner : The Commissioner of Welfare shall select and
appoint the Director of the Division of Hospitals
and Mental Hygiene from a list of three persons
possessing the necessary qualifications as provided
for under this Act , submitted to him by the Council
of the Kentucky State Medical Association . If in
the opinion of the Commissioner of Welfare no one
of the three persons submitted is acceptable for the
position , he may call upon the Council of the Ken-
tucky State Medical Association for another list of
three , from which list of six the appointment must
then be made .
§ 7. Appointment and Discharge of Officers and
Employees :
Subject to the Constitutional provisions limiting
the terms of appointments , neither the Director of
the Division of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene nor
the superintendent of any hospital or institution
shall be removed from office or discharged from his
or her position except for cause , after written
charges have been preferred against him or her by
the Commissioner of Welfare , which charges shall
be heard and tried by ( 1 ) the Governor , ( 2 ) one per-

1180 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
son appointed by the Council of the Kentucky State
Medical Association , and ( 3 ) the Attorney General .
Such superintendent or the Director of the Division
of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene shall not be dis-
missed except by a majority of those conducting the
hearing . Any other employee , either in the office
of the Director of the Division of Hospitals and Men-
tal Hygiene or in any of the institutions under the
general supervision of that Division , who has served
in his position continuously for a period of six
months , shall not be discharged or removed from his
position until such discharge has been approved by
the Director of the Division of Hospitals and Mental
Hygiene .
§ 8. Duties of Superintendents :
The superintendents of the institutions shall be
specifically charged with the medical and psychiatric
care and treatment of the inmates in said institu-
tions . It shall be the duty of the superintendents of
said institutions to select , appoint and employ all
employees under their supervision . Said employees
so selected must possess all qualifications required
of such employees by law or regulations adopted by
the Director of the Division of Hospitals and Men-
tal Hygiene , provided , however , that the superin-
tendents of said institutions shall not select , appoint ,
or employ any person until the employment thereof
shall have been approved by the Commissioner of
Welfare , in compliance with the Governmental Re-
organization Act of 1936 .
$ 9. Qualifications of Officers and Employees :
The following persons appointed to any office
created under this Act or any employees where
qualifications are required , shall, before such ap-
pointment by the appointing authorities , possess the
following qualifications :
(a ) The assistant director of the Division of

CHAPTER 1 1181
Hospitals and Mental Hygiene shall be a graduate
physician with special training in psychiatry , which
training must include at least one year's experience
in an institution for the care of the insane or feeble-
minded .
(b) The superintendent of any institution for
the insane shall be a qualified physician with at least
five years ' experience in the practice of his profes-
sion , at least three of which must have been in an
institution for the care of the insane .
( c ) The superintendent of any institution for
feeble -minded persons shall be a person with special
experience in the training and care of the feeble-
minded . He or she must have had at least two or
more years ' administrative experience in a qualified
institution for the care and treatment of this type of
patient .
(d ) The clinical director of institutions for the
care of the insane must be a physician who has had
previous psychiatric experience and training , with
at least one year's experience in an institution for
the insane .
(e ) The educational director for the institutions
for the care of feeble -minded persons must be a per-
son who is a college graduate , presenting evidence of
special training and experience in the education of
the feeble -minded or backward school children , and
who has had at least one year's experience in teach-
ing the feeble - minded or backward pupils .
(f) A director of occupational therapy must pre-
sent satisfactory evidence of training and experi-
ence in this type of work .
(g) A field liaison officer shall be a person who
has had training and experience in social welfare
work, and one who possesses a certificate showing
that he or she has been graduated from a school ac-

1182 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
credited by the American Association of Schools of
Social Workers .
(h) A supervisor of nurses must be a registered
trained nurse with supervisory experience .
(i ) . Where the qualifications of employees are not
specifically mentioned in this Act, they shall be pre-
scribed by general regulations promulgated by the
Director of the Division of Hospitals and Mental
Hygiene .
§ 10. Minimum Salary of Certain Officers and
Employees :
The Director of the Division of Hospitals and
Mental Hygiene shall receive a minimum annual sal-
ary.of five thousand ($5,000.00 ) dollars .
The Assistant Director of the Division of Hos-
pitals and Mental Hygiene shall receive a minimum
annual salary of thirty -six hundred ( $3,600.00 ) dol-
lars .
The superintendent of each hospital or institu-
tion, provided for in this Act , shall receive a mini-
mum annual salary of four thousand ($4,000.00 )
dollars , plus maintenance for self and family , such
maintenance to include quarters , subsistence , and
laundry .
The clinical director for each hospital for the care
and treatment of the insane shall receive a minimum
annual salary of three thousand ( $3,000.00 ) dollars
and maintenance for self and family , which mainte-
nance shall include quarters , subsistence , and
laundry .
The director of education for the institutions for
the care of the feeble -minded shall receive a mini-
mum annual salary of three thousand ( $3,000.00 )
dollars and maintenance for self and family , said
maintenance to include quarters , subsistence , and
laundry .
The supervisor of nurses for each hospital or in-

CHAPTER 1 1183
stitution shall receive a minimum annual salary of
fifteen hundred ( $ 1,500.00 ) dollars and maintenance
for self, said maintenance to include quarters , sub-
sistence , and laundry .
Senior physicians in institutions shall receive a
minimum annual salary of twenty -four hundred
($2,400.00 ) dollars .
Junior physicians in institutions shall receive a
minimum annual salary of eighteen hundred
($1,800.00 ) dollars .
Dentists in institutions shall receive a minimum
annual salary of twenty -four hundred ( $ 2,400.00 )
dollars .
The physicians and dentists above mentioned
shall , in addition to the salary set out , receive main-
tenance for self and family , said maintenance to in-
clude quarters , subsistence , and laundry .
The director of occupational therapy shall receive
a minimum annual salary of fifteen hundred
( $1,500.00 ) dollars , and maintenance for self , in-
cluding quarters , subsistence , and laundry .
The field liaison officer shall receive a minimum
salary of eighteen hundred ( $ 1,800.00 ) dollars , and
maintenance for self , said maintenance to include
quarters , subsistence , and laundry .
The business manager shall receive a minimum
annual salary of twenty -four hundred ( $2,400.00 )
dollars , and maintenance for self and family , said
maintenance to include quarters , subsistence , and
laundry .
All the above salaries shall be paid in monthly in-
stallments , provided , however , that the salary and
value of maintenance of any officer or employee pro-
vided for herein when added together shall in no
event exceed the sum of five thousand ( $5,000.00 )
dollars per annum .

1184 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 11. Duties of the Field Liaison Officer :
The duties of the field liaison officer shall be to
establish contacts between patients in hospitals and
their families , collect all information which may
have a bearing upon the proper treatment and care
of the patient while in the hospital ; and contact the
families and social agencies and county health of-
ficials in the community to which the patient may be
returned , for the purpose of promoting his ultimate
recovery and preventing the necessity for his return
to the institution after he has been paroled or dis-
charged therefrom .
The Director of the Division of Hospitals and
Mental Hygiene shall have the right to utilize the
services of the workers of the Department of Health
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and social work-
ers of the Department of Welfare , when authorized
by the Commissioner of Health or the Commissioner
of the Department of Welfare ; and is authorized to
pay the actual traveling expenses of the workers so
used , in accordance with the regulations set out for
the traveling expenses of other State officials .
$ 12. Transfer of Inmates :
The Director of the Division of Hospitals and
Mental Hygiene shall have power to transfer in-
mates from one institution to another , as the good of
the patient and the exigencies of the service require .
Whenever any patient , not a convict , who has been
committed by an order of court , is transferred from
one institution to another , the order of commitment
shall be held to apply to the hospital to which such
patient is transferred , as though said hospital or
institution had been the one to which the said order
of commitment had originally been made .
§ 13. Transfer of Personnel :
The Director of the Division of Hospitals and
Mental Hygiene shall have authority to transfer be-

CHAPTER 1 1185
tween institutions , or to the headquarters of the Di-
vision of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene , any super-
intendent , or any other employee . Necessary travel
expenses involved in such transfers shall be paid in
the same manner as provided by law for any other
employee of the Commonwealth traveling in the per-
formance of official duties .
§ 14. Inspection of Institutions :
At least four times in each calendar year , the Di-
rector of the Division of Hospitals and Mental Hy-
giene shall visit or cause to be visited by the As-
sistant Director , or a physician of the staff of one
of the state hospitals , designated by the Director of
the Division of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene for
that purpose , every state institution over which the
Division of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene has con-
trol . Such visits to state institutions shall be made
on such days and at such hours of the day or night ,
and for such length of time , as the Director , As-
sistant Director or member of the staff of the state
institution designated by the Director of the Divi-
sion of Hospitals and Mental Hygiene may choose .
In addition to these regular visits to be made at least
four times a year to the state institutions , the Di-
rector or his representative may make such other
visits as the Director may deem necessary . Every
visit to a state institution shall include , to the full-
est extent possible , an inspection of every part of
the institution , including the buildings and grounds
in connection therewith , the examination of all rec-
ords and the methods of administration , the general
and special dietary , and , so far as circumstances
may permit , of every patient confined therein , espe-
cially those admitted since the preceding visit , giv-
ing such as may require it suitable opportunity to
converse with the Director or his representative ,
apart from the officers and attendants . The Director
Acts- 38

1186 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or his authorized representative shall , so far as it is
deemed necessary , examine the officers , attendants
and other employees , and make such inquiries as will
determine their fitness for their respective duties .
§ 15. Political Activities :
Neither the Director of the Division of Hospitals
and Mental Hygiene , Assistant Director , nor super-
intendent of any hospital or institution shall be per-
mitted to engage in any political campaign , and any
political activity on the part of such officer or of-
ficers shall be considered as cause for dismissal ;
provided that any officer accused of such political
activity shall have charges preferred against him
and given the benefit of trial as provided in Section 7
of this Act .
§ 16. Voluntary Admission and Admission on
Health Officers ' Request :
In addition to existing laws governing commit-
ment and admission to hospitals and institutions , the
following provisions for voluntary admission , and
admission on health officers ' certificates , are hereby
enacted :

superintendent of the said hospital , if in his opinion
the patient is dangerous to himself or society , shall ,
within the same ten days , sign a petition for an in-
quest concerning the sanity of the said patient , as is
now provided by law.
Voluntary admissions , as provided for in this Sec-
tion , shall be paid for by the patient , as now pro-
vided by law , except in the cases of pauper patients .

1188 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
17. Psychiatric Service to Court :
It is hereby provided that hereafter it shall be
the duty of the clerk of a circuit court in which a
person having been twice previously convicted of a
felony , and is indicted by a grand jury as an habitual
criminal , to give notice to the Division of Hospitals
and Mental Hygiene within seven days after such
indictment is returned by the grand jury , on a blank
supplied by that Division , of such indictment or in-
dictments ; and the Director of the Division of Hos-
pitals and Mental Hygiene shall cause such person
to be examined by a psychiatrist already in the em-
ploy of the Department of Welfare , with the view of
determining his mental condition and the existence
of any mental disease or defect which would affect
his criminal responsibility . This examination shall
be made without expense other than the amount to
cover necessary travel , as provided by law for any
other employee of the State traveling on official
business . The psychiatrist making the examination
shall submit a written report of his findings to the
judge of the court having jurisdiction , who shall
make said report available to the prosecuting attor-
ney and the attorney for the defendant ; provided ,
however , that the Director of the Division of Hos-
pitals and Mental Hygiene may decline to cause such
examination to be made if the number of psychia-
trists on duty in the Department of Welfare is in-
sufficient to spare one from his regular official duties ,
in which event the Director of the Division of Hos-
pitals and Mental Hygiene shall notify the clerk of
the circuit court to that effect within three days .
§ 18. Discharge of Patients from Hospitals for
the Insane :
The superintendent of a hospital for the insane
may discharge recovered patients without an inquest
for restoration of legal status of sanity , provided

CHAPTER 1 1189
that in every such instance the superintendent shall
immediately send the name of such discharged pa-
tient to the Court having jurisdiction in his case .
Upon the receipt by the Court of such notice of re-
covery , the Court shall then hold an inquest to ascer-
tain whether or not such patient's legal status should
be restored . Provided , that nothing in this Section
shall be construed as applying to patients in the
hospitals for the criminal insane , nor shall this Sec-
tion be construed to repeal the existing law under
which a person may be declared sane after an in-
quest is held for such a purpose .
Whenever a person legally committed to a hospi-
tal for the criminal insane is found by the superin-
tendent of such hospital not to be insane , or , if in-
sane , to have recovered , the superintendent shall
notify the court which declared such person crim-
inally insane , and such court shall forthwith remove
the prisoner from such hospital .
§ 19. Repealing Clause :
All laws and parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed to the extent of such conflict . How-
ever , nothing contained in this act shall be deemed
to repeal , supersede or modify the provisions of sec-
tions 4618-85 to 4618-90 inclusive , Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1936 Edition , the same being Article
Ten of the Governmental Reorganization Act of
1936. The provisions of this act relating to such
matter affected by said Article Ten of the Govern-
mental Reorganization Act of 1936 shall be con-
strued to be additional and supplementary and not
in lieu of the said provisions of the Governmental
Reorganization Act.
§ 20. Appropriation :
( a ) For the purpose of carrying out the provi-
sions of this Act , in addition to the sum heretofore
appropriated by the General Assembly at its 1938

1190 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
regular session , for the fiscal year 1938-1939 , there
is hereby appropriated for the fiscal year beginning
July 1st , 1938 , and ending June 30th , 1939 , the sum
of three hundred thousand ( $ 300,000 ) dollars .
(b) For the purpose of carrying out the provi-
sions of this Act, in addition to the sum heretofore
appropriated by the General Assembly at its 1938
regular session , for the fiscal year 1939-1940 , there
is hereby appropriated for the fiscal year beginning
July 1st , 1939 , and ending June 30th , 1940 , the sum
of five hundred thousand ( $500,000 ) dollars .
(c ) Every requisition for a disbursement from
the State Treasury from any of the appropriations
made to the Division of Hospitals and Mental Hy-
giene of the Department of Welfare shall be ap-
proved by the Department of Welfare and there-
after by the Department of Finance prior to the is-
suance of any warant to the State Teasurer by the
Auditor of Public Accounts or any agent or em-
ployee of his office , and shall be subject to the per-
tinent provisions of the Governmental Re -Organi-
zation Act of 1936 .
§ 21. Construction of Act ; Effect of Partial
Invalidity:
It is the intention of the General Assembly to
enact this Act in sections and if any provision or
section of this Act shall be held for any reason to be
void or ineffective , it shall not affect the remainder
of the Act .
§ 22. Emergency Clause:
Whereas , the existing methods and equipment , in-
cluding personnel , for the operation of the various
State Institutions for the treatment of mentally
diseased persons is inadequate , and the personnel is
insufficient , and
Whereas, there is a large number of persons now
confined in said institutions without proper medical

CHAPTER 2 1191
care , nurses , attendants , medical supplies , and
equipment , and
Whereas , unless proper medical care , attendants ,
nurses , medical supplies , and equipment are pro-
vided for the care of said persons they will not re-
ceive proper treatment and their lives and health
are now endangered by reason of the existing con-
ditions , an emergency is hereby declared to exist,
and this Act shall become effective with its passage
and approval by the Governor of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , and the Director of the Division of
Hospitals and Mental Hygiene , provided for in this
Act, shall be appointed immediately by the Com-
missioner of Welfare subject to the approval of the
Governor ; provided , however , that the Governor
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky may , by
proclamation , fix the effective date of the other pro-
visions of the Act as he may deem proper and ad-
visable not later than July 1 , 1938 .
Approved by the Governor : May 28 , 1938 .
CHAPTER 2,
AN ACT appropriating money for the Special Session of the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , begin-
ning Monday , May 23rd , 1938 , and declaring an emergency to
exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated out of the
general expenditure fund for the payment of sal-
aries and other necessary expenses of the 1938 Spe-
cial Session of the General Assembly, beginning
Monday, May 23rd , 1938 , the sum of Twenty - Five
Thousand ( $ 25,000.00 ) Dollars .

1192 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 2. Whereas, the funds heretofore appropri
ated for the 1938 General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky are practically exhausted , and
Whereas , the work of the 1938 General Assembly
is not completed , an emergency is declared to exist
and this Act shall become effective upon
itspassage
and approval by the Governor
.
Approved by the Governor
: May
28, 1938
